To calculate what leave increment you need to apply for, use the below table:\nUse of Annual Leave for Unplanned Absences:\nWhen annual leave is applied for unplanned situations (for example, arriving late to work), leave will always be deducted in standard increments. This means the employee cannot “bank” or use any remaining time within that increment later in the day\n.\nExample (For explanation purposes only):\nIf an employee arrives 35 minutes late, they must apply for 0.25 days of annual leave (equal to 2 hours). The remaining 1 hour 25 minutes within that increment cannot be used to leave early on the same day or carried over for another time.\nSick Leave\nSick leave is granted when you are incapacitated from work due to illness or injury. Sick leave is not permitted for renewing prescriptions or collecting medication. It is intended for when an employee is genuinely too ill to work.\nEntitlement:\nYou are entitled to\nsix (6) weeks of paid sick leave (30 day's)\nover a\nthirty-six (36) month sick leave cycle\n.\nFirst Six Months of Employment:\nDuring your first six (6) months of employment, you are entitled to one (1) day of sick leave per month.\nMedical Certificates:\nFor any sick leave taken, you are required to submit a valid, signed medical certificate from a registered medical practitioner. Refer to the\nMedical certificate requirements (Sick notes) policy.\nAwesim Telecoms reserves the right to reject a medical certificate if it does not meet legislative requirements. Failure to provide a valid medical certificate, or if a certificate is rejected, may result in the application of a \\\"no work, no pay\\\" policy and potential disciplinary action.\nFamily Illness:\nSick leave is not granted for absence due to the illness of a child. Please refer to the Family Responsibility Leave section for such instances.\nNotification:\nYou must notify your manager/immediate supervisor at least\none (1) hour before your normal working start time\non the first day of absence due to illness. For prolonged illnesses, regular updates are required. Failure to do so may lead to disciplinary action.\nApplication Submission:\nUpon your return to work, you must submit a sick leave application (or an appropriate leave application if no sick leave is available) on the ESS system in BCX before 09:00am, attaching the required medical certificate. Failure to do so may result in disciplinary action.\nTermination:\nUpon termination of employment, any sick leave taken in excess of your allowed entitlement may be deducted from your final payment.\nFamily Responsibility Leave\nFamily Responsibility Leave is provided to assist employees with certain urgent family matters.\nEligibility:\nEmployees who have worked for Awesim Telecoms for more than four (4) months and work more than four (4) days a week are entitled to\nthree (3) days of paid family responsibility leave\nduring each\ntwelve (12) month leave cycle\n.\nPermitted Uses (as per BCEA):\nWhen your child is sick.\nIn the unfortunate event of the death of your:\nSpouse or life partner.\nParent, adoptive parent, grandparent, child, adoptive child, grandchild, or sibling.\nSupporting documents:\nYou are required to attach reasonable supporting documents for the event (e.g., medical certificate for a sick child, death certificate) to your family responsibility leave application in the ESS system in BCX. Failure to provide the required documents will result in a \\\"no work, no pay\\\" deduction if leave has already been taken, or you will be required to apply for annual leave if leave has not yet been taken.\nAccumulation:\nFamily responsibility leave cannot be accumulated from one leave cycle to the next.\nMaternity Leave\nAwesim Telecoms supports new mothers by providing maternity leave.\nEntitlement:\nFemale employees are entitled to\nfour (4) consecutive months of maternity leave\n.\nPayment Schedule:\nMaternity leave payments are determined by your length of service with Awesim Telecoms, as per the table below. This payment is a percentage of your basic cash component paid out per month:\nLength of Service (Payroll Start Date to Due Date)\nPayment (% of basic cash component per month)\n0 - 12 months\n0%\n12 - 24 months\n35%\n24+ months\n50%\nCommencement:\nMaternity leave may commence:\nAt any time from four (4) weeks before the expected date of birth, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing with Awesim Telecoms.\nOn a date specified by your attending medical practitioner or midwife.\nPost-Birth Return:\nYou are not required or permitted to return to work within six (6) weeks of the child's birth, unless a medical practitioner or midwife certifies that you are fit to do so.\nAnnual Leave Concurrency:\nYou may be permitted to take annual leave prior to or following your maternity leave period, upon prior approval through the ESS system and based on operational requirements.\nNotification:\nYou must notify the CHRO and your direct Line Manager in writing of your expected due date at least\nfour (4) months prior\nto the expected delivery/birth date and submit all required supporting documentation.\nIntention to Commence/Return:\nYou must notify Awesim Telecoms in writing of the date you intend to commence maternity leave and the date you intend to return to work after maternity leave. This notification must be given at least\nfour (4) months before\nyou intend to commence maternity leave.\nMiscarriage/Stillbirth:\nAn employee who has a miscarriage during the third trimester of pregnancy or bears a stillborn child is entitled to maternity leave for six (6) weeks after the miscarriage or stillbirth, regardless of whether maternity leave had already commenced.\nParental Leave\nParental leave allows parents to bond with their new child.\nEntitlement:\nAn employee who is a parent of a child is entitled to at least\nten (10) consecutive days of unpaid parental leave\n.\nCommencement:\nParental leave may commence on:\nThe day your child is born.\nThe date the adoption order is granted, or the date a child is placed in the care of a prospective adoptive parent by a competent court, pending the finalization of an adoption order, whichever occurs first.\nNotification:\nYou must notify Awesim Telecoms in writing of the date you intend to commence parental leave and the date you intend to return to work after parental leave. This notification must be given at least\ntwo (2) months before\nyour child is expected to be born or the date of adoption/placement.\nPayment:\nParental benefits will be determined by the Minister, subject to the provisions of the Unemployment Insurance Act, 2001 (Act No. 63 of 2001). This leave is\nunpaid by Awesim Telecoms\n.\nAdoption Leave\nAdoption leave provides time for adoptive parents to integrate a new child into their family.\nEntitlement:\nAn employee who is an adoptive parent of a child below the age of two is entitled to:\nAdoption leave of at least\nten (10) consecutive weeks\n;\nOR\nThe parental leave referred to in the above section.\nCommencement:\nAdoption leave may commence on the date:\nThe adoption order is granted.\nA child is placed in the care of a prospective adoptive parent by a competent court, pending the finalization of an adoption order, whichever occurs first.\nNotification:\nYou must notify Awesim Telecoms in writing of the date you intend to commence adoption leave and the date you intend to return to work after adoption leave. This notification must be given at least\ntwo (2) months before\nthe date referred to in the commencement criteria.\nPayment:\nAdoption benefits by Awesim Telecoms will be\nunpaid\n. Employees may apply to the Unemployment Insurance Fund (UIF) for payment, which will be determined by the Minister, subject to the provisions of the Unemployment Insurance Act, 2001 (Act No. 63 of 2001).\nShared Leave:\nIf an adoption order is made for two adoptive parents, one may apply for adoption leave and the other for parental leave, at their discretion. Similarly, if a child is placed in the care of two prospective adoptive parents, one may apply for adoption leave and the other for parental leave, at their discretion.\nStudy and Examination Leave\nAwesim Telecoms recognises the importance of continuous learning and professional development.\nEligibility:\nStudy leave may be granted for courses that have been approved in line with the\nTraining & Development Policy.\nEntitlement:\nYou are entitled to a maximum of\nten (10) days of study leave in a calendar year\n. For your first year of employment, this is subject to the sole discretion of the company.\nPermitted Use:\nStudy leave will be granted for the day of the examination and the day preceding the examination date, up to the maximum of ten (10) days per calendar year.\nApplication Process:\nRefer to the\nTraining & Development Policy\non how to apply for a study subsidy or get approval to use study leave for a relevant course.\nESS Submission:\nPrior to the examination period, you are expected to submit your study leave request on the ESS system for approval, attaching the examination timetable.\nRepeat Courses/Exams:\nStudy leave will not be granted for repeating failed examinations or courses.\nAccumulation:\nStudy leave cannot be accumulated from one calendar year to the next.\nUnpaid Leave\nUnpaid leave is a discretionary leave option for exceptional circumstances.\nEligibility:\nUnpaid leave may be granted at the sole discretion of Awesim Telecoms where exceptional circumstances exist, and must be authorised and approved by your manager, subject to the following conditions:\nUnpaid leave will only be considered once all your accumulated\nannual leave has been taken\n.\nIn cases of illness, unpaid leave will only be considered once:\nYour sick leave entitlement has been exhausted.\nAll accumulated annual leave has been taken.\nUsed for \\\"no work No Pay\\\" cases.\nApplication:\nRequests for unpaid leave must be submitted on the ESS system at least\nseven (7) days prior\nto the intended start date for your manager's review. In cases of illness, unpaid leave must be captured into the ESS system on the day of your return to work following the illness.\nUnpaid Leave Process for Managers:\nOnce\nunpaid leave\nhas been approved, managers must forward the\nemail notification containing all unpaid leave details to the CHRO\n. This is essential for the CHRO to ensure the correct deduction is made from the employee's salary. Should approved unpaid leave be\ncancelled after the 10th of the month\n, it's crucial to\nimmediately inform the CHRO\n. This allows them to cancel the salary deduction before payroll is approved. If payroll has already been approved and the deduction cannot be cancelled, the CHRO will arrange for the deducted funds to be reimbursed to the employee in the subsequent payroll run.\nOvertime Leave (Leave in Lieu)\nOvertime leave may be granted as compensation for approved overtime worked.\nEligibility:\nAwesim Telecoms may grant leave in lieu of overtime worked for employees exceed the BCEA threshold and do not qualify for overtime payments.\nRequesting Overtime Work:\nIf you are required to work overtime, you must submit a written motivation to your direct Line Manager and the CEO at least\nthree (3) working days prior\nto the anticipated start date of the overtime, or as soon as reasonably possible. Your Line Manager and CEO will review the application based on operational requirements.\nDiscretionary:\nOvertime work is not guaranteed and will be considered on the merits of each application. Leave in lieu of overtime work is not guaranteed and will be considered on the merits of each application.\nContractual Overtime:\nPlease note that your contract of employment may stipulate a certain amount of expected overtime. Additional hours recorded in time tracking systems (e.g., Toggl) do not automatically qualify for overtime leave.\nPrior Approval:\nOvertime work may only commence once prior written approval has been obtained.\nApplication for Leave:\nApproved leave in lieu of overtime must be applied for in the ESS system following the overtime worked. The leave must be taken within a month of the overtime worked. Leave not taken within the month will be automatically forfeited.\nSpecial Leave\nSpecial leave is granted at Awesim Telecoms' discretion for specific circumstances.\nDiscretionary Granting:\nAwesim Telecoms may grant special leave at its sole discretion after considering all available facts.\nCompany-Issued Special Leave:\nAwesim Telecoms may issue up to\nthree (3) special leave days per annum\nfor dates aligned with operational requirements and/or operational close periods, at its discretion.\nMerit-Based Review:\nAwesim Telecoms reserves the right to review each application for special leave on a merit basis.\nApplication:\nShould special leave be issued or approved, the leave must be applied for in the ESS system for the respective dates. The issuing or approval of special leave does not set a precedent or expectation for future special leave to be issued or approved.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"376f4ea6-a0a0-4671-be3b-7951e2bc2a2d\",\"title\":\"Awesim Leave Policy & Procedure\",\"content\":\"

Scope/Objective

This policy outlines the guidelines and procedures for all employees of Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd regarding various types of leave. Our aim is to ensure a healthy work-life balance and foster a positive work environment, aligning with the Basic Conditions of Employment Act (BCEA) 75 of 1997.

This policy applies to all employees, with the exception of those working less than 24 hours per month for Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd or sub-contractors, who do not accrue annual leave.

Definitions

For the purpose of this policy:

  • Leave: Approved absence from work, applied for through the prescribed method.
  • Public Holiday: A public holiday as defined by the Public Holidays Act 1994 (Act 36 of 1994) or any other day designated as such by Awesim Telecoms.
  • Leave Cycle: A 12-month period calculated from your employment start date (anniversary date of joining Awesim Telecoms).
  • Sick Leave Cycle: A 36-month period calculated from your employment start date (anniversary date of joining Awesim Telecoms).
  • BCEA: Refers to the Basic Conditions of Employment Act and its Regulations (75/1997).

Leave Types and Procedures

Leave Application Approvals

All leave must be recorded and approved in the Employee Self-Service (ESS) system in BCX.

To ensure timely processing of leave requests, line managers must approve their subordinates' leave applications within four (4) working days. If no action is taken within this timeframe, the system will automatically forward the application to the next designated approver.

When a manager is on leave for two (2) or more consecutive days, they are required to activate their \\\"Out of Office\\\" setting in the ESS system within BCX. This setting, found at the bottom of the left navigation panel in each individual's ESS account, will automatically redirect any incoming leave applications to an alternative leave approver for review and decision.

Annual Leave

Awesim Telecoms encourages employees to take regular annual leave for rest and rejuvenation.

  • Accrual: Your annual leave entitlement is specified in your employment contract. Your monthly accrual will load on the 1st of every month from your employment start date.
  • Application: All annual leave requests must be submitted via the Employee Self-Service (ESS) system in BCX at least seven (7) days before your intended leave start date.
  • Approval: Leave approval is subject to your manager's approval and Awesim's operational requirements. If an agreement cannot be reached, Awesim Telecoms reserves the right to make the final decision.
  • Leave Balance: You may only take annual leave equivalent to your accrued days.
  • Public Holidays: Public holidays that fall within your approved annual leave period will not be deducted from your annual leave balance and will be paid.
  • Concurrency: Annual leave cannot be taken concurrently with paid sick leave, as stipulated by the BCEA.
  • Religious Days: Days observed for religious purposes, other than official South African public holidays, must be applied for as annual leave.
  • Payouts: Annual leave cannot be exchanged for cash. Any outstanding annual leave will only be paid out upon termination of your employment with Awesim Telecoms, in accordance with this policy.
  • Notice Period: Annual leave may not be taken during your notice period unless prior written approval has been granted by management.
  • Max Accumulation: You are permitted to accumulate a maximum of twenty-two (22) annual leave days. The ESS system in BCX will automatically stop accruing leave once this limit is reached. It is your responsibility to regularly check your leave balance on BCX and apply for leave accordingly to prevent forfeiture of accrued days.
  • Leave increments calculation: The ESS system in BCX offers standard increments that can be taken as annual leave. To calculate what leave increment you need to apply for, use the below table:

  • Use of Annual Leave for Unplanned Absences: When annual leave is applied for unplanned situations (for example, arriving late to work), leave will always be deducted in standard increments. This means the employee cannot “bank” or use any remaining time within that increment later in the day. Example (For explanation purposes only): If an employee arrives 35 minutes late, they must apply for 0.25 days of annual leave (equal to 2 hours). The remaining 1 hour 25 minutes within that increment cannot be used to leave early on the same day or carried over for another time.

Sick Leave

Sick leave is granted when you are incapacitated from work due to illness or injury. Sick leave is not permitted for renewing prescriptions or collecting medication. It is intended for when an employee is genuinely too ill to work.

  • Entitlement: You are entitled to six (6) weeks of paid sick leave (30 day's) over a thirty-six (36) month sick leave cycle.
  • First Six Months of Employment: During your first six (6) months of employment, you are entitled to one (1) day of sick leave per month.
  • Medical Certificates: For any sick leave taken, you are required to submit a valid, signed medical certificate from a registered medical practitioner. Refer to the Medical certificate requirements (Sick notes) policy. Awesim Telecoms reserves the right to reject a medical certificate if it does not meet legislative requirements. Failure to provide a valid medical certificate, or if a certificate is rejected, may result in the application of a \\\"no work, no pay\\\" policy and potential disciplinary action.
  • Family Illness: Sick leave is not granted for absence due to the illness of a child. Please refer to the Family Responsibility Leave section for such instances.
  • Notification: You must notify your manager/immediate supervisor at least one (1) hour before your normal working start time on the first day of absence due to illness. For prolonged illnesses, regular updates are required. Failure to do so may lead to disciplinary action.
  • Application Submission: Upon your return to work, you must submit a sick leave application (or an appropriate leave application if no sick leave is available) on the ESS system in BCX before 09:00am, attaching the required medical certificate. Failure to do so may result in disciplinary action.
  • Termination: Upon termination of employment, any sick leave taken in excess of your allowed entitlement may be deducted from your final payment.

Family Responsibility Leave

Family Responsibility Leave is provided to assist employees with certain urgent family matters.

  • Eligibility: Employees who have worked for Awesim Telecoms for more than four (4) months and work more than four (4) days a week are entitled to three (3) days of paid family responsibility leave during each twelve (12) month leave cycle.
  • Permitted Uses (as per BCEA):
    • When your child is sick.
    • In the unfortunate event of the death of your:
      • Spouse or life partner.
      • Parent, adoptive parent, grandparent, child, adoptive child, grandchild, or sibling.
  • Supporting documents: You are required to attach reasonable supporting documents for the event (e.g., medical certificate for a sick child, death certificate) to your family responsibility leave application in the ESS system in BCX. Failure to provide the required documents will result in a \\\"no work, no pay\\\" deduction if leave has already been taken, or you will be required to apply for annual leave if leave has not yet been taken.
  • Accumulation: Family responsibility leave cannot be accumulated from one leave cycle to the next.

Maternity Leave

Awesim Telecoms supports new mothers by providing maternity leave.

  • Entitlement: Female employees are entitled to four (4) consecutive months of maternity leave.
  • Payment Schedule: Maternity leave payments are determined by your length of service with Awesim Telecoms, as per the table below. This payment is a percentage of your basic cash component paid out per month:

Length of Service (Payroll Start Date to Due Date)

Payment (% of basic cash component per month)

0 - 12 months

0%

12 - 24 months

35%

24+ months

50%

Commencement: Maternity leave may commence:

    • At any time from four (4) weeks before the expected date of birth, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing with Awesim Telecoms.
    • On a date specified by your attending medical practitioner or midwife.
  • Post-Birth Return: You are not required or permitted to return to work within six (6) weeks of the child's birth, unless a medical practitioner or midwife certifies that you are fit to do so.
  • Annual Leave Concurrency: You may be permitted to take annual leave prior to or following your maternity leave period, upon prior approval through the ESS system and based on operational requirements.
  • Notification: You must notify the CHRO and your direct Line Manager in writing of your expected due date at least four (4) months prior to the expected delivery/birth date and submit all required supporting documentation.
  • Intention to Commence/Return: You must notify Awesim Telecoms in writing of the date you intend to commence maternity leave and the date you intend to return to work after maternity leave. This notification must be given at least four (4) months before you intend to commence maternity leave.
  • Miscarriage/Stillbirth: An employee who has a miscarriage during the third trimester of pregnancy or bears a stillborn child is entitled to maternity leave for six (6) weeks after the miscarriage or stillbirth, regardless of whether maternity leave had already commenced.

Parental Leave

Parental leave allows parents to bond with their new child.

  • Entitlement: An employee who is a parent of a child is entitled to at least ten (10) consecutive days of unpaid parental leave.
  • Commencement: Parental leave may commence on:
    • The day your child is born.
    • The date the adoption order is granted, or the date a child is placed in the care of a prospective adoptive parent by a competent court, pending the finalization of an adoption order, whichever occurs first.
  • Notification: You must notify Awesim Telecoms in writing of the date you intend to commence parental leave and the date you intend to return to work after parental leave. This notification must be given at least two (2) months before your child is expected to be born or the date of adoption/placement.
  • Payment: Parental benefits will be determined by the Minister, subject to the provisions of the Unemployment Insurance Act, 2001 (Act No. 63 of 2001). This leave is unpaid by Awesim Telecoms.

Adoption Leave

Adoption leave provides time for adoptive parents to integrate a new child into their family.

  • Entitlement: An employee who is an adoptive parent of a child below the age of two is entitled to:
    • Adoption leave of at least ten (10) consecutive weeks; OR
    • The parental leave referred to in the above section.
  • Commencement: Adoption leave may commence on the date:
    • The adoption order is granted.
    • A child is placed in the care of a prospective adoptive parent by a competent court, pending the finalization of an adoption order, whichever occurs first.
  • Notification: You must notify Awesim Telecoms in writing of the date you intend to commence adoption leave and the date you intend to return to work after adoption leave. This notification must be given at least two (2) months before the date referred to in the commencement criteria.
  • Payment: Adoption benefits by Awesim Telecoms will be unpaid. Employees may apply to the Unemployment Insurance Fund (UIF) for payment, which will be determined by the Minister, subject to the provisions of the Unemployment Insurance Act, 2001 (Act No. 63 of 2001).
  • Shared Leave: If an adoption order is made for two adoptive parents, one may apply for adoption leave and the other for parental leave, at their discretion. Similarly, if a child is placed in the care of two prospective adoptive parents, one may apply for adoption leave and the other for parental leave, at their discretion.

Study and Examination Leave

Awesim Telecoms recognises the importance of continuous learning and professional development.

  • Eligibility: Study leave may be granted for courses that have been approved in line with the Training & Development Policy.
  • Entitlement: You are entitled to a maximum of ten (10) days of study leave in a calendar year. For your first year of employment, this is subject to the sole discretion of the company.
  • Permitted Use: Study leave will be granted for the day of the examination and the day preceding the examination date, up to the maximum of ten (10) days per calendar year.
  • Application Process: Refer to the Training & Development Policy on how to apply for a study subsidy or get approval to use study leave for a relevant course.
  • ESS Submission: Prior to the examination period, you are expected to submit your study leave request on the ESS system for approval, attaching the examination timetable.
  • Repeat Courses/Exams: Study leave will not be granted for repeating failed examinations or courses.
  • Accumulation: Study leave cannot be accumulated from one calendar year to the next.

Unpaid Leave

Unpaid leave is a discretionary leave option for exceptional circumstances.

  • Eligibility: Unpaid leave may be granted at the sole discretion of Awesim Telecoms where exceptional circumstances exist, and must be authorised and approved by your manager, subject to the following conditions:
    • Unpaid leave will only be considered once all your accumulated annual leave has been taken.
    • In cases of illness, unpaid leave will only be considered once:
      • Your sick leave entitlement has been exhausted.
      • All accumulated annual leave has been taken.
    • Used for \\\"no work No Pay\\\" cases.
  • Application: Requests for unpaid leave must be submitted on the ESS system at least seven (7) days prior to the intended start date for your manager's review. In cases of illness, unpaid leave must be captured into the ESS system on the day of your return to work following the illness.
  • Unpaid Leave Process for Managers: Once unpaid leave has been approved, managers must forward the email notification containing all unpaid leave details to the CHRO. This is essential for the CHRO to ensure the correct deduction is made from the employee's salary. Should approved unpaid leave be cancelled after the 10th of the month, it's crucial to immediately inform the CHRO. This allows them to cancel the salary deduction before payroll is approved. If payroll has already been approved and the deduction cannot be cancelled, the CHRO will arrange for the deducted funds to be reimbursed to the employee in the subsequent payroll run.

Overtime Leave (Leave in Lieu)

Overtime leave may be granted as compensation for approved overtime worked.

  • Eligibility: Awesim Telecoms may grant leave in lieu of overtime worked for employees exceed the BCEA threshold and do not qualify for overtime payments.
  • Requesting Overtime Work: If you are required to work overtime, you must submit a written motivation to your direct Line Manager and the CEO at least three (3) working days prior to the anticipated start date of the overtime, or as soon as reasonably possible. Your Line Manager and CEO will review the application based on operational requirements.
  • Discretionary: Overtime work is not guaranteed and will be considered on the merits of each application. Leave in lieu of overtime work is not guaranteed and will be considered on the merits of each application.
  • Contractual Overtime: Please note that your contract of employment may stipulate a certain amount of expected overtime. Additional hours recorded in time tracking systems (e.g., Toggl) do not automatically qualify for overtime leave.
  • Prior Approval: Overtime work may only commence once prior written approval has been obtained.
  • Application for Leave: Approved leave in lieu of overtime must be applied for in the ESS system following the overtime worked. The leave must be taken within a month of the overtime worked. Leave not taken within the month will be automatically forfeited.

Special Leave

Special leave is granted at Awesim Telecoms' discretion for specific circumstances.

  • Discretionary Granting: Awesim Telecoms may grant special leave at its sole discretion after considering all available facts.
  • Company-Issued Special Leave: Awesim Telecoms may issue up to three (3) special leave days per annum for dates aligned with operational requirements and/or operational close periods, at its discretion.
  • Merit-Based Review: Awesim Telecoms reserves the right to review each application for special leave on a merit basis.
  • Application: Should special leave be issued or approved, the leave must be applied for in the ESS system for the respective dates. The issuing or approval of special leave does not set a precedent or expectation for future special leave to be issued or approved.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Annual Leave", "id": "df1a3b0a-88fa-4914-a4b7-1ede5f2b08b8", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Leave", "id": "1fa6ecdf-95e4-4278-bbf8-b9d000f9dfc2", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Sick Leave", "id": "7572a827-4f8f-43ec-b6a8-32e8d1c18f9e", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2026-04-14T13:49:42.775+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/376f4ea6-a0a0-4671-be3b-7951e2bc2a2d" }, { "id": "b450eb01-8818-4f00-b8ef-7c2c3ba6fa23", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"b450eb01-8818-4f00-b8ef-7c2c3ba6fa23\",\"title\":\"Guidelines to Offenses & Possible Sanctions Policy\",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nThe disciplinary code provides guidelines in terms of desirable behaviour.  In addition, it provides guidelines in terms of correcting unacceptable behaviour and/or unsatisfactory performance.\nThis guideline is not an exhaustive list. Any form of misconduct not listed herein will be dealt with in terms of the norms of similar offences. Employees may be charged for transgressions not necessarily contained herein. In serious instances of misconduct, the precedent as set out herein will be disregarded and a disciplinary inquiry imposed for a first offense. Mitigating and aggravating factors could also determine that the Chairperson uses his discretion to deviate from the recommended sanction and impose lighter or heavier sanction as the case may be.\nMisconduct that is regarded as related or similar will considered collectively and therefore any previous disciplinary records in any related/similar category will be taken into consideration when applying progressive disciplinary action where applicable (e.g. poor time keeping, unauthorised absenteeism, arriving late/early for work are considered related/similar offenses).\nGUIDELINES OF MISCONDUCT\nABSENTEEISM & TIMEKEEPING OFFENSES\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\n1\nArriving for work late and/or leaving work early without permission and/or authorisation, and/or a valid reason\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nFailing to inform your Line Manager of the fact that you will be unable to attend to work as a result of illness or otherwise within the timeframe required\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nTaking of extended lunch/tea/smoke breaks and/or unauthorised breaks during working hours\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nPoor time keeping and/or loafing and/or loitering and/or idling during working hours\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n5\nClock in irregularities: Clocking in on behalf of an employee and/or an employee clocking in on your behalf\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n6\nPerforming private work/activities during official working hours (e.g. gambling, watching movies, watching videos, etc.) that have an impact on work performance/output\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n7\nRegular absence for reasons of illness which forms a pattern i.e. Mondays/Fridays etc. irrelevant whether a medical certificate is produced or not\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n8\nFailure to report for work or duty without permission and/or authorization and/or a valid reason and/or informing management/ supervisors (Less than 3 days)\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n9\nDesertion: Absence from work for 3 or more consecutive days without permission and/or authorization and/or a valid reason and/or informing management/ supervisors and/or a valid medical certificate\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nWORK STANDARD OFFENSES\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\nFourth Offense\nFifth Offense\n1\nNegligence: Failure to comply with a standard of care that would be reasonably expected for the completion and fulfilment of a duty/tasks.\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nGross negligence: A serious failure to comply with a standard of care that would be reasonably expected for the completion and fulfilment of a duty/tasks. Generally gross negligence would have had or could possibly have had a financial loss to the company.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nDereliction of duty: Failure to comply with a duty to perform a task or job function in terms of your allocated role and/or allocated responsibilities and/or job profile\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nFailure to perform duties and responsibilities in a reasonable and diligent manner and/or in accordance with company standards\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n5\nFailure to carry out instruction given by manager/supervisor and/or person having authority to do so\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n6\nBreach of company policies and/or procedures and/or instructions\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n7\nFailure to protect and/or promote the company at all times to the best of their ability and with due regard to the interests of management and fellow employees\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n8\nFailure to stay up to date on a regular basis will all company communication platforms i.e. Slack, Emails, etc.\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n9\nShortages in stock and/or material without a valid reason\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n10\nSwapping of stock and/or agents’ stock without permission and/or authorisation and/or a valid reason\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n11\nPerforming a PRE-RICA on any sim card\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n12\nInvoicing stock to the incorrect agent and/or agent’s account without a valid reason.\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n13\nFailure to confirm receipt of stock following delivery via Self Service portals (USSD; my.awesim, Infield invoicing, etc.) / required process.\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n14\nFailure to ensure agents confirm receipt of their stock following delivery via Self Service portals (USSD; my.awesim, Infield invoicing, etc.)/ required process.\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n15\nCreating SYSPRO accounts to receive stock for personal and/or family members use without managements permission and/or authorisation\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n16\nCreating SYSPRO accounts with fake and/or intentionally incorrect details\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n17\nChanging SYSPRO account details without agents’ permission, this includes any payment details\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n18\nRICA’ing stock in order to get paid on any account but your own (after obtaining managements permission and/or authorisation to create your own account)\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n19\nOpening sealed packages and/or stock without permission and/or authorisation and/or a valid reason\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n20\nRemoving any items from sealed packages without permission and/or authorisation and/or a valid reason\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n21\nRedeeming Reward Points on behalf of an agent/dealer without the permission of that agent/dealer\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n22\nAny act by an employee to interfere with the normal operations of the company and/or damaging the company/agents/colleagues image and/or income/profits, etc.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n23\nFailure to return the Smartload to Awesim within two (2) business days after a promotion has ended\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n24\nParticipation in an unlawful strike contradicting the LRA where the matters in dispute have not been processed through all relevant procedures set out in any law and/or agreement\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n25\nIncitement to participation in a strike contradicting the LRA including – any concerted action by one or more employees to withdraw their labour and/or “go slow” and/or work to rule and/or otherwise interfere with the normal operations of the company in furtherance of a dispute and/or with the object of compelling management to take and/or refrain from taking any specific action where the matters in dispute have not been processed through all relevant procedures set out in any law and/or agreement\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n26\nInvoicing Team: Negligence / Gross Negligence\nInvoicing inaccuracy, invoicing errors or failure to process invoicing SR's with a standard of care and accuracy that would be reasonably expected.\nWritten Warning (Valid for 3 months)\nWritten Warning (Valid for 3 months)\nWritten Warning (Valid for 3 months)\nFinal Written Warning (Valid for 6months)\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n27\nCustomer Care Team: Negligence / Gross Negligence\nFailure to uphold expected customer service standards: including but not limited to, inadequate handling of service requests (SRs), providing inaccurate information or responses, and failure to provide assistance or resolutions aligned with the customer's needs or expectations.\nLack of attention to customer context or history: neglecting to review and consider a customer's interaction history or previous requests, which could lead to service inconsistencies or unresolved issues.\nInsufficient standard of care and accuracy in SR processing: not exercising the level of diligence, accuracy, and professionalism reasonably expected to process customer SRs.\nWritten Warning\n(Valid for 3 months)\nWritten Warning\n(Valid for 3 months)\nWritten Warning\n(Valid for 3 months)\nFinal Written Warning\n(Valid for 6months)\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n28\nSubmitting or processing Agent account merges incorrectly, providing incorrect information, or failing to follow the required steps and validations as outlined in the SOP.\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n29\nAgents’ login credentials may not be used, accessed, or shared on any device other than the Agent’s own device. Any unauthorised or unethical use of such credentials, including transactions conducted without proper consent, is strictly prohibited.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nBEHAVIOURAL / CONDUCT RELATED OFFENSES\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\nFourth Offense\n1\nSmoking, eating or drinking in an area which prohibits such activities\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nSleeping at work and/or sleeping whilst on duty\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nInsubordination and/or insolence and/or disgraceful and/or improper behaviour\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nWastage of material and/or stationary and/or supplies, etc.\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n5\nUse of abusive and/or vulgar and/or offensive and/or inappropriate language\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n6\nIntimidation/fighting and/or attempted fighting and/or assaulting and/or attempted assault\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n7\nAny sexual harassment or intention of a sexual nature that is offensive and unwanted\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n8\nBehaviour that constitutes a threat of harm on another person or property which could be verbal or physical actions.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n9\nGossiping and/or spreading rumours and/or false allegations/information and/or unconfirmed information\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n10\nInjury to others as a result of rough play and/or negligence\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n11\nUse of and/or in possession of a dangerous weapon\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n12\nUnauthorised presence on company premises\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n13\nBreach of company's dress code, including attire that may be viewed as unprofessional, disruptive, or inappropriate in the work environment.\nWritten Warning\n(Valid for 3 months)\nWritten Warning\n(Valid for 6 months)\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nVEHICLE & ASSET RELATED OFFENSES\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\n1\nSpeeding and/or exceeding the national speed limit in a company vehicle and/or third parties’ vehicle\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nPoor maintenance of and/or damage to employer’s and/or third parties’ vehicle(s) and/or equipment and/or assets which are under the control of the company. This includes sim cards and/or any type of promotional material/Rewards items that are being transported to agents/dealers.\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nReckless and/or negligent driving of company vehicle(s)\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nFailing to comply with traffic regulations/legislation\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n5\nDriving a vehicle without an appropriate and/or valid driver’s license and/or in breach of legislation in regard hereto\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n6\nDriving and/or operating company vehicles and/or machinery and/or equipment under and/or whilst on a balance of probabilities under the influence of drugs and/or alcohol and/or intoxicating and/or illegal substances\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n7\nUnauthorised conveying of passengers and/or non-employees and/or goods in company vehicle\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n8\nFailure to maintain and/or service and/or repair and/or look after company vehicle\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n9\nFailure to report damage to company vehicles and/or property and/or equipment and/or assets to the required person and within the required time frame\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n10\nRemoval and/or attempted removal of any company assets and/or equipment and/or material from the company premises without permission and/or authorisation\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n11\nWillful and/or deliberate and/or attempt to damage or destroy company property and/or equipment and/or assets, etc.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n12\nUnauthorised possession and/or use of company and/or another’s goods and/or possessions\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nDISHONESTY OFFENSES\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\n1\nMaking false statements and/or issuing of false documentation with the intent to deceive or conceal the truth. Typical examples include lying, deception, insincerity, corruption, etc.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nFraud/Misappropriation: Making of false statements and/or issuing of false documentation with the intention to obtain some form of benefit and/or unjustified enrichment/payment\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nBribery/corruption: Any action in terms of which a third party is persuaded by any means to act in a manner which that person would not have normally acted/behaved\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nCriminal offense and/or illegal activity and/or conviction and/or transgression and or any illegal activity or cause recognised in law\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n5\nAccepting claim and/or commission and/or money and/or fees and/or gifts/rewards without managements permission and/or authorisation (Not part of remuneration)\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n6\nConfirming/marking an agent/s attendance on the promotions bus when the agent was not on the bus or was not in attendance.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nIT RELATED OFFENSES\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\n1\nUnauthorised access to company information, systems or data\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nDownloading and/or uploading and/or saving any personal unauthorised content on company computers and/or equipment (e.g. Music, Video’s, etc.)\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nUploading and/or downloading and/or distributing unauthorised software, media or files that violate copyright laws.\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nIntroducing viruses or malware into company networks or systems whether intentional or by negligence\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n5\nUploading and/or downloading and/or transmitting and/or distributing and/or in possession of any offensive material from the internet, intranet, cell phone, or otherwise\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n6\nUploading and/or removing and/or downloading and/or bypassing and/or changing computer software/hardware without permission and/or authorisation\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n7\nSharing confidential or proprietary information with unauthorised employees and/or third parties\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n8\nPlugging in and/or making use of personal and/or unauthorised devices (i.e. USB devices, Wi-Fi devices, etc.) to company computers and/or devices and/or networks without permission which could potentially introduce malware, other security risks or slows systems/access\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n9\nObtaining and/or sharing and/or using and/or accessing log in details and/or accounts belonging to another, not yourself, for any Awesim or third party systems/accounts (my.awesim, Awesim App, Promo booking system, VPN, CMS, etc.).\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nDETRIMENTAL OFFENSES TO AWESIM\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\n1\nBringing the company name into disrepute and/or possibly bringing the company name into disrepute and/or portraying a bad image of the company\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nBreach of confidentiality/trust. Unauthorised disclosure of confidential information including but not limited to company trade secrets, confidential documentation, technical knowledge and data, systems, methods software processes, client lists or information, programmes, financial information, etc.\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nFailing to disclose interest in another business. Being a member or partner of another business, whether you are involved in the day to day or not.\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nFailure to disclose relationships (romantic, family, friends, etc.) of individuals working within a company that Awesim uses as a supplier or service provider.\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nVIOLATION OF HEALTH & SAFETY\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\n1\nNot reporting an accident and/or injury on duty involving self immediately\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nNot reporting an accident and/or injury on duty involving the company and/or another and/or third party immediately\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n3\nUse of equipment and/or material in a manner that endangers own and/or another’s safety and/or health and/or the environment\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n4\nFailure to observe and/or comply with the safety rules and regulations prescribed by the company\nWritten Warning\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n5\nFailure and/or refusal to wear/use personal protective wear and/or equipment\nFinal Written Warning\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\nALCOHOL AND DRUG RELATED OFFENSES\nNo.\nNature of misconduct\nFirst Offense\nSecond Offense\nThird Offense\n1\nIncapable of working properly and/or reporting for work whilst on a balance of probabilities under the influence of drugs and/or alcohol and/or intoxicating and/or illegal substances\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n2\nUse of and/or under the influence of and/or in possession of drugs and/or alcohol and/or intoxicating and/or illegal substances during working hours\nDisciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)\n**Note: Where permission and/or authorisation is specified, written permission is required.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"b450eb01-8818-4f00-b8ef-7c2c3ba6fa23\",\"title\":\"Guidelines to Offenses & Possible Sanctions Policy\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

The disciplinary code provides guidelines in terms of desirable behaviour.  In addition, it provides guidelines in terms of correcting unacceptable behaviour and/or unsatisfactory performance.

This guideline is not an exhaustive list. Any form of misconduct not listed herein will be dealt with in terms of the norms of similar offences. Employees may be charged for transgressions not necessarily contained herein. In serious instances of misconduct, the precedent as set out herein will be disregarded and a disciplinary inquiry imposed for a first offense. Mitigating and aggravating factors could also determine that the Chairperson uses his discretion to deviate from the recommended sanction and impose lighter or heavier sanction as the case may be.

Misconduct that is regarded as related or similar will considered collectively and therefore any previous disciplinary records in any related/similar category will be taken into consideration when applying progressive disciplinary action where applicable (e.g. poor time keeping, unauthorised absenteeism, arriving late/early for work are considered related/similar offenses).

GUIDELINES OF MISCONDUCT

ABSENTEEISM & TIMEKEEPING OFFENSES

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

1

Arriving for work late and/or leaving work early without permission and/or authorisation, and/or a valid reason

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Failing to inform your Line Manager of the fact that you will be unable to attend to work as a result of illness or otherwise within the timeframe required

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Taking of extended lunch/tea/smoke breaks and/or unauthorised breaks during working hours

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Poor time keeping and/or loafing and/or loitering and/or idling during working hours

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

5

Clock in irregularities: Clocking in on behalf of an employee and/or an employee clocking in on your behalf

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

6

Performing private work/activities during official working hours (e.g. gambling, watching movies, watching videos, etc.) that have an impact on work performance/output

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

7

Regular absence for reasons of illness which forms a pattern i.e. Mondays/Fridays etc. irrelevant whether a medical certificate is produced or not

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

8

Failure to report for work or duty without permission and/or authorization and/or a valid reason and/or informing management/ supervisors (Less than 3 days)

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

9

Desertion: Absence from work for 3 or more consecutive days without permission and/or authorization and/or a valid reason and/or informing management/ supervisors and/or a valid medical certificate

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

WORK STANDARD OFFENSES

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

Fourth Offense

Fifth Offense

1

Negligence: Failure to comply with a standard of care that would be reasonably expected for the completion and fulfilment of a duty/tasks.

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Gross negligence: A serious failure to comply with a standard of care that would be reasonably expected for the completion and fulfilment of a duty/tasks. Generally gross negligence would have had or could possibly have had a financial loss to the company.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Dereliction of duty: Failure to comply with a duty to perform a task or job function in terms of your allocated role and/or allocated responsibilities and/or job profile

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Failure to perform duties and responsibilities in a reasonable and diligent manner and/or in accordance with company standards

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

5

Failure to carry out instruction given by manager/supervisor and/or person having authority to do so

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

6

Breach of company policies and/or procedures and/or instructions

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

7

Failure to protect and/or promote the company at all times to the best of their ability and with due regard to the interests of management and fellow employees

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

8

Failure to stay up to date on a regular basis will all company communication platforms i.e. Slack, Emails, etc. 

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

9

Shortages in stock and/or material without a valid reason

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

10

Swapping of stock and/or agents’ stock without permission and/or authorisation and/or a valid reason

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

11

Performing a PRE-RICA on any sim card

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

12

Invoicing stock to the incorrect agent and/or agent’s account without a valid reason.

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

13

Failure to confirm receipt of stock following delivery via Self Service portals (USSD; my.awesim, Infield invoicing, etc.) / required process.

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

14

Failure to ensure agents confirm receipt of their stock following delivery via Self Service portals (USSD; my.awesim, Infield invoicing, etc.)/ required process.

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

15

Creating SYSPRO accounts to receive stock for personal and/or family members use without managements permission and/or authorisation

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

16

Creating SYSPRO accounts with fake and/or intentionally incorrect details

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

17

Changing SYSPRO account details without agents’ permission, this includes any payment details

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

18

RICA’ing stock in order to get paid on any account but your own (after obtaining managements permission and/or authorisation to create your own account)

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

19

Opening sealed packages and/or stock without permission and/or authorisation and/or a valid reason

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

20

Removing any items from sealed packages without permission and/or authorisation and/or a valid reason

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

21

Redeeming Reward Points on behalf of an agent/dealer without the permission of that agent/dealer

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

22

Any act by an employee to interfere with the normal operations of the company and/or damaging the company/agents/colleagues image and/or income/profits, etc.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

23

Failure to return the Smartload to Awesim within two (2) business days after a promotion has ended

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

24

Participation in an unlawful strike contradicting the LRA where the matters in dispute have not been processed through all relevant procedures set out in any law and/or agreement

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

25

Incitement to participation in a strike contradicting the LRA including – any concerted action by one or more employees to withdraw their labour and/or “go slow” and/or work to rule and/or otherwise interfere with the normal operations of the company in furtherance of a dispute and/or with the object of compelling management to take and/or refrain from taking any specific action where the matters in dispute have not been processed through all relevant procedures set out in any law and/or agreement

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

26

Invoicing Team: Negligence / Gross Negligence

  • Invoicing inaccuracy, invoicing errors or failure to process invoicing SR's with a standard of care and accuracy that would be reasonably expected.

Written Warning (Valid for 3 months)

Written Warning (Valid for 3 months)

Written Warning (Valid for 3 months)

Final Written Warning (Valid for 6months)

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

27

Customer Care Team: Negligence / Gross Negligence

  • Failure to uphold expected customer service standards: including but not limited to, inadequate handling of service requests (SRs), providing inaccurate information or responses, and failure to provide assistance or resolutions aligned with the customer's needs or expectations.
  • Lack of attention to customer context or history: neglecting to review and consider a customer's interaction history or previous requests, which could lead to service inconsistencies or unresolved issues.
  • Insufficient standard of care and accuracy in SR processing: not exercising the level of diligence, accuracy, and professionalism reasonably expected to process customer SRs.

Written Warning

(Valid for 3 months)

Written Warning

(Valid for 3 months)

Written Warning

(Valid for 3 months)

Final Written Warning

(Valid for 6months)

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

28

Submitting or processing Agent account merges incorrectly, providing incorrect information, or failing to follow the required steps and validations as outlined in the SOP.

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

29

Agents’ login credentials may not be used, accessed, or shared on any device other than the Agent’s own device. Any unauthorised or unethical use of such credentials, including transactions conducted without proper consent, is strictly prohibited.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

BEHAVIOURAL / CONDUCT RELATED OFFENSES

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

Fourth Offense

1

Smoking, eating or drinking in an area which prohibits such activities

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Sleeping at work and/or sleeping whilst on duty

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Insubordination and/or insolence and/or disgraceful and/or improper behaviour

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Wastage of material and/or stationary and/or supplies, etc.

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

5

Use of abusive and/or vulgar and/or offensive and/or inappropriate language

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

6

Intimidation/fighting and/or attempted fighting and/or assaulting and/or attempted assault

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

7

Any sexual harassment or intention of a sexual nature that is offensive and unwanted

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

8

Behaviour that constitutes a threat of harm on another person or property which could be verbal or physical actions.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

9

Gossiping and/or spreading rumours and/or false allegations/information and/or unconfirmed information

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

10

Injury to others as a result of rough play and/or negligence

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

11

Use of and/or in possession of a dangerous weapon

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

12

Unauthorised presence on company premises

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

13

Breach of company's dress code, including attire that may be viewed as unprofessional, disruptive, or inappropriate in the work environment.

Written Warning

(Valid for 3 months)

Written Warning

(Valid for 6 months)

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

VEHICLE & ASSET RELATED OFFENSES

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

1

Speeding and/or exceeding the national speed limit in a company vehicle and/or third parties’ vehicle

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Poor maintenance of and/or damage to employer’s and/or third parties’ vehicle(s) and/or equipment and/or assets which are under the control of the company. This includes sim cards and/or any type of promotional material/Rewards items that are being transported to agents/dealers.

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Reckless and/or negligent driving of company vehicle(s)

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Failing to comply with traffic regulations/legislation

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

5

Driving a vehicle without an appropriate and/or valid driver’s license and/or in breach of legislation in regard hereto

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

6

Driving and/or operating company vehicles and/or machinery and/or equipment under and/or whilst on a balance of probabilities under the influence of drugs and/or alcohol and/or intoxicating and/or illegal substances

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

7

Unauthorised conveying of passengers and/or non-employees and/or goods in company vehicle

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

8

Failure to maintain and/or service and/or repair and/or look after company vehicle

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

9

Failure to report damage to company vehicles and/or property and/or equipment and/or assets to the required person and within the required time frame

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

10

Removal and/or attempted removal of any company assets and/or equipment and/or material from the company premises without permission and/or authorisation

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

11

Willful and/or deliberate and/or attempt to damage or destroy company property and/or equipment and/or assets, etc.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

12

Unauthorised possession and/or use of company and/or another’s goods and/or possessions

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

DISHONESTY OFFENSES

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

1

Making false statements and/or issuing of false documentation with the intent to deceive or conceal the truth. Typical examples include lying, deception, insincerity, corruption, etc.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Fraud/Misappropriation: Making of false statements and/or issuing of false documentation with the intention to obtain some form of benefit and/or unjustified enrichment/payment

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Bribery/corruption: Any action in terms of which a third party is persuaded by any means to act in a manner which that person would not have normally acted/behaved

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Criminal offense and/or illegal activity and/or conviction and/or transgression and or any illegal activity or cause recognised in law

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

5

Accepting claim and/or commission and/or money and/or fees and/or gifts/rewards without managements permission and/or authorisation (Not part of remuneration)

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

6

Confirming/marking an agent/s attendance on the promotions bus when the agent was not on the bus or was not in attendance.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

IT RELATED OFFENSES

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

1

Unauthorised access to company information, systems or data

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Downloading and/or uploading and/or saving any personal unauthorised content on company computers and/or equipment (e.g. Music, Video’s, etc.)

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Uploading and/or downloading and/or distributing unauthorised software, media or files that violate copyright laws.

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Introducing viruses or malware into company networks or systems whether intentional or by negligence

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

5

Uploading and/or downloading and/or transmitting and/or distributing and/or in possession of any offensive material from the internet, intranet, cell phone, or otherwise

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

6

Uploading and/or removing and/or downloading and/or bypassing and/or changing computer software/hardware without permission and/or authorisation

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

7

Sharing confidential or proprietary information with unauthorised employees and/or third parties

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

8

Plugging in and/or making use of personal and/or unauthorised devices (i.e. USB devices, Wi-Fi devices, etc.) to company computers and/or devices and/or networks without permission which could potentially introduce malware, other security risks or slows systems/access

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

9

Obtaining and/or sharing and/or using and/or accessing log in details and/or accounts belonging to another, not yourself, for any Awesim or third party systems/accounts (my.awesim, Awesim App, Promo booking system, VPN, CMS, etc.).

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

DETRIMENTAL OFFENSES TO AWESIM

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

1

Bringing the company name into disrepute and/or possibly bringing the company name into disrepute and/or portraying a bad image of the company

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Breach of confidentiality/trust. Unauthorised disclosure of confidential information including but not limited to company trade secrets, confidential documentation, technical knowledge and data, systems, methods software processes, client lists or information, programmes, financial information, etc.

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Failing to disclose interest in another business. Being a member or partner of another business, whether you are involved in the day to day or not.

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Failure to disclose relationships (romantic, family, friends, etc.) of individuals working within a company that Awesim uses as a supplier or service provider. 

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

VIOLATION OF HEALTH & SAFETY

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

1

Not reporting an accident and/or injury on duty involving self immediately

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Not reporting an accident and/or injury on duty involving the company and/or another and/or third party immediately

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

3

Use of equipment and/or material in a manner that endangers own and/or another’s safety and/or health and/or the environment

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

4

Failure to observe and/or comply with the safety rules and regulations prescribed by the company

Written Warning

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

5

Failure and/or refusal to wear/use personal protective wear and/or equipment

Final Written Warning

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

ALCOHOL AND DRUG RELATED OFFENSES

No.

Nature of misconduct

First Offense

Second Offense

Third Offense

1

Incapable of working properly and/or reporting for work whilst on a balance of probabilities under the influence of drugs and/or alcohol and/or intoxicating and/or illegal substances

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

2

Use of and/or under the influence of and/or in possession of drugs and/or alcohol and/or intoxicating and/or illegal substances during working hours

Disciplinary Inquiry (Dismissal)

**Note: Where permission and/or authorisation is specified, written permission is required.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Disciplinary Code", "id": "338a97dc-9110-4c32-8413-58b69230642f", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Discipline", "id": "cfbd10cb-9200-4858-8c7e-48a16eef2e1e", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "HR policy", "id": "6dee3fb5-0aa6-432f-b8af-753794b08509", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2026-04-14T12:08:52.469+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/b450eb01-8818-4f00-b8ef-7c2c3ba6fa23" }, { "id": "cb7e2f84-96e8-4459-96f1-8d4442abc926", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"cb7e2f84-96e8-4459-96f1-8d4442abc926\",\"title\":\"Returning Agent (RA): Stock Order Qtys SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.\nPURPOSE\nThe purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to provide a clear framework for Customer Care (CC) Consultants on how to efficiently assist returning agents (by account and/or network) and how to manage their requests for SIM cards.\nSummary:\nThere are\ntwo categories\nfor Returning Agents:\nDefault Order Type (DOT):\nMarked as\nExpired\n– see\nExpired Accounts (04) NOT Paid - System Rules\nOR\nCustomer On Hold:\nY [Tick] and\nDate of Last Sale:\nNo orders in the past 2 years with\nCRM Audit Reason:\nMove from Expired to Agent because of payment\n1.1.\nPoor-Performing\nExpired Returning Agent (RA)\n1.2.\nGood-Performing\nExpired Returning Agent (RA)\nQuality Rating “EX”:\nAgent hasn’t received stock for a specific network in over a year but DOT is active.\nExpired Account with\nBlank Stock Order Table\nDefault Order Type (DOT):\nExpired\nPoor-Performing:\nDefault Order Type: Expired Accounts (04) NOT Paid -\nno\nlonger qualify for the Free RA Starter Package\n.\nAgent must\ndownload the app\nand purchase SIMs.\nIf they show good performance on the paid package,\nadditional Free SIMs will be allocated\nas per standard system performance algorithm.\nHow to identify\nPoor (Underperforming)\nLifetime Performance:\nExpired Returning Agent (RA)\nDOT: Expired Accounts (04) NOT Paid\nAllowed Balance: Nil\nAvailable Paid Qty: Paid SIMs available: Max 10 per stock code / 3 RPs per SIM\nUnderperforming expired agents:\n🔴⬇️Below (less than) 33% lifetime\nconnection %\n🔴⬇️Below (less than) 11% lifetime\nactivation %\nCheck the Intranet Report for lifetime performance by searching the Syspro Customer:\nExample (to follow)\nGood-Performing:\nDefault Order Type: Expired Accounts (04) NOT Paid - Order Limit(s)\nHow to identify\nGood\nLifetime Performance\n: Expired Returning Agent (RA)\nDOT: Expired Accounts (04) NOT Paid\nAllowed Balance: 10\nQty Allowed = 10\nMonthly Allowed = 10\nAvailable Paid Qty: Nil\nPerforming expired agents:\n⬆️Over 33% lifetime\nconnection %\n⬆️Over 11% lifetime\nactivation %\nCheck the Intranet Report for lifetime performance by searching the Syspro Customer:\nFor Stock_Category:\nVODACOM Unstuck\nMTN Starters\nCELLC ValuePack\nSim-Sonke Starters\nReturning Agent – Good/Poor-performing Expired (04) Flow\nVerification\nOnce all Returning Agent verification checks are completed,\nroute SR\nto:\nDropDown: Expired (04) ESC\nAMB ESC Update\nThe SR will route to\nAMB ESC (CC Ambassador Queue)\n.\nCC Ambassador must update\nDefault Order Type\nfrom\nExpired (04)\n→\nAccount Agent (0)\nCRM - Relationship - Edit Tree Structure - Select Forest [Expired Accounts] - Arrows -\nCRM Audit Reason:\nDOT 04 --> 0 (CRM). Account Agent.\nCC Ambassador selects\nDropDown →\nINFO SMS\nThe INFO SMS includes the Awesim App link so the agent can login to the App and;\nGood-Performing: Expired Returning Agent (RA) =\nPlace a\nFast Track (FT) Order to\nPoor-Performing: Expired Returning Agent (RA) =\nBuy\nthe Returning Agent package.\nAgent Benefit:\nQualify for 60 Reward Points on Delivery Confirmation.\nQuality Rating “EX”\nEX Stock Codes – Order Limit\nFor any stock code with\nQuality Rating “EX”\n, agents can only receive\nmax\n10 units per network\n, no matter how many networks they request.\nAgents who have\nnever\nreceived a specific network should follow the EX Stock Code rule of thumb: a maximum of 10 units per network.\nExample:\nAgent requests MTN, Telkom, and Vodacom.\n5 Vodacom (not EX)\n10 MTN (EX)\n10 Telkom (EX)\n=\n25 total units\n➡️\nDoes not qualify\n– minimum shipping requirement is\n30 SIMs\n.\nExpired Account with Blank Stock Order Table\nA blank table typically occurs because:\nThe system previously executed the Expired Allocation Process.\nThere has been no activity for over a month following that process.\nConsequently, the system has moved the account status back to Expired.\nAssessment Criteria\nSince the Stock Order Table is unavailable, consultants must assess performance manually using the Intranet Report to search for the Syspro Customer.\nPerformance Category\nMetrics (Lifetime)\nAction Required\nGood-Performing\n↑ Over 33%\nconnection %\n↑ Over 11% activation %\nManual Order:\nThere is no system function to automatically add quantities back. The order must be placed by\nHead Office\n.\nPoor-Performing\n↓ Below 33%\nconnection %\n↓ Below 11% activation %\nStock Code Flag Query:\nA query must be logged to specifically add\nBuy SIMs\nfunctionality for the agent.\nProcess Flow for Blank Tables\nStep 1: Verify the \\\"Expired\\\" status and confirm the Stock Order table is empty.\nStep 2: Run the Intranet Report for lifetime connection and activation percentages.\nStep 3:\nBased on the results, route the request:\nGood:\nEscalate to\nHead Office\nfor manual quantity allocation.\nPoor: Log a Stock Code Flag Query to enable the \\\"Buy SIMs\\\" package via the app.\nNote:\nWe\ndo not\nhave the system functionality to add “free SIMs” to an account under Allowed Balance, as this is linked to the automatic performance calculator.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"cb7e2f84-96e8-4459-96f1-8d4442abc926\",\"title\":\"Returning Agent (RA): Stock Order Qtys SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.

PURPOSE

The purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to provide a clear framework for Customer Care (CC) Consultants on how to efficiently assist returning agents (by account and/or network) and how to manage their requests for SIM cards.

Summary:

There are two categories for Returning Agents:

  1. Default Order Type (DOT): Marked as Expired – see Expired Accounts (04) NOT Paid - System Rules
    OR
    Customer On Hold: Y [Tick] and Date of Last Sale: No orders in the past 2 years with CRM Audit Reason: Move from Expired to Agent because of payment

    1.1. Poor-Performing Expired Returning Agent (RA)
    1.2. Good-Performing Expired Returning Agent (RA)
  2. Quality Rating “EX”: Agent hasn’t received stock for a specific network in over a year but DOT is active.
  3. Expired Account with Blank Stock Order Table

Default Order Type (DOT): Expired 

Poor-Performing: Default Order Type: Expired Accounts (04) NOT Paid - no longer qualify for the Free RA Starter Package.

Agent must download the app and purchase SIMs.

If they show good performance on the paid package, additional Free SIMs will be allocated as per standard system performance algorithm.


How to identify Poor (Underperforming) Lifetime Performance: Expired Returning Agent (RA)

  • DOT: Expired Accounts (04) NOT Paid
  • Allowed Balance: Nil
  • Available Paid Qty: Paid SIMs available: Max 10 per stock code / 3 RPs per SIM



Underperforming expired agents:
