25 Dec 2025–04 Jan 2025

Outlook (info@ / RTS)

Out-of-office message active.

05 Jan 2026 (08h00)

All Systems

Normal operations resume.

YE Ops 2025/26 Schedule

IT HelpDesk Ticket Templates

1. Twilio Chat Auto Response and Closure - Company Event: Friday, 14 November 2025

Hi Team,

Please see below the Chat Auto Response and Closure for the scheduled Company Event: Friday, 14 November 2025.

Thursday, 13 November 16:00 - 17:00

\\\"We have stopped processing chat msgs 'til Mon, 17 Nov 2025. If you need to speak to us urgently, pls call 011 268 1348 before 17h00 and we’ll call you back.\\\"

Thursday, 13 November 17:01 - Friday, 14 November 17:00

\\\"Thank you for your message. We are no longer processing msgs due to a staff function. Please send us another msg on Mon, 17 Nov after 08:00.\\\"

Any questions, please let us know.

Thank you.

2. Twilio Chat Auto Response and Closure - Public Holiday “Day of Reconciliation”: Tuesday, 16 December 2025 - Chat Auto Response & Closure

Hi team 

Please see below the Chat Auto Response and Closure for the Public Holiday - Day of Reconciliation.

Monday, 15 December 16:00 - 17:00

\\\"We have stopped processing msgs due to the Public Holiday tomorrow. If you need to speak to us urgently, call 011 268 1348 before 17h00 and we’ll call you back.\\\"

Monday, 15 December 17:01 - 00:00

\\\"We have stopped processing msgs due to the Public Holiday tomorrow. Please send us another msg Wed, 17 Dec after 08:00.\\\"

Tuesday, 16 December 00:01 - 17:00

\\\"Thank you for your message. It is a Public Holiday - Day of Reconciliation. Please send us another msg tomorrow Wed, 17 Dec after 08:00.\\\"

Thank you.

3. CMS - MISReport: \\\"CMS - CC Roles - Check Status\\\" - Remove Creations from automation proc

Hi Team,

In preparation for year-end operations, please assist wrt the below daily \\\"Job\\\"

Task: Remove Creations from automation proc

Details:

  • Remove Creations: [Specify date] December 2026 at 17h00 (date to align with 2026 / 2027 YE Planner)
  • Reactivate Creations automation proc : 5 January 2027 at 07h00 in line with January Call Backs Proc

4. CMS - YE 2025 - BitCo Table Incomings + Auto-System SRs

Hi Team,

In preparation for year-end operations, please assist wrt auto-replies and their auto-closures for Incoming BitCo SRs. SMSes (all SMSes).

1. Wednesday, 24th December from 13:45 to 14:29 Notification: We are no longer processing msgs for 2025. If you need to speak to us urgently before we close, call 011 268 1348 before 14h30 and we’ll call you back.

2. Wednesday, 24th December from 14:30 'til Fri, 4th January (midnight) >> There is no weekend message.

Notification: We wish all our agents a wonderful festive season. We are no longer processing msgs for 2025. Please SMS again on Mon 5th of Jan 2026, 08:00 when we reopen.

Thank you.

NOTE TO CCA : Head office will close at 15h00 on Wed, 24 December 2025 - however this is confidential and will only be communicated on Slack #general on the day.

Above times allow us to process the last of any queries.

Any questions, please let us know.

5. My.Awesim, USSD & App - YE 2025 - Order functionality (Closure & Reactivation)

Hi Team

In preparation for year-end operations, please see below system notifications:

1. My.Awesim, USSD & App notification

  • 8th 'til 16th December 2025 (midnight)

Notification: \\\"Please note - orders placed during this period (08th - 16th Dec) will be processed, invoiced & shipped ahead of the festive season.\\\"

2. My.Awesim, USSD & App notification

  • Wednesday, 17th December till Thursday, 18th December 'til 17:00

*Notification: \\\"Please note - orders placed today & tomorrow (17th – 18th Dec) will be processed, however we cannot guarantee delivery due to the festive season.\\\"

3. Disable option to order stock: My.Awesim & USSD notification

Disable the option to order stock - ‘disabled’ on Thursday, 18th December 2025 from 17:00 'til 04th January 2026 (midnight).

Notification: \\\"Please note - ordering is now closed (18th Dec – 04th Jan). You will be able to order again from 5 Jan 2026.

It will need to be re-activated from the 04th January 2026 at 23:59 (midnight) >> 5 Jan 2026.

4. Disable option to order stock: App

Disable: 18 December 2025 17h00
Reactivate: 1 Jan 2026 (Midnight)

Notification: \\\"Please note - ordering is now closed (18th – 31st Dec). You will be able to order again from 1 Jan 2026 – App Only!”

See below screenshot of Last Year's notification:

6. Twilio Chat Auto reply & Auto Closure Templates - YE 2025/26

Hi Team

In preparation for year-end operations, please assist with putting in place auto-replies and auto-closures for Chats whilst closed for the festive season. The auto-reply + chat-closure (ONLY for *NEW chats within the following timeframes), needs to be activated:

*chats already in the system will be addressed and get the 24hr closure if not completed.

Message 1: Wednesday, 24th December from 13:45 to 14:29:29

Notification: We are no longer processing msgs for 2025. If you need to speak to us urgently before we close, call 011 268 1348 before 14h30 and we’ll call you back.


Message 2: Wednesday, 24th December from 14:30 'til 31st December 00:00 (midnight)

Notification: We wish all our agents a wonderful festive season. Please msg us again from 08:00 on Mon the 5th Jan 2026, when we reopen.


Message 3: 1st – 2nd Jan 2026 >> the weekend message can kick back in again for the weekend (2nd Jan – 4th Jan 2026 17h00 (SRs can start to generate again to address on our return).

Notification: Thank you for your message. Happy New Year. Please msg us again on Mon 5th of Jan 2026, 08:00 when we reopen.

Any questions, please let me know.  
Thank you.

7. CMS - YE 2025 - SRs Descriptions, TO INVOICE DropDown & \\\"January Follow Up\\\" Call List

Hi Arno / Team,

In preparation for year-end operations, please adjust Incoming Queue Source Service Request (SR) Description(s) as outlined below:

Part 1: Service Request (SR) Notifications

See attached proc table with details.

FestiveClosureNotifications.xlsx

  1. FestiveClosure - Qualifying Agents
    Date: 2025-12-17 (Midnight) to 2025-12-18 17h00
    Message: Advise agent that they can order, but that we cannot guarantee delivery before we close. They may want to rather wait to order in Jan.
  1. FestiveClosure - Returning and New Agents
    Date: 2025-12-15 (17h00) to 2026-01-04 (Midnight)
    Message:     This is either a NEW or RETURNING Agent. Their package will only be invoiced and sent to them in JANUARY. This is to avoid RTSes. SR Comment: JANUARY FOLLOW UP


  1. FestiveClosure - All Agents
    Date: 2025-12-18 (17h00) to 2026-01-04 (Midnight)
    Message:     If needed, advise the agent that ordering is closed for 2025. They can contact us in the New Year - 5th Jan - to process an order. SR Comment: JANUARY FOLLOW UP

+Inform the below January Call Back List

Part 2: DropDown(s)

TO INVOICE DropDown \\\"Block\\\" and CMS Notification
Date: 2025-12-18 (17h00) to 2026-01-04 (Midnight)

Notification: If needed, advise the agent that ordering is closed for 2025. They can contact us in the New Year - 5th Jan - to process an order.

Part 3: January Call Backs Proc

With reference to:
Table created called “ServiceOrder.[dbo].[JanuaryCallBacks]

Export List w SR Comment: JANUARY FOLLOW UP
Import List: Custom Uploads (AI) Queue
Date: Tues, 6th Jan 2026

8. CMS - New Agent Courtesy SRs

Stop: 24th Dec 2025.
Restart: 5 Jan 2026 and share

1. Manually stop the the Job.
The name of the job is: CMS Auto Courtesy Calls
And it runs this proc: AddAutoCourtesyCalls_NewAgents

9. Out of Office Notifications for info@ and RTS Inbox for the following events

info@ & RTS Mailboxes

  • 14 Nov 2025 (Company Event):
    “Awesim is closed for the day due to a company event. We will reopen Mon, 17 Nov.”
  • 16 Dec 2025 (Public Holiday):
    “Awesim is closed for the day due to a Public Holiday. We will reopen Wed, 17 Dec.”
  • 24 Dec 2025–04 Jan 2026 (Festive Closure):
    “Awesim offices are closed for the festive season. We reopen on Mon, 05 Jan 2026.”

Slack #general

  • Post YE closure message on 24 Dec (Head Office early closure 15h00) - c/o CEO, Greg S.

  1. Date: Friday 14 November 2025 - Company Event

Good day,

Awesim is closed for the day Friday, 14 November 2025 due to a company event. 

We will be open and available again on Monday, 17 November 2025, to respond to your email.

Thank you and have a great day further.

The Awesim Team 

  1. Date: Tuesday, 16 December 2025 - Public Holiday

Good day,

Awesim is closed for the day, Tue, 16 December 2025 due to the Public Holiday Day of Reconciliation. 

We will be open and available again on Wed, 17 December 2025, to respond to your email.

Thank you, and have a great day.

The Awesim Team


  1. Date: Wednesday, 24 December 2025 to 5 January 2025 - Office closed

Good day,

Thank you for reaching out to Awesim. We are currently out of the office, this festive season.

Our offices will be closed from Wed, 24 December 2025, and will reopen on Monday, 05 January 2026. During this period, we won't be available to respond to emails.

Thank you, we look forward to connecting with you upon our return. In the meantime, we wish you a joyful and restful Festive Season.

The Awesim Team

10. IVR Setup (YE)

Ensure the IVR system is updated and ready for the Year-End period.

    • Update Scripts: Review and update messages to reflect YE operating hours and process changes.
    • Test Functionality: Check call routing, voicemail, and message clarity.
    • Configure System: Schedule holiday greetings, after-hours routing, and closure dates.
    • Communicate Updates: Inform IT, Operations, and Support teams once setup is complete.
    • Backup Plan: Confirm contingency routing and escalation procedures are in place.

Voice Recordings

Set-Up IVR - YE 2025/26

11. maintenance Settings Table

To be turned off and set to zero at COB on 24 December 2025, and turned back on at the start of the new year 5 Jan 2026 and /or in line with below Filters and Deadlines:

  • New Leads - turn off from 15th Dec 5pm (in line with Creations Closed)
  • Retention Call - turn off 23rd Dec COB (day before we Close)
  • Unreachable Retry - turn off 23rd Dec COB (day before we Close)

1. Filters to be turned Off and qty



2. Filters to be turned back on and qty

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "SOP Template", "id": "20027f4a-e23a-4c23-bd9d-c28d2bd2dbc7", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "YE", "id": "154f5ce7-4a7e-4736-ba50-91dcf1597400", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2026-01-23T09:05:02.438+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/3014c301-89d3-46ff-9073-39519afe6ab5" }, { "id": "1e349b80-9ee1-403a-b4b8-a97d27898e95", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"1e349b80-9ee1-403a-b4b8-a97d27898e95\",\"title\":\"CMS - Twilio (WhatsApp) \\\"End Chat\\\" Button SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.\nPURPOSE\nThe purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to ensure consistent and efficient handling and closure of Service Requests (SRs) by Customer Care (CC) Consultants, reducing delays in handling time reporting caused by open-ended chat waiting sessions.\nCMS - Twilio (WhatsApp) \\\"End Chat\\\" Button\nOnce the agent's query has been resolved in line with the\nCustomer Care Service Request (SR) Handling SOP\n, the CC Consultant must click the End Chat button, which will display the confirmation message below.\nWe have closed this Service Request because we believe we have resolved your query. Please rate our service here: Need more help? Send us a WhatsApp message to start a new session.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"1e349b80-9ee1-403a-b4b8-a97d27898e95\",\"title\":\"CMS - Twilio (WhatsApp) \\\"End Chat\\\" Button SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.

PURPOSE

The purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to ensure consistent and efficient handling and closure of Service Requests (SRs) by Customer Care (CC) Consultants, reducing delays in handling time reporting caused by open-ended chat waiting sessions.

CMS - Twilio (WhatsApp) \\\"End Chat\\\" Button

Once the agent's query has been resolved in line with the Customer Care Service Request (SR) Handling SOP, the CC Consultant must click the End Chat button, which will display the confirmation message below.

We have closed this Service Request because we believe we have resolved your query. Please rate our service here: <https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/BGDNK6Z> Need more help? Send us a WhatsApp message to start a new session.   

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2026-01-20T07:55:52.647+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/1e349b80-9ee1-403a-b4b8-a97d27898e95" }, { "id": "af14618f-3aa6-4ea7-a0b6-1d8be3db0c79", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"af14618f-3aa6-4ea7-a0b6-1d8be3db0c79\",\"title\":\"CC Queue Management SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to ensure an efficient balance between Social Media (Chats) and Calls in the Customer Care (CC) queue by monitoring workload distribution, optimizing response times and resource allocation, and providing data-driven insights for informed decision-making.\nScope\nThis SOP applies to the CCA responsible for tracking queue performance, identifying trends, and offering recommendations to optimize efficiency.\nResponsibilities\nCustomer Care Administrator (CCA):\nActively monitor SM (Chats) and call queues, analyze workload distribution, and provide insights to the supervisor and manager to support queue management decisions.\nSupervisors/ Line Manager:\nOversee queue trends, address backlog issues, and implement improvements.\nSYSTEM RULES\nFirst In, First Out (FIFO)\n- Orders tasks based on date (oldest first).\nPriority Override\n- DEV's highest display priority (e.g., 20) moves those SRs to the top.\nSR Type Hierarchy\n- Specific SR types take precedence over others in this order:\nTop Priority SRs (oldest first within this group):\nEmail w Number\nSMSd 43203\nNEW REQUEST\nBASE CHANGE\nUSSD\nSR Specific based on Role update to Add Specific (ASP)\nNext Priority (FIFO):\nSocial Media (Twilio WhatsApp Chat)\nCalls (Inbound & Proactive)\nQueue Monitoring Process\nDaily Task Updates\n07h00 - 08h00:\nUpdated roles to SM only in preparation for the next day.\nMIS Reports Tasks:\nCMS - Change CC roles\nBOTH\n(AR/RR) (AA/RA) (ASP/RSP) at\n08h00\nCMS - Check CC Role Status\nFilter proactive calls [CUSTOM UPLOAD and / or UPLOAD LIST] when ever SM and Courtesy Calls are manageable.\nChange CC roles\nUpdate Roles\nto SM Only! at the end of COB in preparation for the next day: 07h00 - 08h00 MISReports - [CMS - Change CC roles (CC/SM/BOTH) (AR/RR) (AA/RA) (ASP/RSP)] MISReports >>> [CMS - Check CC Role Status]\nCC Roles Update:\nUpdate Roles to BOTH at 08:00, Change CC roles to RAM (AR / RR, AA / RA) for CC Ambassadors and CC team on the 07:00 shift.\nChecking Queue Status - CCA\nLog in to the queue management system at the start of the shift and capture a screenshot.\nReview current call and chat volumes.\nIdentify peak activity periods and potential bottlenecks.\nTask Priority:\nCCA\nCC SUP (2IC)\n– When CCA is on leave.\nMonitoring Queue Performance Metrics - CC SUP\nCheck response times and resolution times for chats and calls in alignment with\nSurveyMonkey (WhatsApp) Chat Rating Daily Report SOP\nas one of the measuring tools.\nCompare current stats against set targets (e.g., average handling time, response time, completion rate).\nIdentify any deviations or trends that may require adjustments.\nProviding Insights and Recommendations (CCA & CC SUP)\nCompile key observations and performance trends.\nNotify the supervisor of any emerging issues (e.g., high backlog, underutilized agents).\nSuggest staffing adjustments, training needs, or process improvements where applicable.\nClose-of-Business (COB) Review\nAt the end of each business day, the following steps must be completed to ensure accurate queue monitoring and documentation:\n✅\nCapture and share a screenshot\nof the queue management dashboard.\n💾\nSave the screenshot in the following location:\nY:\\\\Customer Care SOPs\\\\CC Management Queue\\\\2025\\\\25_Apr\nCC Roles Update:\nUpdate roles to\nSM Only\nat the end of COB in preparation for the next day (\n07h00 – 08h00\n).\nPerform the following tasks via\nMISReports\n:\n[CMS - Change CC roles (CC/SM/BOTH) (AR/RR) (AA/RA) (ASP/RSP)] - select\nSM Only\n[CMS - Check CC Role Status]\n🧹\nEnsure all chats are resolved\n— no unresolved chats should remain in the queue by COB.\n📊\nDocument key findings\n, such as queue fluctuations and any critical issues encountered during the day.\n📝\nPrepare a summary report\nfor the supervisor and manager, if required.\nEscalation Process\nIf the queue becomes unmanageable, notify the supervisor and / or line manager immediately on\n#\ncc-queue-management-filters\n(Slack Channel)\nEscalate any system-related issues affecting queue performance to IT support on\n#critical-support\n(Slack Channel) refer to\n#calling_problems - Tech Down Time SOP\nContinuous Improvement\nRegularly review queue trends to identify recurring challenges.\nSuggest improvements to enhance queue efficiency and service quality.\nParticipate in team discussions to refine queue management strategies.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"af14618f-3aa6-4ea7-a0b6-1d8be3db0c79\",\"title\":\"CC Queue Management SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.


PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to ensure an efficient balance between Social Media (Chats) and Calls in the Customer Care (CC) queue by monitoring workload distribution, optimizing response times and resource allocation, and providing data-driven insights for informed decision-making.

Scope

This SOP applies to the CCA responsible for tracking queue performance, identifying trends, and offering recommendations to optimize efficiency.

Responsibilities

  1. Customer Care Administrator (CCA): Actively monitor SM (Chats) and call queues, analyze workload distribution, and provide insights to the supervisor and manager to support queue management decisions.
  2. Supervisors/ Line Manager: Oversee queue trends, address backlog issues, and implement improvements.

SYSTEM RULES

  1. First In, First Out (FIFO) - Orders tasks based on date (oldest first).
  2. Priority Override - DEV's highest display priority (e.g., 20) moves those SRs to the top.
  3. SR Type Hierarchy - Specific SR types take precedence over others in this order:

Top Priority SRs (oldest first within this group):

  • Email w Number
  • SMSd 43203
  • NEW REQUEST
  • BASE CHANGE
  • USSD
  • SR Specific based on Role update to Add Specific (ASP)

    Next Priority (FIFO):
  • Social Media (Twilio WhatsApp Chat)
  • Calls (Inbound & Proactive)

Queue Monitoring Process

Daily Task Updates

  1. 07h00 - 08h00: Updated roles to SM only in preparation for the next day.
  2. MIS Reports Tasks:
  3. CMS - Change CC roles BOTH (AR/RR) (AA/RA) (ASP/RSP) at 08h00
  4. CMS - Check CC Role Status
  5. Filter proactive calls [CUSTOM UPLOAD and / or UPLOAD LIST] when ever SM and Courtesy Calls are manageable.

Change CC roles

  1. Update Roles to SM Only! at the end of COB in preparation for the next day: 07h00 - 08h00 MISReports - [CMS - Change CC roles (CC/SM/BOTH) (AR/RR) (AA/RA) (ASP/RSP)] MISReports >>> [CMS - Check CC Role Status] 
  2. CC Roles Update: Update Roles to BOTH at 08:00, Change CC roles to RAM (AR / RR, AA / RA) for CC Ambassadors and CC team on the 07:00 shift.

Checking Queue Status - CCA

  • Log in to the queue management system at the start of the shift and capture a screenshot.
  • Review current call and chat volumes.
  • Identify peak activity periods and potential bottlenecks.

Task Priority:

  1. CCA
  2. CC SUP (2IC) – When CCA is on leave.

Monitoring Queue Performance Metrics - CC SUP

  • Check response times and resolution times for chats and calls in alignment with SurveyMonkey (WhatsApp) Chat Rating Daily Report SOP as one of the measuring tools.
  • Compare current stats against set targets (e.g., average handling time, response time, completion rate).
  • Identify any deviations or trends that may require adjustments.

Providing Insights and Recommendations (CCA & CC SUP)

  • Compile key observations and performance trends.
  • Notify the supervisor of any emerging issues (e.g., high backlog, underutilized agents).
  • Suggest staffing adjustments, training needs, or process improvements where applicable.

Close-of-Business (COB) Review

At the end of each business day, the following steps must be completed to ensure accurate queue monitoring and documentation:

  • ✅ Capture and share a screenshot of the queue management dashboard.
  • 💾 Save the screenshot in the following location:
    Y:\\\\Customer Care SOPs\\\\CC Management Queue\\\\2025\\\\25_Apr
  • CC Roles Update: Update roles to SM Only at the end of COB in preparation for the next day (07h00 – 08h00).
  • Perform the following tasks via MISReports:
    • [CMS - Change CC roles (CC/SM/BOTH) (AR/RR) (AA/RA) (ASP/RSP)] - select SM Only
    • [CMS - Check CC Role Status]
  • 🧹 Ensure all chats are resolved — no unresolved chats should remain in the queue by COB.
  • 📊 Document key findings, such as queue fluctuations and any critical issues encountered during the day.
  • 📝 Prepare a summary report for the supervisor and manager, if required.

Escalation Process

  • If the queue becomes unmanageable, notify the supervisor and / or line manager immediately on
    #cc-queue-management-filters (Slack Channel)
  • Escalate any system-related issues affecting queue performance to IT support on #critical-support (Slack Channel) refer to #calling_problems - Tech Down Time SOP

Continuous Improvement

  • Regularly review queue trends to identify recurring challenges.
  • Suggest improvements to enhance queue efficiency and service quality.
  • Participate in team discussions to refine queue management strategies.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "CC", "id": "ff2a290e-4d6e-4cb0-9faa-25872bd02ba0", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "cc queues", "id": "99537c21-9e8e-4c3f-84f4-0c7a5ea8c964", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "filters", "id": "3b484c2b-2b04-4f64-b177-888a8eab6cce", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "queue", "id": "8066546a-91f2-4bad-9cd9-d5dea644fee6", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2026-01-15T09:47:05.217+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/af14618f-3aa6-4ea7-a0b6-1d8be3db0c79" }, { "id": "227024c7-4d87-4683-8f06-8c91a6378b02", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"227024c7-4d87-4683-8f06-8c91a6378b02\",\"title\":\"MTN SIM Error(s) - “Restart the phone” OR No Connection\",\"content\":\"\nWe are receiving multiple agent queries related to the errors below. Please note that there appear to be\ntwo separate issues\n:\n1. RICA & Connected Successfully – Message: “Restart the phone”\nThe SIM connects and an MSISDN is issued, but the agent is prompted to restart the device.\n2. MTN SIM – Possible Defect (No Connection)\nThe SIM\ndoes not connect at all\n, and the device is unable to read the SIM.\nAction Required\nPlease escalate\nall\nagent queries affected by either of these errors and include the following details in the SR Comments:\nWhich MTN error is being experienced:\n#1 – Restart the Phone\nOR\n#2 – SIM does not connect at all\n2. INV impacted:\nInclude examples of affected SIM serials\n3. Optional:\nScreenshot of the error message (for Error #1)\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"227024c7-4d87-4683-8f06-8c91a6378b02\",\"title\":\"MTN SIM Error(s) - “Restart the phone” OR No Connection\",\"content\":\"

We are receiving multiple agent queries related to the errors below. Please note that there appear to be two separate issues:

1. RICA & Connected Successfully – Message: “Restart the phone”
 The SIM connects and an MSISDN is issued, but the agent is prompted to restart the device.

2. MTN SIM – Possible Defect (No Connection)
The SIM does not connect at all, and the device is unable to read the SIM.Action Required

Please escalate all agent queries affected by either of these errors and include the following details in the SR Comments:

  1. Which MTN error is being experienced:
    •  #1 – Restart the Phone OR
    •  #2 – SIM does not connect at all

2. INV impacted: Include examples of affected SIM serials
3. Optional: Screenshot of the error message (for Error #1)

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2026-01-13T07:49:08.699+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/227024c7-4d87-4683-8f06-8c91a6378b02" }, { "id": "6b71d27c-337e-4230-8f3a-31e1e37549ae", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"6b71d27c-337e-4230-8f3a-31e1e37549ae\",\"title\":\"Code of Conduct\",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nThis Code of Conduct outlines the standards of behaviour expected from both employees and Awesim to ensure a workplace aligned with ethical, legal, and professional principles. Management, leadership, and employees share responsibility for fostering a positive, respectful, and productive environment that upholds Awesims’ values and reputation.\nLEGAL PRINCIPLES\nThis Code of Conduct aligns with South African legislation, including but not limited to:\nBasic Conditions of Employment Act, Act 75 of 1997\nLabour Relations Act, Act 66 of 1995\nEmployment Equity Act, Act 55 of 1998\nProtection of Personal Information Act, Act 4 of 2013 (POPIA)\nOccupational Health and Safety Act, Act 85 of 1993\nPOLICY/PROCEDURE\nGENERAL PRINCIPLES\nAwesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd is committed to maintaining an ethical, respectful, and professional workplace. This requires shared accountability between the organisation, management, the leadership team, and employees.\nOrganisational Responsibilities\n:\nProvide a safe and healthy work environment in line with occupational health and safety standards.\nEnsure fair and non-discriminatory treatment of all employees.\nProtect employee privacy and manage personal information in accordance with POPIA.\nEstablish clear policies and provide the necessary resources and training to employees.\nFoster a culture of inclusion, respect, and support for diversity.\nManagement and Leadership Responsibilities\n:\nLead by example, modelling the ethical and professional behaviour expected of employees.\nCommunicate company policies, expectations, and changes effectively.\nProvide regular feedback, support employee development, and recognise achievements.\nAddress misconduct promptly and fairly.\nEncourage an open-door policy for concerns or grievances.\nEmployee Responsibilities\n:\nComply with company policies, procedures, and all applicable legal frameworks.\nComply with the terms and conditions of their contract of employment, working hours, and the allocated 1 hour break (lunch, tea, coffee, smoke breaks, etc.) taken at times in line with operational requirements and with managements agreement.\nPerform duties with diligence, integrity, professionalism, and to the required minimum standard.\nCarry out all reasonable and lawful instructions given by management or an authorised person.\nContribute positively to the organisational culture and work environment.\nCONDUCT AND BEHAVIOUR EXPECTATIONS\nProfessionalism and Respect\n:\nEmployees are expected to:\nTreat colleagues, clients, Agents, and stakeholders with courtesy and respect.\nExhibit professionalism to uphold and protect the company’s reputation.\nEmbrace diversity and avoid discriminatory, offensive, gossiping, or harassing behaviour.\nTreat colleagues and stakeholders courteously, even when opinions differ.\nBe transparent in decision-making and share relevant information unless restricted by managent.\nProtect the dignity, privacy, and confidentiality of others.\nManagement and leadership are expected to:\nFoster a respectful work environment and address any issues of disrespect or harassment.\nAct as role models for professional conduct and inclusivity.\nIntegrity and Ethics\n:\nEmployees are expected to:\nAct honestly and in good faith, prioritising the interests of Awesim.\nRefrain from using company resources for personal gain or improper purposes.\nUphold the company’s values:\nTake Care\nHave Respect\nBe a Team Player\nGo the Extra Mile\nChoose What Is Right\nThe organisation commits to:\nOperating transparently and ethically in all business dealings.\nProviding channels for employees to report improper or unethical conduct safely and confidentially.\nConfidentiality and Privacy\n:\nEmployees must:\nKeep sensitive company and client information confidential unless disclosure is legally required.\nNot discuss or share remuneration or employment terms with fellow colleagues or unauthorised parties.\nThe organisation will:\nEnsure secure handling and storage of employee and client data in compliance with POPIA.\nUSE OF COMPANY RESOURCES\nEmployees are responsible for:\nUsing company resources efficiently and minimising waste.\nSafeguarding company property and reporting damage or misuse in line with Awesims'\nAsset & Equipment Policy\n.\nManagement and leadership are responsible for:\nEnsuring employees have the tools and resources necessary to perform their duties effectively.\nMonitoring resource use and implementing measures to reduce waste and inefficiency.\nDRESS CODE\nEmployees are required to dress appropriately for their role, ensuring neatness and professionalism. Specific guidelines include:\nField-based staff dress code:\nSales field staff (including Regional Team Leaders) are required to wear their allocated Awesim uniform every working day (Awesim/Network branded golf shirts).\nOn cold days/seasons, Sales Field staff (including Regional Team Leaders) are permitted to wear a plain jersey or jacket, over their Awesim/Network golf shirt, that is presentable and appropriate; however, it must not be branded with other companies' logos (e.g Hugo Boss, Lacoste, etc.). It is preferable, where possible, for staff to wear their Awesim/Telkom branded jackets.\nShorts, 3/4 pants, and tracksuit pants are not permitted to be worn in the field or during client or network engagement. Staff must wear long pants (e.g. chinos, jeans, cargo pants) every working day and when in the field or during client/network engagement events.\nOpen or inappropriate shoes (e.g. slops, crocs, slippers, etc.) are not permitted to be worn in the field or during client or network engagement. Closed shoes (e.g. takkies, etc.) must be worn every working day and when in the field or during client or network engagement events.\nProtective or specialised clothing must be worn when required for safety or when executing tasks that require personal protective equipment (PPE).\nHead office based staff dress code:\nDress code for head office-based staff is casual (e.g. jeans, top, dress, etc.). All outfits or clothing must be appropriate for work.\nFridays are usually casual days, where employees are encouraged to wear their Awesim T-shirts or a funky colourful shirt/outfit.\nA smart casual dress code is required for client or service provider meetings/engagements.\nProtective or specialised clothing must be worn when required for safety or when executing tasks that require personal protective equipment (PPE).\nManagement will:\nCommunicate and enforce dress code expectations fairly and consistently.\nPROMOTING CORPORATE CITIZENSHIP\nEmployees are encouraged to:\nConsider the environmental, social, and economic impact of their actions.\nSupport community engagement initiatives and contribute to Awesims' sustainability goals.\nThe organisation will:\nAct as a responsible corporate citizen by prioritising sustainability and community well-being.\nCOMPLIANCE\nAll employees, management, and leadership are expected to comply with this Code of Conduct. Non-compliance will be addressed in accordance with the company’s disciplinary code and applicable legislation. Misconduct, unethical behaviour, or breaches of confidentiality may lead to disciplinary action, including termination where warranted.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"6b71d27c-337e-4230-8f3a-31e1e37549ae\",\"title\":\"Code of Conduct\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

This Code of Conduct outlines the standards of behaviour expected from both employees and Awesim to ensure a workplace aligned with ethical, legal, and professional principles. Management, leadership, and employees share responsibility for fostering a positive, respectful, and productive environment that upholds Awesims’ values and reputation.

LEGAL PRINCIPLES

This Code of Conduct aligns with South African legislation, including but not limited to:

  1. Basic Conditions of Employment Act, Act 75 of 1997
  2. Labour Relations Act, Act 66 of 1995
  3. Employment Equity Act, Act 55 of 1998
  4. Protection of Personal Information Act, Act 4 of 2013 (POPIA)
  5. Occupational Health and Safety Act, Act 85 of 1993

POLICY/PROCEDURE

GENERAL PRINCIPLES

Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd is committed to maintaining an ethical, respectful, and professional workplace. This requires shared accountability between the organisation, management, the leadership team, and employees.

Organisational Responsibilities:

    • Provide a safe and healthy work environment in line with occupational health and safety standards.
    • Ensure fair and non-discriminatory treatment of all employees.
    • Protect employee privacy and manage personal information in accordance with POPIA.
    • Establish clear policies and provide the necessary resources and training to employees.
    • Foster a culture of inclusion, respect, and support for diversity.

Management and Leadership Responsibilities:

    • Lead by example, modelling the ethical and professional behaviour expected of employees.
    • Communicate company policies, expectations, and changes effectively.
    • Provide regular feedback, support employee development, and recognise achievements.
    • Address misconduct promptly and fairly.
    • Encourage an open-door policy for concerns or grievances.

Employee Responsibilities:

    • Comply with company policies, procedures, and all applicable legal frameworks.
    • Comply with the terms and conditions of their contract of employment, working hours, and the allocated 1 hour break (lunch, tea, coffee, smoke breaks, etc.) taken at times in line with operational requirements and with managements agreement.
    • Perform duties with diligence, integrity, professionalism, and to the required minimum standard.
    • Carry out all reasonable and lawful instructions given by management or an authorised person.
    • Contribute positively to the organisational culture and work environment.

CONDUCT AND BEHAVIOUR EXPECTATIONS

  1. Professionalism and Respect:

Employees are expected to:

    • Treat colleagues, clients, Agents, and stakeholders with courtesy and respect.
    • Exhibit professionalism to uphold and protect the company’s reputation.
    • Embrace diversity and avoid discriminatory, offensive, gossiping, or harassing behaviour.
    • Treat colleagues and stakeholders courteously, even when opinions differ.
    • Be transparent in decision-making and share relevant information unless restricted by managent.
    • Protect the dignity, privacy, and confidentiality of others.

Management and leadership are expected to:

    • Foster a respectful work environment and address any issues of disrespect or harassment.
    • Act as role models for professional conduct and inclusivity.

  1. Integrity and Ethics:

Employees are expected to:

    • Act honestly and in good faith, prioritising the interests of Awesim.
    • Refrain from using company resources for personal gain or improper purposes.
    • Uphold the company’s values:
      • Take Care
      • Have Respect
      • Be a Team Player
      • Go the Extra Mile
      • Choose What Is Right

The organisation commits to:

    • Operating transparently and ethically in all business dealings.
    • Providing channels for employees to report improper or unethical conduct safely and confidentially.

  1. Confidentiality and Privacy:

Employees must:

    • Keep sensitive company and client information confidential unless disclosure is legally required.
    • Not discuss or share remuneration or employment terms with fellow colleagues or unauthorised parties.

The organisation will:

    • Ensure secure handling and storage of employee and client data in compliance with POPIA.

USE OF COMPANY RESOURCES

Employees are responsible for:

  • Using company resources efficiently and minimising waste.
  • Safeguarding company property and reporting damage or misuse in line with Awesims' Asset & Equipment Policy.

Management and leadership are responsible for:

  • Ensuring employees have the tools and resources necessary to perform their duties effectively.
  • Monitoring resource use and implementing measures to reduce waste and inefficiency.

DRESS CODE

Employees are required to dress appropriately for their role, ensuring neatness and professionalism. Specific guidelines include:

  • Field-based staff dress code:
    • Sales field staff (including Regional Team Leaders) are required to wear their allocated Awesim uniform every working day (Awesim/Network branded golf shirts).
    • On cold days/seasons, Sales Field staff (including Regional Team Leaders) are permitted to wear a plain jersey or jacket, over their Awesim/Network golf shirt, that is presentable and appropriate; however, it must not be branded with other companies' logos (e.g Hugo Boss, Lacoste, etc.). It is preferable, where possible, for staff to wear their Awesim/Telkom branded jackets.
    • Shorts, 3/4 pants, and tracksuit pants are not permitted to be worn in the field or during client or network engagement. Staff must wear long pants (e.g. chinos, jeans, cargo pants) every working day and when in the field or during client/network engagement events.
    • Open or inappropriate shoes (e.g. slops, crocs, slippers, etc.) are not permitted to be worn in the field or during client or network engagement. Closed shoes (e.g. takkies, etc.) must be worn every working day and when in the field or during client or network engagement events.
    • Protective or specialised clothing must be worn when required for safety or when executing tasks that require personal protective equipment (PPE).
  • Head office based staff dress code:
    • Dress code for head office-based staff is casual (e.g. jeans, top, dress, etc.). All outfits or clothing must be appropriate for work.
    • Fridays are usually casual days, where employees are encouraged to wear their Awesim T-shirts or a funky colourful shirt/outfit.
    • A smart casual dress code is required for client or service provider meetings/engagements.
    • Protective or specialised clothing must be worn when required for safety or when executing tasks that require personal protective equipment (PPE).

Management will:

  • Communicate and enforce dress code expectations fairly and consistently.

PROMOTING CORPORATE CITIZENSHIP

Employees are encouraged to:

  • Consider the environmental, social, and economic impact of their actions.
  • Support community engagement initiatives and contribute to Awesims' sustainability goals.

The organisation will:

  • Act as a responsible corporate citizen by prioritising sustainability and community well-being.

COMPLIANCE

All employees, management, and leadership are expected to comply with this Code of Conduct. Non-compliance will be addressed in accordance with the company’s disciplinary code and applicable legislation. Misconduct, unethical behaviour, or breaches of confidentiality may lead to disciplinary action, including termination where warranted.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Code of conduct", "id": "b6958556-dd90-4f1e-9b46-2c6b8e0dacc3", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "HR policy", "id": "6dee3fb5-0aa6-432f-b8af-753794b08509", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-12-08T10:03:00.885+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/6b71d27c-337e-4230-8f3a-31e1e37549ae" }, { "id": "c95f3cc8-ce37-4b10-9aa5-fcb3a90ca9e8", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"c95f3cc8-ce37-4b10-9aa5-fcb3a90ca9e8\",\"title\":\"How to Order SIMs (Free, Buy and eSIMS) on the Awesim App\",\"content\":\"\nAgents can order\n\\\"Free\\\",\n\\\"Buy\\\" and \\\"eSIMS\\\"\nSims from the Awesim App.\nAgents need a\nminimum of 30 sims\n(Free and Buy) to place an order.\n\\\"Login\nto the App\nOn the Dashboard, select \\\"\nOrder\\\"\nThe next page will show how many available\n\\\"Free\\\"\nand\n\\\"Buy\\\"\nsims you can order.\nIf the\ncombined total is over 30 sims\n, you\nqualify\nto order.\nNote:\nBuy SIMs not only limited to Free SIMs.\nIf\nno Free SIMs\nand agent\nqualifies for 30+ Buy SIMs\n- agents\ncan process an order.\nNot only limited to FREE + BUY\nChange the \\\"\nQuantities\\\"\nof \\\"free SIMs\\\" you want to order and then select\n\\\"Add SIMs\\\"\nChange the\n\\\"Quantities\\\"\nof\n\\\"buy SIMs\\\"\nyou want to order.\nIf you don't want to order, make sure to change the quantities to \\\"0\\\".\nChange the quantities of\n\\\"eSIMs\\\"\nyou want to order.\nIf you\ndon't want\nto order, make sure to change the quantities to\n\\\"0\\\"\n.\nReview your order and if all the information is correct, select \\\"Order\\\"\nConfirm your\ndelivery address\nby selecting confirm.\nOnce confirmed, you will see the order number and a notification that the order is placed.\nADDITIONAL NOTES:\nNot all phones support eSIMs. Please check the device’s compatibility before purchasing an eSIM.\nBuy SIMs not only limited to Free SIMs.\nIf\nno Free SIMs\nand agent\nqualifies for 30+ Buy SIMs\n- agents\ncan process an order.\nNot only limited to FREE + BUY\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"c95f3cc8-ce37-4b10-9aa5-fcb3a90ca9e8\",\"title\":\"How to Order SIMs (Free, Buy and eSIMS) on the Awesim App\",\"content\":\"

Agents can order \\\"Free\\\", \\\"Buy\\\" and \\\"eSIMS\\\" Sims from the Awesim App.
Agents need a minimum of 30 sims (Free and Buy) to place an order.

  1. \\\"Login to the App

  1. On the Dashboard, select \\\"Order\\\"

  1. The next page will show how many available \\\"Free\\\" and \\\"Buy\\\" sims you can order.
    If the combined total is over 30 sims, you qualify to order.

Note:

Buy SIMs not only limited to Free SIMs.
If no Free SIMs and agent qualifies for 30+ Buy SIMs - agents can process an order.
Not only limited to FREE + BUY

  1. Change the \\\"Quantities\\\" of \\\"free SIMs\\\" you want to order and then select \\\"Add SIMs\\\"

  1. Change the \\\"Quantities\\\" of \\\"buy SIMs\\\" you want to order.
    If you don't want to order, make sure to change the quantities to \\\"0\\\".

  1. Change the quantities of \\\"eSIMs\\\" you want to order.
    If you don't want to order, make sure to change the quantities to \\\"0\\\".

  1. Review your order and if all the information is correct, select \\\"Order\\\"

  1. Confirm your delivery address by selecting confirm.

  1. Once confirmed, you will see the order number and a notification that the order is placed.

ADDITIONAL NOTES:

  • Not all phones support eSIMs. Please check the device’s compatibility before purchasing an eSIM.
  • Buy SIMs not only limited to Free SIMs.
    If     no Free SIMs and agent qualifies for 30+ Buy SIMs - agents can process an order.
    Not only limited to FREE + BUY

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "awsim app", "id": "43ed5c94-818f-48e4-9ca8-213b37a6e30d", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "esim", "id": "fc68cb0e-77ab-4511-a067-48367111f9fd", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "How-To", "id": "4e797f33-da93-451c-a2fa-1c935d7545a0", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-12-04T07:44:39.398+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/c95f3cc8-ce37-4b10-9aa5-fcb3a90ca9e8" }, { "id": "9a1786bb-93c2-4cba-bdcd-9557df9085f5", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"9a1786bb-93c2-4cba-bdcd-9557df9085f5\",\"title\":\"Awesim Welcome Email Template\",\"content\":\"\nDear ___________________,\nOn behalf of the entire team at Awesim, I am thrilled to extend a warm and hearty welcome to you!\nWe are delighted to have you join our Team, and we can't wait to embark on this exciting journey together.\nWe look forward to you starting with us on _____________.\nPREPARATION FOR YOUR START:\nPayroll Info/documents:\nPlease complete our\nNEW EMPLOYEE INFO FORM\n(Click on this link to take you to the form).\nPlease ensure you have the following documents available as you will need to attach them in the form:\nCopy of ID/Passport\nCopy of Work permit (If foreign national) (Remove if we already see they have a valid SA ID on CV, etc)\nCopy of valid driver’s license (must be submitted for Sales Rep) - Remove if non Sales Rep Role\nProof of bank details (Letter from the bank)\nProof of address/residence (Municipal invoice/rental agreements, etc.)\nProof of SARS Tax Reference number (Letter from SARS)\nProvident & Funeral Fund documents:\nAttached are your fund documentation - please complete each of the forms in full.\nTake note of the below in the Discovery Funeral Fund Form:\nBeneficiary nomination (Funeral Cover Benefit) section -\nOnly one person can be nominated to receive the funeral benefit. Please also send through the ID copy of your nominated beneficiary (person older than 18).\nPlease ensure all the above documents and forms are completed and returned to me by latest_________________.\nON-BOARDING/START WITH AWESIM:\nHere's a glimpse of what to expect.\nWeek 1 – In field time (Date):\nFor your first week you will be in the field with XX.\nXX will be in contact with you in order to give insight and arrange for your time in the field.\nPlease ensure you dress presentable whilst in the field as you are representing the company image. Please note that slops, crocks, slippers, etc. are not permitted to be warn in the field.\nJHB on-boarding training (Date):\nWe have scheduled your formal on-boarding training for this week.\nOn-boarding will be based at our office in JHB.\nDetails of the training schedule/agenda will be shared with you once finalised.\nTyra will be in contact with you in regard to your travel and accommodation arrangements. Breakfast and dinner will be included in the accommodation booking, alternatively, Tyra will advise in this regard.\nPlease bring your original ID with you as you will need this in order to be loaded as a RICA agent.\nAfter your formal on-boarding training, your Line Manager will arrange dates and times to do on the job training with you.\nXX (RTL/ Line Manager) will liaise with you in terms of what will be expected thereafter\n.\nGENERAL:\nPlease ensure you arrive at the office before 8am each day.\nOn arrival on your first day at HO, please ask the reception ladies to call myself so I can come through and show you around the office, as well as introduce you to the rest of the team.\nPlease note that you will need to bring lunch for yourself each day as you are locally based. We have a kitchen with a microwave that you can use to heat up/prep your food.\nPlease note that a lunch allowance will be arranged for you for your on-boarding week at HO. Cash will be given on day #1 of on-boarding and thereafter an EFT will be made for the remainder of the week. Hyde Park shopping centre is in walkable distance from HO whereby you can purchase lunch each day. You will be responsible for arranging your own lunch for in the field and each day after your on-boarding training at HO.\nPlease also feel free to bring your own allocated mug that you can use for your tea/coffee at the office.\nDress code for HO will be casual (T-shirt, jeans, top, takkies etc.) but presentable is acceptable. Advisable to bring warm clothes as well just in case the weather changes.\nPlease do not hesitate to contact myself or __LM____ if you have any queries.\nWe Look forward to having you apart of the Team. 😊\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"9a1786bb-93c2-4cba-bdcd-9557df9085f5\",\"title\":\"Awesim Welcome Email Template\",\"content\":\"

Dear ___________________,

On behalf of the entire team at Awesim, I am thrilled to extend a warm and hearty welcome to you!

We are delighted to have you join our Team, and we can't wait to embark on this exciting journey together.

We look forward to you starting with us on _____________.

PREPARATION FOR YOUR START:

Payroll Info/documents:

Please complete our NEW EMPLOYEE INFO FORM (Click on this link to take you to the form).

Please ensure you have the following documents available as you will need to attach them in the form:

  • Copy of ID/Passport
  • Copy of Work permit (If foreign national) (Remove if we already see they have a valid SA ID on CV, etc)
  • Copy of valid driver’s license (must be submitted for Sales Rep) - Remove if non Sales Rep Role
  • Proof of bank details (Letter from the bank)
  • Proof of address/residence (Municipal invoice/rental agreements, etc.)
  • Proof of SARS Tax Reference number (Letter from SARS)

Provident & Funeral Fund documents:

Attached are your fund documentation - please complete each of the forms in full.

Take note of the below in the Discovery Funeral Fund Form:

  • Beneficiary nomination (Funeral Cover Benefit) section - Only one person can be nominated to receive the funeral benefit. Please also send through the ID copy of your nominated beneficiary (person older than 18).

Please ensure all the above documents and forms are completed and returned to me by latest_________________.

ON-BOARDING/START WITH AWESIM:

Here's a glimpse of what to expect.

Week 1 – In field time (Date):

  • For your first week you will be in the field with XX.
  • XX will be in contact with you in order to give insight and arrange for your time in the field.
  • Please ensure you dress presentable whilst in the field as you are representing the company image. Please note that slops, crocks, slippers, etc. are not permitted to be warn in the field.

JHB on-boarding training (Date):

  • We have scheduled your formal on-boarding training for this week.
  • On-boarding will be based at our office in JHB.
  • Details of the training schedule/agenda will be shared with you once finalised.
  • Tyra will be in contact with you in regard to your travel and accommodation arrangements. Breakfast and dinner will be included in the accommodation booking, alternatively, Tyra will advise in this regard.
  • Please bring your original ID with you as you will need this in order to be loaded as a RICA agent.
  • After your formal on-boarding training, your Line Manager will arrange dates and times to do on the job training with you.
  • XX (RTL/ Line Manager) will liaise with you in terms of what will be expected thereafter.

GENERAL:

  • Please ensure you arrive at the office before 8am each day.
  • On arrival on your first day at HO, please ask the reception ladies to call myself so I can come through and show you around the office, as well as introduce you to the rest of the team.
  • Please note that you will need to bring lunch for yourself each day as you are locally based. We have a kitchen with a microwave that you can use to heat up/prep your food.
  • Please note that a lunch allowance will be arranged for you for your on-boarding week at HO. Cash will be given on day #1 of on-boarding and thereafter an EFT will be made for the remainder of the week. Hyde Park shopping centre is in walkable distance from HO whereby you can purchase lunch each day. You will be responsible for arranging your own lunch for in the field and each day after your on-boarding training at HO.
  • Please also feel free to bring your own allocated mug that you can use for your tea/coffee at the office.
  • Dress code for HO will be casual (T-shirt, jeans, top, takkies etc.) but presentable is acceptable. Advisable to bring warm clothes as well just in case the weather changes.

Please do not hesitate to contact myself or __LM____ if you have any queries.

We Look forward to having you apart of the Team. 😊

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "welcome email template", "id": "596d3197-7aec-45f4-9e9d-fc6726f34fa5", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-12-02T11:51:58.266+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/9a1786bb-93c2-4cba-bdcd-9557df9085f5" }, { "id": "873d0a50-e6cb-45b4-b1ce-5daabb4df2a7", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"873d0a50-e6cb-45b4-b1ce-5daabb4df2a7\",\"title\":\"Medical Insurance (Momentum - Health4Me)\",\"content\":\"\nINTRODUCTION\nIn today's fast-paced world, having access to healthcare is more crucial than ever. While medical aid is often more expensive and regulated by strict guidelines, medical insurance is designed to be a more affordable alternative, focusing primarily on day-to-day healthcare expenses and specific health events. Awesim has therefore partnered with Momentum to offer employees the opportunity to enroll in the Health4Me medical insurance plan. This programme allows employees to voluntarily sign up at their own expense while the company manages the administrative. The following details outline the benefits and structure of this medical insurance offering.\nMomentum Health 4Me medical insurance cover is available only to employees below a certain earning threshold. Employees interested in this cover should confirm their eligibility with the Chief Human Resource Officer before signing up.\nKEY FEATURES OF MOMENTUM - HEALTH4ME\n(A) DAY TO DAY BENEFITS:\nGold\n-\n2025_Gold_day-to-day_benefit_flyer.pdf\nSilver\n-\n2025_Silver_day-to-day_benefit_flyer.pdf\nBronze\n-\n2025_Bronze_day-to-day_benefit_flyer.pdf\n(B) ACCIDENT & EMERGENCY COVER\nAn\naccident\nshall mean a medical emergency that is an external, unexpected event that is not traceable, directly or indirectly, to a member’s state of mental or physical health prior to the event.\nAn\nemergency\nshall mean the sudden and, at the time, unexpected onset of a medical condition that requires immediate medical or surgical treatment, where failure to provide medical or surgical treatment would result in serious impairment to bodily functions or serious dysfunction of a body organ or part, or would place the member’s life in serious jeopardy, and for the purpose of this benefit includes, and is limited to accidents, heart attacks and strokes.\nThere are two types:\nStandard\n-\n2025_Standard_accident_and_emergency_cover_flyer.pdf\nBase\n-\n2025_Base_accident_and_emergency_cover_flyer.pdf\n(C) HOSPITAL CASH & MATERNITY LUMP SUM BENEFIT\nThis benefit provides financial assistance when you or one of the members on your plan are hospitalised.\nHospital Cash\n- you receive a set amount of money for each day you are in the hospital, provided that hospitalisation is longer than 48 hours.\nMaternity Lump Sum Benefit\n- payable to a member if hospitalisation results in childbirth (live birth), irrespective of number of days member has been hospitalised.\nAttached document outlines details on this benefit:\n2025_Standard_hospital_cash_and_maternity_lump_sum_benefit.pdf\nBENEFIT GROUPS OFFERED AT AWESIM:\nAt Awesim we have 5 Benefit Groups that employees can choose from:\n2026 PREMIUMS:\nNETWORK PROVIDER LIST:\n2026-Health4Me GP Network.xlsx\n2026-Health4Me Dental Network.xlsx\n2026-Health4Me Optometry Network.xlsx\nWAITING PERIODS\nThree months waiting period applied in respect of the following benefits:\nSpecialist visits\nChronic benefit\nHIV benefit\nBasic and emergency dentistry\nBasic optometry\nHospital cash benefit (all hospitalisation reasons, excluding childbirth)\nTwelve months condition specific waiting period applied in respect of childbirth as reason for hospitalisation\nSUPPORTING DOCUMENTS FOR ADDITIONS OF DEPENDANTS:\nCATEGORY\nDETAILS\nDOCUMENT REQUIRED\nSpouse\nA marriage in terms of the marriage act\nMarriage Certificate\nSpouse\nA civil partnership as described in the civil union act\nMarriage Certificate\nSpouse\nA customary marriage as described in the recognition of customary marriages act\nMarriage Certificate\nSpouse\nA union recognised as a marriage by any Asiatic religion of the member\nMarriage Certificate\nSpouse\nA permanent life partnership - provided that Momentum receives satisfactory proof that a permanent life partnership exists, as well as proof that the partners in the relationship have been living together for at least the last six months\nAffidavit\nChild/Child student\nMember's natural child\nBirth Certificate\nChild/Child student\nMember's legally adopted child\nProof of Adoption/Letter from Social Worker or court stating that the child is in the legal care of the member\nChild/Child student\nFinancially dependent child that is not the member's natural or legally adopted child\nAffidavit or Proof of Guardianship\nChild/Child student\nChild student (student/mentally disabled/physically disabled, aged 18–26 years)\nProof of Studies from the institution or Proof of Disability\n**It is the members responsibility to ensure that Momentum is updated once a child/child student has completed his/her studies/schooling to be moved over to an adult dependent on the plan.\nIn cases where proof of studies/schooling has not been provided as a status update to Momentum by the member, Momentum may automatically move the child dependent over to an adult dependent on the plan.\nCOVER AGES:\nMEMBER\nENTRY AGE\nTERMINATION AGE\nEmployee\n18\nNone (actively at work and temporary absence clauses apply)\nSpouse\n18\nNone\nChild\n0\n18\nChild student (student/mentally disabled/physically disabled)\n18\n26\nCOVER WILL CEASE:\nIn the event that the member is absent from Southern Africa for a period of twelve consecutive months;\nIn the event that the member no longer satisfies the eligibility criteria for cover under the policy;\nIn the event of cessation of service with the employer;\nIn the event of cessation of the payment of premiums;\nUpon the policy termination date; or\nFor all dependents (spouse/s and child/ren) upon the death of an employee.\nRULES REGARDING MEMBERSHIP CHANGES AND JOINING:\nThe deadline for all changes/upgrades, joining and additions of dependents is the 15th of the month in order for cover to be effective from the 01st of the following month, of which the premium will then be deducted from the employees salary on the next salary run.\nFor eg. Employee notifies HR that they would like to add their new born baby to their cover on the 10th of January, the cover will be effective from 01st February, and the premium will then be deducted from the employees end of February salary.\nThe below table outlines when changes/upgrades and additions of dependents can be processed.\nSTEPS TO JOIN (NEW EMPLOYEES & EXISTING EMPLOYEES)\n(1) New employees:\nComplete the\nHealth4Me\nworkflow found on the #general channel on slack.\nThis workflow is to be completed within three months of the employees start date with Awesim. For eg. if the employee\nstarts 01st March\n, the workflow would need to be\ncompleted by COB 05th May in order for cover to be active 01st June and to be deducted from the employees June salary.\n(2) Existing employees (joining during the december annual joining period):\nComplete the\nHealth4Me\nworkflow found on the #general channel on slack.\nThis workflow is to be\ncompleted by COB the 1st Friday of December in order for cover to be active from the 01st January and to be deducted from the employees January salary.\nYour request will be reveiwed by the HR Department.\nMEMBERSHIP CHANGE TYPE\nMEMBERSHIP CHANGE RULES\nOption Changes\n(New members/employees)\nNew members (employees) who are added to a group, either from policy inception date, or ad-hoc, will have a three month window period from inception date of cover, in which they will be allowed to exercise a benefit option change (provided that more than one benefit option has been selected by the employer)\nOption Changes\n(Existing members/employees)\nOption changes not exercised within the window period stated above will only be allowed under the following circumstances:\n**Year-end changes for existing members (increase/decrease to benefit group or additions of dependents):\nForm:\nHealth4Me Employee membership changes form fillable.pdf\n- The above form must be submitted to the HR Department by\nCOB the 1st Friday of December.\nAnnual salary increase - an option change will be allowed during a benefit year upon a member’s annual salary increase, provided that proof is supplied to Momentum (annual salary increase letter)\n**Option changes or additions of dependents for existing members due to salary increase:\n- The general increase for Awesim employees is April, hence existing members are to complete this form\nHealth4Me Employee membership changes form fillable.pdf\nand send it through to the HR Department by\nCOB 30th April.\nAddition of dependents\n(New members/employees)\nNew members (employees) who are added to a group, either from policy inception date, or ad-hoc, will have a three month window period from inception date of cover, in which they will be allowed to add dependents (provided that dependant cover has been selected by the employer)\nAddition of dependents\n(Existing members/employees)\nAddition of dependent changes not exercised within the window period stated above will only be allowed under the following circumstances:\nEffective 1st January of every year;\nA birth - a new child is born or officially adopted (within three months of the child’s birth date, or within three months of the child’s official adoption date);\nA marriage - a new spouse (within three months of the marriage date); or\nAnnual salary increase - an addition of dependent will be allowed during a benefit year upon a member’s annual salary increase, provided that proof is supplied to Momentum (annual salary increase letter)\nTermination of membership\nIf the membership of an employee or an employee’s dependent(s) is terminated during the course of a benefit year, the employee, or the employee’s dependent(s) in question, will only be allowed to be placed back on benefit effective 1st January of the upcoming year, should they wish to continue with cover (unless there was a break in service in terms of the membership of the employee)\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"873d0a50-e6cb-45b4-b1ce-5daabb4df2a7\",\"title\":\"Medical Insurance (Momentum - Health4Me)\",\"content\":\"

INTRODUCTION

In today's fast-paced world, having access to healthcare is more crucial than ever. While medical aid is often more expensive and regulated by strict guidelines, medical insurance is designed to be a more affordable alternative, focusing primarily on day-to-day healthcare expenses and specific health events. Awesim has therefore partnered with Momentum to offer employees the opportunity to enroll in the Health4Me medical insurance plan. This programme allows employees to voluntarily sign up at their own expense while the company manages the administrative. The following details outline the benefits and structure of this medical insurance offering.

Momentum Health 4Me medical insurance cover is available only to employees below a certain earning threshold. Employees interested in this cover should confirm their eligibility with the Chief Human Resource Officer before signing up.

KEY FEATURES OF MOMENTUM - HEALTH4ME

(A) DAY TO DAY BENEFITS:

  • Gold - 2025_Gold_day-to-day_benefit_flyer.pdf
  • Silver - 2025_Silver_day-to-day_benefit_flyer.pdf
  • Bronze - 2025_Bronze_day-to-day_benefit_flyer.pdf

(B) ACCIDENT & EMERGENCY COVER

An accident shall mean a medical emergency that is an external, unexpected event that is not traceable, directly or indirectly, to a member’s state of mental or physical health prior to the event.


An emergency shall mean the sudden and, at the time, unexpected onset of a medical condition that requires immediate medical or surgical treatment, where failure to provide medical or surgical treatment would result in serious impairment to bodily functions or serious dysfunction of a body organ or part, or would place the member’s life in serious jeopardy, and for the purpose of this benefit includes, and is limited to accidents, heart attacks and strokes.

There are two types:

  • Standard - 2025_Standard_accident_and_emergency_cover_flyer.pdf
  • Base - 2025_Base_accident_and_emergency_cover_flyer.pdf

(C) HOSPITAL CASH & MATERNITY LUMP SUM BENEFIT

This benefit provides financial assistance when you or one of the members on your plan are hospitalised.

Hospital Cash - you receive a set amount of money for each day you are in the hospital, provided that hospitalisation is longer than 48 hours.

Maternity Lump Sum Benefit - payable to a member if hospitalisation results in childbirth (live birth), irrespective of number of days member has been hospitalised.

Attached document outlines details on this benefit: 2025_Standard_hospital_cash_and_maternity_lump_sum_benefit.pdf

BENEFIT GROUPS OFFERED AT AWESIM:

At Awesim we have 5 Benefit Groups that employees can choose from:

2026 PREMIUMS:

NETWORK PROVIDER LIST:

  • 2026-Health4Me GP Network.xlsx
  • 2026-Health4Me Dental Network.xlsx
  • 2026-Health4Me Optometry Network.xlsx

WAITING PERIODS

Three months waiting period applied in respect of the following benefits:

  • Specialist visits
  • Chronic benefit
  • HIV benefit
  • Basic and emergency dentistry
  • Basic optometry
  • Hospital cash benefit (all hospitalisation reasons, excluding childbirth)
  • Twelve months condition specific waiting period applied in respect of childbirth as reason for hospitalisation

SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS FOR ADDITIONS OF DEPENDANTS:

CATEGORY

DETAILS

DOCUMENT REQUIRED

Spouse

A marriage in terms of the marriage act

Marriage Certificate

Spouse

A civil partnership as described in the civil union act

Marriage Certificate

Spouse

A customary marriage as described in the recognition of customary marriages act

Marriage Certificate

Spouse

A union recognised as a marriage by any Asiatic religion of the member

Marriage Certificate

Spouse

A permanent life partnership - provided that Momentum receives satisfactory proof that a permanent life partnership exists, as well as proof that the partners in the relationship have been living together for at least the last six months

Affidavit

Child/Child student

Member's natural child

Birth Certificate

Child/Child student

Member's legally adopted child

Proof of Adoption/Letter from Social Worker or court stating that the child is in the legal care of the member

Child/Child student

Financially dependent child that is not the member's natural or legally adopted child

Affidavit or Proof of Guardianship

Child/Child student

Child student (student/mentally disabled/physically disabled, aged 18–26 years)

Proof of Studies from the institution or Proof of Disability

**It is the members responsibility to ensure that Momentum is updated once a child/child student has completed his/her studies/schooling to be moved over to an adult dependent on the plan.


In cases where proof of studies/schooling has not been provided as a status update to Momentum by the member, Momentum may automatically move the child dependent over to an adult dependent on the plan.

COVER AGES:

MEMBER

ENTRY AGE

TERMINATION AGE

Employee

18

None (actively at work and temporary absence clauses apply)

Spouse

18

None

Child

0

18

Child student (student/mentally disabled/physically disabled)

18

26

COVER WILL CEASE:

  • In the event that the member is absent from Southern Africa for a period of twelve consecutive months;
  • In the event that the member no longer satisfies the eligibility criteria for cover under the policy;
  • In the event of cessation of service with the employer;
  • In the event of cessation of the payment of premiums;
  • Upon the policy termination date; or
  • For all dependents (spouse/s and child/ren) upon the death of an employee.

RULES REGARDING MEMBERSHIP CHANGES AND JOINING:

  • The deadline for all changes/upgrades, joining and additions of dependents is the 15th of the month in order for cover to be effective from the 01st of the following month, of which the premium will then be deducted from the employees salary on the next salary run.
  • For eg. Employee notifies HR that they would like to add their new born baby to their cover on the 10th of January, the cover will be effective from 01st February, and the premium will then be deducted from the employees end of February salary.
  • The below table outlines when changes/upgrades and additions of dependents can be processed.

STEPS TO JOIN (NEW EMPLOYEES & EXISTING EMPLOYEES)

(1) New employees:

  • Complete the Health4Me workflow found on the #general channel on slack.
  • This workflow is to be completed within three months of the employees start date with Awesim. For eg. if the employee starts 01st March, the workflow would need to be completed by COB 05th May in order for cover to be active 01st June and to be deducted from the employees June salary.

(2) Existing employees (joining during the december annual joining period):

  • Complete the Health4Me workflow found on the #general channel on slack.
  • This workflow is to be completed by COB the 1st Friday of December in order for cover to be active from the 01st January and to be deducted from the employees January salary.

Your request will be reveiwed by the HR Department.

MEMBERSHIP CHANGE TYPE

MEMBERSHIP CHANGE RULES

Option Changes

(New members/employees)

New members (employees) who are added to a group, either from policy inception date, or ad-hoc, will have a three month window period from inception date of cover, in which they will be allowed to exercise a benefit option change (provided that more than one benefit option has been selected by the employer)

Option Changes

(Existing members/employees)

Option changes not exercised within the window period stated above will only be allowed under the following circumstances:

  • **Year-end changes for existing members (increase/decrease to benefit group or additions of dependents):
  • Form: Health4Me Employee membership changes form fillable.pdf
    - The above form must be submitted to the HR Department by COB the 1st Friday of December.
  • Annual salary increase - an option change will be allowed during a benefit year upon a member’s annual salary increase, provided that proof is supplied to Momentum (annual salary increase letter)
    **Option changes or additions of dependents for existing members due to salary increase:
    - The general increase for Awesim employees is April, hence existing members are to complete this form Health4Me Employee membership changes form fillable.pdf and send it through to the HR Department by COB 30th April.

Addition of dependents

(New members/employees)

New members (employees) who are added to a group, either from policy inception date, or ad-hoc, will have a three month window period from inception date of cover, in which they will be allowed to add dependents (provided that dependant cover has been selected by the employer)

Addition of dependents

(Existing members/employees)

Addition of dependent changes not exercised within the window period stated above will only be allowed under the following circumstances:

  • Effective 1st January of every year;
  • A birth - a new child is born or officially adopted (within three months of the child’s birth date, or within three months of the child’s official adoption date);
  • A marriage - a new spouse (within three months of the marriage date); or
  • Annual salary increase - an addition of dependent will be allowed during a benefit year upon a member’s annual salary increase, provided that proof is supplied to Momentum (annual salary increase letter)

Termination of membership

If the membership of an employee or an employee’s dependent(s) is terminated during the course of a benefit year, the employee, or the employee’s dependent(s) in question, will only be allowed to be placed back on benefit effective 1st January of the upcoming year, should they wish to continue with cover (unless there was a break in service in terms of the membership of the employee)

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "health4me", "id": "2f9d1af5-9bde-4e43-b03e-e2d8c76ce7e0", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Medical Insurance", "id": "9777fd00-cb8b-4b68-9e25-e6079b7ca9f5", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "momentum", "id": "a1754388-29be-41b9-a280-224fdca2428e", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-11-28T08:43:37.794+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/873d0a50-e6cb-45b4-b1ce-5daabb4df2a7" }, { "id": "bf585315-c2b1-439b-a0f0-2af37d833000", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"bf585315-c2b1-439b-a0f0-2af37d833000\",\"title\":\"Download the Awesim App - available on Android, iOS or Huawei\",\"content\":\"\nThe Awesim App is available for download once you've signed up and activated your\nAwesim account\n.\nYou can download the app from the\nGoogle Play Store,\nHuawei\nAppGallery\nor the\niOS App Store.\nFor your convenience, direct links and QR codes are provided to help you easily access all available platforms.\nGoogle Play Store\nDownload link:\nhttps://tinyurl.com/Awesim-App-Google-Play\nScan QR Code:\niOS App Store for iPhone\nDownload link:\nhttps://tinyurl.com/Awesim-App-iPhone\nScan QR Code:\nHuawei AppGallery\nDownload link:\nhttps://tinyurl.com/Huawei-App-Store\nScan QR Code:\nApp Version Control:\nTo enjoy the best app experience, make sure your Awesim App is updated to the\nlatest version\navailable on your app store:\nGoogle Play Store:\nVersion\n200.07\niOS App Store:\nVersion\n200.07\nHuawei AppGallery:\nVersion\n200.07\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"bf585315-c2b1-439b-a0f0-2af37d833000\",\"title\":\"Download the Awesim App - available on Android, iOS or Huawei\",\"content\":\"
  • The Awesim App is available for download once you've signed up and activated your Awesim account.
  • You can download the app from the Google Play Store, Huawei AppGallery or the iOS App Store.

For your convenience, direct links and QR codes are provided to help you easily access all available platforms.

Google Play Store


Download link:
https://tinyurl.com/Awesim-App-Google-Play

Scan QR Code:

iOS App Store for iPhone


Download link:
https://tinyurl.com/Awesim-App-iPhone

Scan QR Code:

Huawei AppGallery


Download link:
https://tinyurl.com/Huawei-App-Store

Scan QR Code:

App Version Control:

To enjoy the best app experience, make sure your Awesim App is updated to the latest version available on your app store:

Google Play Store: Version 200.07

iOS App Store: Version 200.07

Huawei AppGallery: Version 200.07

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Awesim App", "id": "3792b77e-136f-4b77-936c-25c96cf9c697", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "Download Links", "id": "9e8730ac-a076-486f-9aa4-3063c3e05af9", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-11-25T13:41:08.250+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/bf585315-c2b1-439b-a0f0-2af37d833000" }, { "id": "3f8c2490-2063-42a1-a619-fcd73257f4f0", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"3f8c2490-2063-42a1-a619-fcd73257f4f0\",\"title\":\"Help Centre FAQs\",\"content\":\"\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"3f8c2490-2063-42a1-a619-fcd73257f4f0\",\"title\":\"Help Centre FAQs\",\"content\":\"

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-11-24T06:36:32.866+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/a3db98c3-1af0-4b4f-b662-c78aa3829223" }, { "id": "b94096bb-5652-46bb-8fff-0df0de73bd16", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"b94096bb-5652-46bb-8fff-0df0de73bd16\",\"title\":\"Help Centre FAQs\",\"content\":\"\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"b94096bb-5652-46bb-8fff-0df0de73bd16\",\"title\":\"Help Centre FAQs\",\"content\":\"
