If employee is unable to submit, then the workflow must be submitted by either the Line Manager, First Aider, SHE Representative or a witness to the event.\nStep 3\n:\nFirst Aid Applied\n:\nSubmit the First Aid Applied workflow if first aid/treatment was given to an employee or 3rd party person OR if an employee or 3rd party person was assessed for an injuries.\nWorkflow to be submitted as soon as possible, but within 3 hours of the event.\nWorkflow to be submitted by the appointed First Aider who applied first aid/assessed the employee or 3rd party person.\nThe workflow can be found on #heath-and-safety-comittee channel on Slack.\nThis workflow will detail the first aid that was applied and all first aid items/supplies used.\nIf an employee or 3rd party person refuses any first aid treatment or assistance, this workflow must still be completed.\nThe completed and signed First Aid Applied or waiver forms must be attached onto the slack workflow.\nStep 4\n:\nAccident investigation & reporting:\nThe employee must complete the\nAccident Investigation Form\nThis form must be completed within 5 days of the accident.\nThis form documents the details of the accident and injuries sustained during the event.\nThe SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.\n(2) PROPERTY / ASSET DAMAGE\nRefers to any damage or loss to Awesim assets or property.\nProcess to report any property or asset damage:\nStep 1\n:\nImmediate Action:\nThe employee should take immediate steps to secure the area, prevent further damage, and ensure the safety of themselves and others.\nThe employee is to report the accident to their Line Manager. If a vehicle is involved, the employee is to contact the Fleet Administrator (Tyra Daniels).\nVehicle Accidents:\nThe employee must complete Awesim's\nVehicle Accident Report_fillable_HSE_F_012_(22-07-2025).pdf\nat the scene of the accident (Found in company vehicle).\nPhoto's must be taken (see list below) for evidence and SAPS reporting purposes:\nThe accident scene;\nVehicles involved and the damages incurred from the accident;\nLicense plate numbers of all vehicles involved;\nVehicle license discs of each vehicle (VIN Number, etc.);\nDrivers license of all drivers involved;\nDrivers licenses or ID's of all witnesses;\nStreet Name (if possible).\nStep 2\n:\n911 - NewsFlash workflow\n:\nWorkflow to be submitted as soon as possible, but within 1 hour of the event.\nSlack workflow link above, which can be accessed by copying and pasting into any chat on slack.\nThe workflow can also be found on #general channel in Slack.\nMost prevalent information is sent on the workflow which notifies relevant Awesim staff in order for help/assistance to be given.\nWorkflow should be submitted by the employee. If employee is unable to submit, then the workflow must be submitted by either the Line Manager, First Aider, SHE Representative or a witness to the event.\nStep 3:\nPolice Case Report (Accident Report (AR Form):\nVehicle Accidents & hi-jacking:\nThe employee is required to report the vehicle accident at the nearest police station to the scene of the accident within 24 hours of the event.\nThe employee must obtain the police case number.\nAsset Theft:\nThe employee is required to report the theft accident at the nearest police station to the scene of where the theft took place within 24 hours of the event.\nThe employee must obtain the police case number.\nStep 4:\nAccident investigation & reporting:\n(a) Vehicle Accident:\nThe completed\nAwesim Vehicle Accident Report\nmust be submitted on the\nVehicle Accident Form\nThis form must be completed within 5 day of the accident.\nThis form documents the details of the vehicle accident and damages sustained during the event.\nThe SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.\nThe Fleet Administrator must submit the required documentation to Awesim’s insurance company within the required timeframe and guidelines.\nThe Fleet Administrator must obtain quotes to repair/replace the damaged vehicle, get the quote approved and organise the replacement/repair of the vehicle.\nThe Fleet Administrator must send communication to the employee, as well as the CHRO, detailing the costs the employee is liable for and to inform them of the required salary deductions that will be processed accordingly.\n(b) Other Asset Accidents:\nThe employee must complete the\nAccident Investigation Form\nThis form must be completed within 5 day of the accident.\nThis form documents the details of the accident and injuries sustained during the event.\nThe SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.\nFinance must submit the required documentation to Awesim’s insurance company within the required timeframe and guidelines.\nThe Procurement Officer must obtain quotes to repair/replace the damaged assets, get the quote approved and organise the replacement/repair of the assets.\nThe Procurement Officer must send communication to the employee, as well as the CHRO, detailing the costs the employee is liable for and to inform them of the required salary deductions that will be processed accordingly.\n(3) NEAR MISS\nAny unplanned incident that did not result in injury, illness, or damages, but had the potential to do so.\nProcess to report a Near Miss\nStep 1\n:\nAccident investigation & reporting:\nThe employee must complete the\nNear Miss Report\nThis form must be completed within 5 day of the Near Miss (incident).\nThis form documents the details of the Near Miss and possible damages or injuries that could have occurred.\nThe purpose of this report is to investigate and evaluate the possible risk and injury of the near miss event and determine whether any additional action or preventative measures need to be implemented or existing measures enhanced.\nThe SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.\nACCIDENT / INCIDENT FLOW DIAGRAM\nNOTE: MONTHLY HSE REPORT MUST LIST ALL INCIDENT, ACCIDENTS & NEAR MISSES\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"c5eb2365-b204-4fb0-82e1-2c913762a26e\",\"title\":\"Health & Safety Policy\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

Awesim is committed to providing a safe and healthy work environment for all employees, contractors, visitors, and any other individuals affected by our operations.

We recognise that the success of our organisation depends on the well-being and safety of our people. Therefore, we are dedicated to complying with the Occupational Health and Safety Act (Act No. 85 of 1993) and other relevant regulations and standards applicable in South Africa.

This policy outlines our commitment to health and safety and provides a framework for the implementation of effective health and safety practices across our organisation.

RESPONSIBILITIES

(1) Management:

    • Provide leadership, support, and resources for the effective implementation of the health and safety policy.
    • Appoint competent person/s to oversee health and safety matters.
    • Ensure employees comply with our health and safety policies, procedures, and instructions

(2) Employees:

    • Comply with health and safety rules, procedures, and instructions.
    • Report hazards, incidents, and near misses promptly to supervisor/line manager and health and safety representatives.
    • Take reasonable care of their health and safety and that of others.

HAZARD IDENTIFICATION & RISK ASSEMENT

  • The HIRA (Hazard Identification & Risk Assessment) document identifies potential hazards and evaluates associated risks across all work activities.
  • Risk Assessments identify hazards and detail preventative measures required either to eliminate the risk or the impact altogether or to reduce it to as low as possible and ensure control measures are in place to manage the risk/impact.
  • Control measures are established and reviewed to mitigate risks to an acceptable level.
  • Our risk assessment process involves a systematic evaluation of the workplace environment, equipment, processes, and activities to identify any risks that could lead to harm or injury.
  • We consider factors such as the likelihood of occurrence and potential severity of consequences.
  • When risks are identified, they are prioritised based on the level of risk and thereafter proactive steps taken to eliminate or minimize these risks.
  • We encourage all employees to actively participate in the risk assessment process by reporting any hazards or concerns they may identify.
  • Awesim’s HIRA document will be available on Slack and GetGuru.

REPORTING HAZARDS

Reporting hazards is essential to prevent, eliminate or minimse accidents and injuries by ensuring unsafe conditions are quickly identified and addressed. It also helps in maintaining a safe work environment and comply with health and safety regulations.

Steps to report a hazard:

    1. Open the Google Form: Hazard Report Form
    2. Complete the Form: Fill in all the required information.
    3. Attach the supporting documents: Attach a picture of the hazard in the form in the allocated section.
    4. Submit the form: Once completed, click submit, which will automatically send the completed form to HR for processing.

HEALTH & SAFETY COMMITTEE

  • The HSE Committee serves as a platform for collaboration between management and employees to identify, assess, and address potential health and safety risks within our workplace.
  • Committee meetings are an integral part of our proactive approach to ensure continuous improvement in health and safety standards.
  • These meetings provide a forum for open discussions, knowledge sharing, and the formulation of effective strategies to prevent accidents, injuries, and occupational hazards.
  • Posters indicating committee members role and contact details will be placed on notice boards so staff are aware of such information in the case of an emergency/incident.

HEALTH & SAFETY REPRESENTATIVES & THEIR RESPONSIBILITIES

  1. Health and Safety Representative: Responsible for conducting inspections, investigate incidents, attend health and safety committee meetings, and identify potential hazards, risks and dangers.
  2. Fire Warden/Fire Marshal: Responsible for fire safety, evacuation plans, conducting fire drills, and ensuring fire-fighting equipment is maintained.
  3. First Aider: Trained to provide first aid in case of accidents or medical emergencies and responsible for maintaining first aid kits and supplies.
  4. Fire Fighter: Preventing, combating, and extinguishing fires with the goal of protecting lives, and operating tools and equipment used for firefighting safely and effectively.
  5. Health and Safety Committee: A group of employees from various departments that have been trained in health and safety aspects and meet regularly to discuss safety concerns, review incidents, and suggest improvements.

FIRST AID BOX

(1) Location OF FIRST AID BOXES AT AWESIM HO:

    • Box 1: Upstairs – Outside Large Boardroom
    • Box 2: Downstairs – At Reception Area

(2) USAGE:

  • This first aid box serves as an essential resource to promptly address any minor injuries or medical incidents that may occur during the course of work.
  • Stocked with a range of medical supplies, including bandages, antiseptics, sterile dressings, and basic medical equipment.
  • In the case of an accident or emergency that may arise, only Awesim appointed first aiders are allowed to access the First Aid Box as they have been trained on the proper use of items contained in the first aid box.

FIRE EXTINGUISHERS AND RELATED EQUIPMENT

(1) USAGE:

  • Fire Extinguishers and other related equipment are serviced in accordance with legislative requirements and should only be accessed by appointed Fire Fighters, Fire Wardens and staff who have been trained in the proper use of such equipment. 

(2) Types of Fire Extinguishers at Awesim HO:

  • DCP Fire Extinguishers – Dry Chemical Powder – Generally used for electrical fires, combustible fires, and fires from flammable liquids.
  • CO2 Fire Extinguishers – Gas – Stored in extinguisher as a liquid and gives off gas when used – Generally used for flammable liquids and gases and energized electrical fires.

(3) LOCATION OF FIRE EXTINGUISHERS AT AWESIM HO:

(a) Downstairs:

  1. Reception:
  2. Next to female bathroom:
    • 1x hose reel
    • 2x 4,45kg DCP fire extinguishers
  3. Next to male bathroom:
    • 1x hose reel
    • 2x 4,45kg DCP fire extinguishers

  1. Call centre:
    • 1x 9kg fire extinguisher

  1. Stores:
  2. Shelves area:
    • 1x 9kg DCP fire extinguisher

  • Outside stores office:
    • 1x 9kg DCP fire extinguisher

  1. Invoicing:
    • 1x 9kg DCP fire extinguisher

  1. Server Room:
    • 1x 2kg CO2 fire extinguisher

(b) Upstairs:

Passage way:

  • Next to female bathroom
    • 1x hose reel
    • 2x 4,45kg DCP fire extinguishers
  • Next to male bathroom
    • 1x hose reel
    • 2x 4,45kg DCP fire extinguishers

  1. Canteen:
    • 2x 2kg CO2 fire extinguishers

  1. HR/Floating desks:
    • 2x 2kg CO2 fire extinguishers

(c) Basement:

  1. Stores cage:
    • 1x hose reel & 2x 9kg DCP fire extinguishers
  2. Basement Entrance:
    • 1x Fire Hydrant

(d) Outside:

  1. Security gate:
    • 2x Fire Hydrant

(4) Fire Alarms at Awesim HO:

  • 1 x Fire Alarm - Reception door
  • 1 x Fire Alarm - Outside Large Boardroom

PERSONAL PROTECTIVE EQUIPMET (PPE)

  • As stated within South African legislation, any employees working in an environment or performing a task/responsibility where they may be exposed to risk or potential risk are required to wear the stipulated personal protective equipment (PPE). Environment, tasks and/or responsibilities that require the use of PPE will be discussed with the relevant employee/s.
  • Should an environment, task or responsibility require the use of PPE and the employee does not make use of the required PPE, then disciplinary action will be implemented in line with Awesim disciplinary code of conduct.

ACCIDENTS & INCIDENTS

  • An accident is defined as any unplanned event that results in personal injury, property damage, or both.
  • An incident is any unplanned event that has the potential to cause harm, loss, or damage but did not result in injury or property damage.

All accidents and incidents, regardless of severity, must be reported to either the SHE Representative/Line Manager. Employees involved in an accident or incident are responsible for reporting the details accurately and in as much detail as possible. Refer to accident / incident flow diagram herein.

REPORTING

(1) PERSONAL ACCIDENT (PERSON INJURED)

Refers to an unexpected event that results in injury, death, or ill-health of an employee or 3rd party person.  

Process to report a personal accident/incident:

Step 1:

Immediate Action:

  • If an employee is injured, they should immediately seek first aid or medical attention from one of the appointed first aiders. For serious or life threatening injuries, call emergency services (Netcare Ambulance - 082 911).
  • SHE Representative (Liona Govender) & CHRO (Kristen Castle) to be notified immediately.
  • CHRO to provide the relevant Injury on Duty (IOD) forms if required. The employee will require the completed IOD forms prior to being taken to emergency facilities or to a Medical Practitioner for treatment.
  • CHRO to complete the COIDA slack workflow for reporting purposes.
  • If first aid treatment is applied, the appointed first aider is required to complete the First Aid Application Form_HSE_F_009_(18-06-2024).pdf. Should the employee or 3rd party person refuse treatment, then the appointed first aider must get them to sign the First Aid_Medical Treatment Waiver Form_HSE_F_005_(14-06-2024).pdf.

Step 2:

911 - NewsFlash workflow:

  • Workflow to be submitted as soon as possible, but within 1 hour of the event.
  • Slack workflow link above, which can be accessed by copying and pasting into any chat on slack.
  • The workflow can also be found on #general channel in Slack.
  • Most prevalent information is sent on the workflow which notifies relevant Awesim staff in order for help/assistance to be given.
  • Workflow should be submitted by the employee. If employee is unable to submit, then the workflow must be submitted by either the Line Manager, First Aider, SHE Representative or a witness to the event.

Step 3:

First Aid Applied:

  • Submit the First Aid Applied workflow if first aid/treatment was given to an employee or 3rd party person OR if an employee or 3rd party person was assessed for an injuries.
  • Workflow to be submitted as soon as possible, but within 3 hours of the event.
  • Workflow to be submitted by the appointed First Aider who applied first aid/assessed the employee or 3rd party person.
  • The workflow can be found on #heath-and-safety-comittee channel on Slack.
  • This workflow will detail the first aid that was applied and all first aid items/supplies used.
  • If an employee or 3rd party person refuses any first aid treatment or assistance, this workflow must still be completed.
  • The completed and signed First Aid Applied or waiver forms must be attached onto the slack workflow.

Step 4:

Accident investigation & reporting:

  • The employee must complete the Accident Investigation Form
  • This form must be completed within 5 days of the accident.
  • This form documents the details of the accident and injuries sustained during the event.
  • The SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.

(2) PROPERTY / ASSET DAMAGE

Refers to any damage or loss to Awesim assets or property.

Process to report any property or asset damage:

Step 1:

Immediate Action:

  • The employee should take immediate steps to secure the area, prevent further damage, and ensure the safety of themselves and others.
  • The employee is to report the accident to their Line Manager. If a vehicle is involved, the employee is to contact the Fleet Administrator (Tyra Daniels).
  • Vehicle Accidents:
    • The employee must complete Awesim's Vehicle Accident Report_fillable_HSE_F_012_(22-07-2025).pdf at the scene of the accident (Found in company vehicle).
    • Photo's must be taken (see list below) for evidence and SAPS reporting purposes:
      • The accident scene;
      • Vehicles involved and the damages incurred from the accident;
      • License plate numbers of all vehicles involved;
      • Vehicle license discs of each vehicle (VIN Number, etc.);
      • Drivers license of all drivers involved;
      • Drivers licenses or ID's of all witnesses;
      • Street Name (if possible).

Step 2:

911 - NewsFlash workflow:

  • Workflow to be submitted as soon as possible, but within 1 hour of the event.
  • Slack workflow link above, which can be accessed by copying and pasting into any chat on slack.
  • The workflow can also be found on #general channel in Slack.
  • Most prevalent information is sent on the workflow which notifies relevant Awesim staff in order for help/assistance to be given.
  • Workflow should be submitted by the employee. If employee is unable to submit, then the workflow must be submitted by either the Line Manager, First Aider, SHE Representative or a witness to the event.

Step 3:

Police Case Report (Accident Report (AR Form):

  • Vehicle Accidents & hi-jacking:
    • The employee is required to report the vehicle accident at the nearest police station to the scene of the accident within 24 hours of the event.
    • The employee must obtain the police case number.
  • Asset Theft:
    • The employee is required to report the theft accident at the nearest police station to the scene of where the theft took place within 24 hours of the event.
    • The employee must obtain the police case number.

Step 4:

Accident investigation & reporting:

(a) Vehicle Accident:

  • The completed Awesim Vehicle Accident Report must be submitted on the Vehicle Accident Form
  • This form must be completed within 5 day of the accident.
  • This form documents the details of the vehicle accident and damages sustained during the event.
  • The SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.
  • The Fleet Administrator must submit the required documentation to Awesim’s insurance company within the required timeframe and guidelines.
  • The Fleet Administrator must obtain quotes to repair/replace the damaged vehicle, get the quote approved and organise the replacement/repair of the vehicle.
  • The Fleet Administrator must send communication to the employee, as well as the CHRO, detailing the costs the employee is liable for and to inform them of the required salary deductions that will be processed accordingly.

(b) Other Asset Accidents:

  • The employee must complete the Accident Investigation Form
  • This form must be completed within 5 day of the accident.
  • This form documents the details of the accident and injuries sustained during the event.
  • The SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.
  • Finance must submit the required documentation to Awesim’s insurance company within the required timeframe and guidelines.
  • The Procurement Officer must obtain quotes to repair/replace the damaged assets, get the quote approved and organise the replacement/repair of the assets.
  • The Procurement Officer must send communication to the employee, as well as the CHRO, detailing the costs the employee is liable for and to inform them of the required salary deductions that will be processed accordingly.

(3) NEAR MISS

Any unplanned incident that did not result in injury, illness, or damages, but had the potential to do so.

Process to report a Near Miss

Step 1:

Accident investigation & reporting:

  • The employee must complete the Near Miss Report
  • This form must be completed within 5 day of the Near Miss (incident).
  • This form documents the details of the Near Miss and possible damages or injuries that could have occurred.
  • The purpose of this report is to investigate and evaluate the possible risk and injury of the near miss event and determine whether any additional action or preventative measures need to be implemented or existing measures enhanced.
  • The SHE Representative will assist the employee/s with the completion of the form where required.

ACCIDENT / INCIDENT FLOW DIAGRAM

NOTE: MONTHLY HSE REPORT MUST LIST ALL INCIDENT, ACCIDENTS & NEAR MISSES

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Health", "id": "4b29693f-2f7c-4e79-8889-bcd64ae6e2fb", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Heath & Safety", "id": "bcbea658-7f39-44ae-b543-91a7880b25ab", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "HR policy", "id": "6dee3fb5-0aa6-432f-b8af-753794b08509", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "Safety", "id": "9a380ace-2b32-491d-ab8b-716ca7ad1da1", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-10-03T06:09:41.819+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/c5eb2365-b204-4fb0-82e1-2c913762a26e" }, { "id": "8112b1af-6f46-41f7-86fb-7d9508f54458", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"8112b1af-6f46-41f7-86fb-7d9508f54458\",\"title\":\"Customer Care Campaign(s) - Import SOP \",\"content\":\"\nScope/Objective\nText\nImport Template\nPrepare your data and insert into the Import Template.\nYYYY_MM_DD_Campaign Name_SR Type.xlsx\nFile Name Format:\nYYYY_MM_DD_Campaign Name_SERVICEREQUEST_TYPE(AI/NORMAL)\nExample:\n2025_09_09_Retention Reloaded – Agent Qualifies for SIMs – Order Encouragement_N\nImport Process\nThis PC\nSysproImports (\\\\\\\\yoda)\nReceived\nCustom Call List\nCC Campaign Tracking\nUpdate the Google Spreadsheet, for ease of Campaign Tracking.\nLink\nRequest access from Snr. CC Supervisor\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"8112b1af-6f46-41f7-86fb-7d9508f54458\",\"title\":\"Customer Care Campaign(s) - Import SOP \",\"content\":\"

Scope/Objective

Text

Import Template

Prepare your data and insert into the Import Template.

YYYY_MM_DD_Campaign Name_SR Type.xlsx

File Name Format:
YYYY_MM_DD_Campaign Name_SERVICEREQUEST_TYPE(AI/NORMAL)

Example:
2025_09_09_Retention Reloaded – Agent Qualifies for SIMs – Order Encouragement_N

Import Process

  • This PC
  • SysproImports (\\\\\\\\yoda)
  • Received
  • Custom Call List


CC Campaign Tracking


Update the Google Spreadsheet, for ease of Campaign Tracking.
Link
Request access from Snr. CC Supervisor



\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-09-30T06:40:59.754+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/8112b1af-6f46-41f7-86fb-7d9508f54458" }, { "id": "732c03ca-71e1-4daa-8d4f-f7b516cc9e45", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"732c03ca-71e1-4daa-8d4f-f7b516cc9e45\",\"title\":\"What is the process and policies to return Reward items \",\"content\":\"\nReward items - Return policies:\nNo exchanges, swaps, refunds, or returns of items will be processed once the order has been confirmed and placed by the agent, unless one of the below situations applies.\nOut of box failure/faulty items on receipt:\nIf a product is received and is faulty upon first use, the agent must:\nNotify Awesim within 14 calendar days of the invoice date with full details of the issue.\nEnsure the item is unused (other than the original attempt to operate it), intact, and returned with all original packaging and accessories, showing no marks, damages, or signs of wear.\nProvide photos, videos, or other evidence if requested by Awesim.\nIf a product is received and is faulty upon first use, the agent must:\nNotify Awesim within 14 calendar days of the invoice date with full details of the issue.\nEnsure the item is unused (other than the original attempt to operate it), intact, and returned with all original packaging and accessories, showing no marks, damages, or signs of wear.\nProvide photos, videos, or other evidence if requested by Awesim.\nPlease note:\nProcessing may take 2–3 weeks\nfrom the date Awesim receives the item, as external suppliers are involved.\nIncorrect Order:\nIf an agent has incorrectly chosen a reward item and wishes to rectify the mistake:\nThe item must be returned to Awesim’s head office within 14 calendar days of the invoice date, at the agent’s cost.\nThe item must be unused, unopened, intact, and in all original packaging, with no signs of damage or wear.\nReturn process:\nCollection of Faulty Item from Agent\nThe sales representative receives the faulty item directly from the agent.\nThe sales representative is responsible for returning the faulty item to Head Office.\nIf the sales representative is unable to return it personally, their Regional Team Leader (RTL) must ensure the faulty item is delivered to Head Office.\nOnce delivered, the item is handed over to the designated Returns Department staff member for processing.\nReceipt of Returned Item at Head Office\nThe item is assigned to the designated staff member for inspection.\nInspection of Returned Item\nInspect the item against the return conditions (e.g., unused, in original packaging, not damaged, correct product).\nDocument the inspection outcome (pass/fail) with notes on any defects or issues in CRM.\nNon-Compliance Handling\nIf the item does not meet return conditions:\nNotify the agent in writing (email/SMS) within 3 business days.\nInform the agent that they must arrange collection of the item at their own cost.\nRecord communication with the agent in the return log.\nClose the case once the item is collected.\nNo points will be refunded.\nCompliance Handling\nIf the item meets return conditions:\nApprove the return.\nNotify the agent that the return has been accepted.\nSubmit a request to the accounts/rewards team to process the points refund.\nRefund Processing\nThe accounts/rewards team processes the refund to the agent’s account.\nTimelines\nRefunds may take up to 2 weeks from the date Awesim receives the returned item at Head Office.\nAwesim reserves the right to amend or update this policy at any time. All decisions regarding returns are final.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"732c03ca-71e1-4daa-8d4f-f7b516cc9e45\",\"title\":\"What is the process and policies to return Reward items \",\"content\":\"

Reward items - Return policies:

No exchanges, swaps, refunds, or returns of items will be processed once the order has been confirmed and placed by the agent, unless one of the below situations applies.

Out of box failure/faulty items on receipt:

  • If a product is received and is faulty upon first use, the agent must:
  • Notify Awesim within 14 calendar days of the invoice date with full details of the issue.
  • Ensure the item is unused (other than the original attempt to operate it), intact, and returned with all original packaging and accessories, showing no marks, damages, or signs of wear.
  • Provide photos, videos, or other evidence if requested by Awesim.
  • If a product is received and is faulty upon first use, the agent must:
  • Notify Awesim within 14 calendar days of the invoice date with full details of the issue.
  • Ensure the item is unused (other than the original attempt to operate it), intact, and returned with all original packaging and accessories, showing no marks, damages, or signs of wear.
  • Provide photos, videos, or other evidence if requested by Awesim.

Please note: Processing may take 2–3 weeks from the date Awesim receives the item, as external suppliers are involved.

Incorrect Order:

If an agent has incorrectly chosen a reward item and wishes to rectify the mistake:

  • The item must be returned to Awesim’s head office within 14 calendar days of the invoice date, at the agent’s cost.
  • The item must be unused, unopened, intact, and in all original packaging, with no signs of damage or wear.

Return process:

Collection of Faulty Item from Agent

  • The sales representative receives the faulty item directly from the agent.
  • The sales representative is responsible for returning the faulty item to Head Office.
  • If the sales representative is unable to return it personally, their Regional Team Leader (RTL) must ensure the faulty item is delivered to Head Office.
  • Once delivered, the item is handed over to the designated Returns Department staff member for processing.

Receipt of Returned Item at Head Office

  • The item is assigned to the designated staff member for inspection.
  • Inspection of Returned Item
  • Inspect the item against the return conditions (e.g., unused, in original packaging, not damaged, correct product).
  • Document the inspection outcome (pass/fail) with notes on any defects or issues in CRM.

Non-Compliance Handling
If the item does not meet return conditions:

  • Notify the agent in writing (email/SMS) within 3 business days.
  • Inform the agent that they must arrange collection of the item at their own cost.
  • Record communication with the agent in the return log.
  • Close the case once the item is collected.
  • No points will be refunded.

Compliance Handling
If the item meets return conditions:

  • Approve the return.
  • Notify the agent that the return has been accepted.
  • Submit a request to the accounts/rewards team to process the points refund.

Refund Processing

  • The accounts/rewards team processes the refund to the agent’s account.

Timelines

  • Refunds may take up to 2 weeks from the date Awesim receives the returned item at Head Office.

Awesim reserves the right to amend or update this policy at any time. All decisions regarding returns are final.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-09-25T10:54:46.773+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/732c03ca-71e1-4daa-8d4f-f7b516cc9e45" }, { "id": "197f41fe-dca9-4f55-acf9-66ef541bea3a", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"197f41fe-dca9-4f55-acf9-66ef541bea3a\",\"title\":\"Awesim Performance Review Policy\",\"content\":\"\nIntroduction\nThis policy outlines Awesim's performance review process, designed to monitor employee well-being, foster positive relationships between peers, colleagues, and managers, and address any concerns promptly to prevent long-term breakdowns or unhappiness. The review process incorporates weekly, monthly, and quarterly feedback mechanisms to ensure continuous growth and development.\nPurpose of Reviews\nThe purpose of all the review processes outlined in this policy is to monitor employee performance, well-being at work, as well as the relationships between peers, colleagues, and Managers. Any concerns identified through patterns (consecutive ratings or comments) in these reviews will be dis discussed to find resolution as soon as possible to prevent long-term breakdowns, challenges or unhappiness.\n_________________________________________________________________________________\nSLACK REVIEWS\nWEEKLY SLACK CHECK-IN REVIEWS:\nWeekly Slack check-in reviews are a crucial component of our continuous feedback culture.\nSubmission Guidelines:\nFrequency:\nSubmitted weekly.\nReminders:\nA reminder will be sent weekly on Slack every Friday.\nSubmission Date:\nThe date on the submission must always be the Friday's date, regardless of when it is submitted.\nEligibility (Employee):\nEmployees must submit a check-in if they worked three (3) or more days of the week.\nIneligibility (Employee):\nIf an employee worked two (2) or less days in the week, they are not required to submit a check-in.\nSick Leave on Friday:\nIf an employee is sick on a Friday, the weekly check-in must be submitted on the day they return to work.\nLeave on Friday:\nIf an employee will be on leave on a Friday but worked three (3) or more days during that week, they must submit their weekly check-in before commencing their leave.\nLine Manager Rating:\nEmployees will provide a weekly score for their Line Manager based on that week's interactions and relationship.\nEligibility (Employee Rating Manager):\nEmployees will score their manager if their manager worked three (3) or more days in the week.\nIneligibility (Employee Rating Manager):\nIf their manager worked two (2) or less days in the week, employees must mark their manager as \\\"N/A\\\" (Not Applicable).\nRATING SCALE:\nPersonal & Workplace Wellbeing (Employee Self-Rating)\n🥳\nExceptional, Everything is fantastic, Thriving;\n😄\nGreat, Better than just good;\n😊\nGood, Happy, Content;\n😞\nNot good, Unhappy, Sad, Disappointed;\n😠\nExtremely unhappy, Extremely challenged, Extremely stressed.\nManager Rating (Employee Rating Manager)\n🥳\nExceptional, Manager is going over & above their responsibilities (Reason/Comment)\n😄\nGreat, Manager is doing a little more than what's expected;\n😊\nGood, Happy, Content.\n😞\nNot good, Unhappy, Disappointed;\n😠\nExtremely unhappy, Extremely challenged, Extremely Frustrated, Extremely stressed, Breakdown in the relationship (Reason/Comment).\nMONTHLY SLACK TEAM REVIEWS:\nMonthly Slack team reviews facilitate peer feedback within teams.\nSubmission Guidelines:\nFrequency:\nTeams of two (2) or more employees will rate their team peers on the last working day of each month.\nSubmission Date:\nThe date on the submission must always be the last day of the month (e.g., February 28th, March 31st), regardless of the actual submission date.\nSelf-Rating:\nEmployees must rate themselves as \\\"N/A\\\".\nPeer/Colleague Eligibility:\nA peer or colleague in the team will be rated \\\"N/A\\\" if they\ndid not work\n90-100% of the month.\nPeer Rating Scale:\n🥳\nExceptional, Colleague is going over & above their responsibilities (Reason/Comment)\n😄\nGreat, Colleague is doing a little more than what's expected;\n😊\nGood, Happy, Content.\n😞\nNot good, Unhappy, Disappointed;\n😠\nExtremely unhappy, Extremely challenged, Extremely Frustrated, Extremely stressed, Breakdown in the relationship (Reason/Comment).\nComments and Anonymity:\nComments Encouraged:\nFor scores of\n🥳\nand\n😠\n, comments and reasoning are highly encouraged to understand the context and for the CHRO to provide assistance where required.\nRecognition:\nThe 🥳should be used to recognise out-of-the-ordinary performance where Line Managers or peers go above and beyond their responsibilities. Where employees give a pattern of consecutive ratings of 🥳 without a comment/reason, the CHRO automatically lowering the score to either 😄 or 😊.\nAnonymity:\nSubmissions are anonymous. Only the CHRO will have access to individual submissions for Line Manager scores and team peer ratings. Line Managers will see their average team score, and employees will see their average score from their team/peer ratings in their Google Looker Studio Reports.\nSLACK REVIEW PENALTIES\nPenalties will be issued for non-compliance with Slack review instructions.\nPenalty Rules:\nIssuance:\nPenalties will be issued where instructions have not been followed or where manual changes are required by the CHRO.\nPenalty Date:\nThe date of the penalty will reflect the date required on the submission, regardless of the date the penalty occurred.\nReporting:\nPenalties will reflect in each employee's Google Looker Studio report.\nHR Manage KPI Score Impact:\nLine Managers are to lower employees' HR Manage KPI score based on the relevant table below.\n2025 Week Breakdown by Quarter:\nQuarter\nMonths\nStart Date\nEnd Date\nNumber of Weeks\nQ1\nJan – Mar\nJan 1 (Wed)\nMar 31 (Mon)\n13 weeks\nQ2\nApr – Jun\nApr 1 (Tue)\nJun 30 (Mon)\n13 weeks\nQ3\nJul – Sep\nJul 1 (Tue)\nSep 30 (Tue)\n13 weeks\nQ4\nOct – Dec\nOct 1 (Wed)\nDec 31 (Wed)\n13 weeks\n13 Weeks per Quarter Calculations:\nAve # of weeks per quarter\nNumber of Penalties\nHR Manage Score\nFormula/Calculation\n13\n0 – 1\n4 (100%)\n(13 – 1) ÷ 13 x 100 = 92.3%\n13\n2 – 3\n3 (75%)\n(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 76.9–84.6%\n13\n4 – 6\n2 (50%)\n(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 53.8–69.2%\n13\n7 – 9\n1 (25%)\n(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 30.8–46.2%\n13\n10 – 13\n0 (0%)\n(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 0–23.1%\n12 Weeks per Quarter Calculations:\nAve # of weeks per quarter\nNumber of Penalties\nHR Manage Score\nFormula/Calculation\n12\n0 – 1\n4 (100%)\n(12 – 1) ÷ 12 x 100 = 91.7%\n12\n2 – 3\n3 (75%)\n(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 75–83.3%\n12\n4 – 5\n2 (50%)\n(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 58.3–66.7%\n12\n6 – 8\n1 (25%)\n(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 33.3–50%\n12\n9 – 12\n0 (0%)\n(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 0–25%\nSlack Setup - For HR INternal Use:\nMonthly Team Rating Scale Setup:\n:black_nib: [Employee Name] Rating - Required\nN/A\n:partying_face:\n:smiley:\n:slightly_smiling_face:\n:slightly_frowning_face:\n:angry:\n[Employee Name] Reason - Optional\n_________________________________________________________________________________\nQUARTERLY HR MANAGE REVIEWS\nThese reviews are conducted quarterly between the Line Manager and the Employee.\nReview Process:\nPerformance Rating:\nEmployees are rated/scored based on their performance per KPI/task in their job profile.\nManager's Initial Rating:\nThe Line Manager will rate first and submit their scores and comments to the employee for review.\nEmployee Review:\nThe employee will review the scoring and comments provided by the Line Manager.\nAgreement:\nIf the employee agrees with the scores, they will capture the same score as the manager in the system.\nDisagreement:\nIf the employee does not agree, they must capture the score they believe they should receive and provide a clear motivation and reason for the difference. They will then submit their feedback for the Line Manager to review.\nDiscussion and Agreement:\nAny differences in scores must be discussed between the Line Manager and the Employee to reach an agreed-upon score. The agreed score will then be captured by both the employee and the Line Manager into the system.\nSign-off:\nBoth parties will sign off on the review.\nDepartmental Manager Review (if applicable):\nWhere there is a departmental manager, they will review the final scores of both the Line Manager and Employee. Should the departmental manager's scores/feedback differ, all three parties (employee, line manager, and departmental manager) must discuss and decide on the final score. Once all three parties are in agreement and all scores match, all parties will sign off the review.\nDispute Resolution Process:\nNotification:\nWhere disagreements in scoring persist and parties cannot come to an agreement, they must inform the CHRO.\nCHRO Intervention:\nThe CHRO will hear representation from all parties involved and review any supporting documents.\nFinal Decision:\nThereafter, the CHRO will determine the final score.\nCapture and Sign-off:\nThe final score must be captured into HR Manage, and all parties will sign off.\nPenalties for Missed Deadlines:\nIssuance:\nPenalties will be issued for missed deadlines.\nResponsibility:\nThe penalty will be issued to the person where the delay resulted from.\nShared Responsibility:\nWhere there is no clear identification of who caused the delay, all people involved in that review will be issued a penalty.\n_________________________________________________________________________________\nQUARTERLY SURVEY MONKEY REVIEWS\nSurvey Monkey reviews are sent out quarterly to finalise the 360-degree review process.\nCompany Rating:\nRating Scale:\nEmployees will rate the company from 1 to 10 based on how likely they would recommend Awesim as a place to work (1 being \\\"Not likely at all\\\" and 10 being \\\"Extremely likely\\\").\nReasoning:\nA reason for the score must be given. For low scores, employees should indicate the reason why and what they feel should be changed to improve the employee's score.\nAnnual Company Awards Nominations:\nEmployees will nominate four (4) employees within Awesim for the Annual Company Awards:\nMost All-Round\nMost Reliable\nMost Improved\nMost Positive\nFor each award, employees will nominate one employee from Head Office staff and one employee from Field staff.\nExecutive team members are excluded from all nominations.\nRules Resulting in Penalties:\nCannot nominate yourself.\nCannot nominate one person for more than one award.\nCannot nominate peers from your same department.\nCannot nominate your Line Manager.\nLine Managers cannot nominate their subordinates.\nSales/Field staff - Cannot nominate anyone from the Sales Admin team.\nOptional Section: Raising Concerns about Obstructive Colleagues\nThere is an optional section where employees can raise concerns for up to three (3) employees within Awesim (not in their direct team) who are regularly obstructive and challenging to work with. Employees must provide details and reasons for raising these concerns. If there is no one to report, this section can be skipped.\nAnonymity:\nAnonymity:\nSubmissions are anonymous. Only the CHRO will have access to individual submissions.\nPenalties:\nNot submitting or submitting past the due date will result in a penalty.\nPenalties issued in relation to your annual award nominations will result in a reduction to your individual annual nominations per award category (e.g. if you receive 3 survey monkey review penalties, the number of your individual nominations per award will decrease by 3).\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"197f41fe-dca9-4f55-acf9-66ef541bea3a\",\"title\":\"Awesim Performance Review Policy\",\"content\":\"

Introduction

This policy outlines Awesim's performance review process, designed to monitor employee well-being, foster positive relationships between peers, colleagues, and managers, and address any concerns promptly to prevent long-term breakdowns or unhappiness. The review process incorporates weekly, monthly, and quarterly feedback mechanisms to ensure continuous growth and development.

Purpose of Reviews

The purpose of all the review processes outlined in this policy is to monitor employee performance, well-being at work, as well as the relationships between peers, colleagues, and Managers. Any concerns identified through patterns (consecutive ratings or comments) in these reviews will be dis discussed to find resolution as soon as possible to prevent long-term breakdowns, challenges or unhappiness.

_________________________________________________________________________________

SLACK REVIEWS

WEEKLY SLACK CHECK-IN REVIEWS:

Weekly Slack check-in reviews are a crucial component of our continuous feedback culture.

Submission Guidelines:

  • Frequency: Submitted weekly.
  • Reminders: A reminder will be sent weekly on Slack every Friday.
  • Submission Date: The date on the submission must always be the Friday's date, regardless of when it is submitted.
  • Eligibility (Employee): Employees must submit a check-in if they worked three (3) or more days of the week.
  • Ineligibility (Employee): If an employee worked two (2) or less days in the week, they are not required to submit a check-in.
  • Sick Leave on Friday: If an employee is sick on a Friday, the weekly check-in must be submitted on the day they return to work.
  • Leave on Friday: If an employee will be on leave on a Friday but worked three (3) or more days during that week, they must submit their weekly check-in before commencing their leave.

Line Manager Rating:

Employees will provide a weekly score for their Line Manager based on that week's interactions and relationship.

  • Eligibility (Employee Rating Manager): Employees will score their manager if their manager worked three (3) or more days in the week.
  • Ineligibility (Employee Rating Manager): If their manager worked two (2) or less days in the week, employees must mark their manager as \\\"N/A\\\" (Not Applicable).

RATING SCALE:

Personal & Workplace Wellbeing (Employee Self-Rating)

  • 🥳 Exceptional, Everything is fantastic, Thriving;
  • 😄 Great, Better than just good;
  • 😊 Good, Happy, Content;
  • 😞 Not good, Unhappy, Sad, Disappointed;
  • 😠 Extremely unhappy, Extremely challenged, Extremely stressed.

Manager Rating (Employee Rating Manager)

  • 🥳 Exceptional, Manager is going over & above their responsibilities (Reason/Comment)
  • 😄 Great, Manager is doing a little more than what's expected;
  • 😊 Good, Happy, Content.
  • 😞 Not good, Unhappy, Disappointed;
  • 😠 Extremely unhappy, Extremely challenged, Extremely Frustrated, Extremely stressed, Breakdown in the relationship (Reason/Comment).

MONTHLY SLACK TEAM REVIEWS:

Monthly Slack team reviews facilitate peer feedback within teams.

Submission Guidelines:

  • Frequency: Teams of two (2) or more employees will rate their team peers on the last working day of each month.
  • Submission Date: The date on the submission must always be the last day of the month (e.g., February 28th, March 31st), regardless of the actual submission date.
  • Self-Rating: Employees must rate themselves as \\\"N/A\\\".
  • Peer/Colleague Eligibility: A peer or colleague in the team will be rated \\\"N/A\\\" if they did not work 90-100% of the month.

Peer Rating Scale:

  • 🥳 Exceptional, Colleague is going over & above their responsibilities (Reason/Comment)
  • 😄 Great, Colleague is doing a little more than what's expected;
  • 😊 Good, Happy, Content.
  • 😞 Not good, Unhappy, Disappointed;
  • 😠 Extremely unhappy, Extremely challenged, Extremely Frustrated, Extremely stressed, Breakdown in the relationship (Reason/Comment).

Comments and Anonymity:

  • Comments Encouraged: For scores of 🥳 and 😠, comments and reasoning are highly encouraged to understand the context and for the CHRO to provide assistance where required.
  • Recognition: The 🥳should be used to recognise out-of-the-ordinary performance where Line Managers or peers go above and beyond their responsibilities. Where employees give a pattern of consecutive ratings of 🥳 without a comment/reason, the CHRO automatically lowering the score to either 😄 or 😊.
  • Anonymity: Submissions are anonymous. Only the CHRO will have access to individual submissions for Line Manager scores and team peer ratings. Line Managers will see their average team score, and employees will see their average score from their team/peer ratings in their Google Looker Studio Reports.

SLACK REVIEW PENALTIES

Penalties will be issued for non-compliance with Slack review instructions.

Penalty Rules:

  • Issuance: Penalties will be issued where instructions have not been followed or where manual changes are required by the CHRO.
  • Penalty Date: The date of the penalty will reflect the date required on the submission, regardless of the date the penalty occurred.
  • Reporting: Penalties will reflect in each employee's Google Looker Studio report.
  • HR Manage KPI Score Impact: Line Managers are to lower employees' HR Manage KPI score based on the relevant table below.

2025 Week Breakdown by Quarter:

Quarter

Months

Start Date

End Date

Number of Weeks

Q1

Jan – Mar

Jan 1 (Wed)

Mar 31 (Mon)

13 weeks

Q2

Apr – Jun

Apr 1 (Tue)

Jun 30 (Mon)

13 weeks

Q3

Jul – Sep

Jul 1 (Tue)

Sep 30 (Tue)

13 weeks

Q4

Oct – Dec

Oct 1 (Wed)

Dec 31 (Wed)

13 weeks

13 Weeks per Quarter Calculations:

Ave # of weeks per quarter

Number of Penalties

HR Manage Score

Formula/Calculation

13

0 – 1

4 (100%)

(13 – 1) ÷ 13 x 100 = 92.3%

13

2 – 3

3 (75%)

(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 76.9–84.6%

13

4 – 6

2 (50%)

(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 53.8–69.2%

13

7 – 9

1 (25%)

(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 30.8–46.2%

13

10 – 13

0 (0%)

(13 – #penalties) ÷ 13 x 100 = 0–23.1%

12 Weeks per Quarter Calculations:

Ave # of weeks per quarter

Number of Penalties

HR Manage Score

Formula/Calculation

12

0 – 1

4 (100%)

(12 – 1) ÷ 12 x 100 = 91.7%

12

2 – 3

3 (75%)

(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 75–83.3%

12

4 – 5

2 (50%)

(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 58.3–66.7%

12

6 – 8

1 (25%)

(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 33.3–50%

12

9 – 12

0 (0%)

(12 – #penalties) ÷ 12 x 100 = 0–25%

Slack Setup - For HR INternal Use:

Monthly Team Rating Scale Setup:

:black_nib: [Employee Name] Rating - Required

  • N/A
  • :partying_face:
  • :smiley:
  • :slightly_smiling_face:
  • :slightly_frowning_face:
  • :angry:

[Employee Name] Reason - Optional

_________________________________________________________________________________

QUARTERLY HR MANAGE REVIEWS

These reviews are conducted quarterly between the Line Manager and the Employee.

Review Process:

  • Performance Rating: Employees are rated/scored based on their performance per KPI/task in their job profile.
  • Manager's Initial Rating: The Line Manager will rate first and submit their scores and comments to the employee for review.
  • Employee Review: The employee will review the scoring and comments provided by the Line Manager.
    • Agreement: If the employee agrees with the scores, they will capture the same score as the manager in the system.
    • Disagreement: If the employee does not agree, they must capture the score they believe they should receive and provide a clear motivation and reason for the difference. They will then submit their feedback for the Line Manager to review.
  • Discussion and Agreement: Any differences in scores must be discussed between the Line Manager and the Employee to reach an agreed-upon score. The agreed score will then be captured by both the employee and the Line Manager into the system.
  • Sign-off: Both parties will sign off on the review.
  • Departmental Manager Review (if applicable): Where there is a departmental manager, they will review the final scores of both the Line Manager and Employee. Should the departmental manager's scores/feedback differ, all three parties (employee, line manager, and departmental manager) must discuss and decide on the final score. Once all three parties are in agreement and all scores match, all parties will sign off the review.

Dispute Resolution Process:

  • Notification: Where disagreements in scoring persist and parties cannot come to an agreement, they must inform the CHRO.
  • CHRO Intervention: The CHRO will hear representation from all parties involved and review any supporting documents.
  • Final Decision: Thereafter, the CHRO will determine the final score.
  • Capture and Sign-off: The final score must be captured into HR Manage, and all parties will sign off.

Penalties for Missed Deadlines:

  • Issuance: Penalties will be issued for missed deadlines.
  • Responsibility: The penalty will be issued to the person where the delay resulted from.
  • Shared Responsibility: Where there is no clear identification of who caused the delay, all people involved in that review will be issued a penalty.

_________________________________________________________________________________

QUARTERLY SURVEY MONKEY REVIEWS

Survey Monkey reviews are sent out quarterly to finalise the 360-degree review process.

Company Rating:

  • Rating Scale: Employees will rate the company from 1 to 10 based on how likely they would recommend Awesim as a place to work (1 being \\\"Not likely at all\\\" and 10 being \\\"Extremely likely\\\").
  • Reasoning: A reason for the score must be given. For low scores, employees should indicate the reason why and what they feel should be changed to improve the employee's score.

Annual Company Awards Nominations:

Employees will nominate four (4) employees within Awesim for the Annual Company Awards:

  • Most All-Round
  • Most Reliable
  • Most Improved
  • Most Positive

For each award, employees will nominate one employee from Head Office staff and one employee from Field staff.

Executive team members are excluded from all nominations.

Rules Resulting in Penalties:

  • Cannot nominate yourself.
  • Cannot nominate one person for more than one award.
  • Cannot nominate peers from your same department.
  • Cannot nominate your Line Manager.
  • Line Managers cannot nominate their subordinates.
  • Sales/Field staff - Cannot nominate anyone from the Sales Admin team.

Optional Section: Raising Concerns about Obstructive Colleagues

There is an optional section where employees can raise concerns for up to three (3) employees within Awesim (not in their direct team) who are regularly obstructive and challenging to work with. Employees must provide details and reasons for raising these concerns. If there is no one to report, this section can be skipped.

Anonymity:

  • Anonymity: Submissions are anonymous. Only the CHRO will have access to individual submissions.

Penalties:

  • Not submitting or submitting past the due date will result in a penalty.
  • Penalties issued in relation to your annual award nominations will result in a reduction to your individual annual nominations per award category (e.g. if you receive 3 survey monkey review penalties, the number of your individual nominations per award will decrease by 3).

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Performance", "id": "af0ac5f7-59b5-412c-b794-93a33ca70786", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Reviews", "id": "8483c120-a67a-45c5-9e87-573eed518ecc", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-09-25T08:26:58.374+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/197f41fe-dca9-4f55-acf9-66ef541bea3a" }, { "id": "6ce9221c-571e-4e50-89d4-e702814fdba3", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"6ce9221c-571e-4e50-89d4-e702814fdba3\",\"title\":\"Contact Us\",\"content\":\"\nOpen Hours:\nMon-Fri: 8AM - 5PM\nAddress:\nStreet:\n36 Morsim Rd\nSuburb:\nHyde Park\nCity:\nJohannesburg\nPostal Code:\n2196\nGoogle Maps Link:\nhttps://maps.app.goo.gl/MuVpP9JTPVgFRpza9\nEmail:\ninfo@awesim.co.za\nWhatsApp:\n060 070 2666\nWhatsApp channel:\nFollow our WhatsApp Channel for exclusive updates, special content, and more:\nhttps://tinyurl.com/awesim-WhatsApp-Channel\nTelephone:\n011 268 1348\nSMS:\nSMS \\\"Call\\\" to\n43203\nWebsite:\nhttps://awesim.co.za/\nFacebook:\nhttps://www.facebook.com/awesimtelecoms\nWe aim to acknowledge and respond to all queries within 24 business hours (excluding weekends and public holidays).\nIf your query requires further investigation, we will let you know the expected lead time within the same 24-hour period.\nBy query type:\nBilling queries\n– Initial response within 24 business hours. Complex account investigations may take longer; you’ll be notified of expected timelines.\nTechnical support\n– Initial response within 24 business hours. If escalation or troubleshooting is needed, we’ll keep you updated on progress and next steps.\nSales enquiries\n– Initial response within 24 business hours with product or service information.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"6ce9221c-571e-4e50-89d4-e702814fdba3\",\"title\":\"Contact Us\",\"content\":\"

Open Hours:

  • Mon-Fri: 8AM - 5PM

Address:

  • Street: 36 Morsim Rd
  • Suburb: Hyde Park
  • City: Johannesburg
  • Postal Code: 2196
  • Google Maps Link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/MuVpP9JTPVgFRpza9

Email:

  • info@awesim.co.za

WhatsApp:

  • 060 070 2666

WhatsApp channel:

Follow our WhatsApp Channel for exclusive updates, special content, and more:
https://tinyurl.com/awesim-WhatsApp-Channel

Telephone:

  • 011 268 1348

SMS:

  • SMS \\\"Call\\\" to 43203

Website:

  • https://awesim.co.za/

Facebook:

  • https://www.facebook.com/awesimtelecoms

We aim to acknowledge and respond to all queries within 24 business hours (excluding weekends and public holidays).
If your query requires further investigation, we will let you know the expected lead time within the same 24-hour period.

By query type:

  • Billing queries – Initial response within 24 business hours. Complex account investigations may take longer; you’ll be notified of expected timelines.
  • Technical support – Initial response within 24 business hours. If escalation or troubleshooting is needed, we’ll keep you updated on progress and next steps.
  • Sales enquiries – Initial response within 24 business hours with product or service information.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-09-23T08:03:22.570+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/6ce9221c-571e-4e50-89d4-e702814fdba3" }, { "id": "89c7a681-f0c1-4105-b352-c68fd08201bf", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"89c7a681-f0c1-4105-b352-c68fd08201bf\",\"title\":\"Asset & Equipment Policy\",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nThis policy establishes the guidelines for the use, maintenance, and return of company assets and equipment. All employees are required to adhere to these guidelines to ensure the proper care, security, and longevity of company property.\nUSAGE\nCompany assets and equipment provided by Awesim are for business purposes only.\nEmployees must use issued assets and equipment safely, following all manufacturer instructions.\nA checklist documenting the issuance and condition of the assets/equipment must be signed by both the employee and the issuer. Any faults or missing items will be noted.\nEmployees must store all assets and equipment securely, especially during transport or when off-site. If there are concerns about the safety or storage of these items, employees should consult the company for guidance in advance.\nAwesim reserves the right to request the return of any issued equipment at any time without prior notice.\nMAINTENANCE\nEmployees are responsible for keeping all assets and equipment clean and in good working condition.\nAny maintenance needs must be reported within 24 hours by emailing\naccounts@awesim.co.za\n, with the employee's Line Manager copied on the email.\nMaintenance costs are the employee’s responsibility, except for those due to normal wear and tear.\nLOSS, DAMAGE, OR THEFT\nIn case of loss, damage, or theft, the employee must report it immediately by emailing\naccounts@awesim.co.za\n, with the employee's Line Manager copied on the email. If the employee is unable to report it immediately, then they must do so within 24 hours of the incident.\nThe employee is responsible for obtaining and submitting any necessary documentation (e.g., SAPS Case number) to Awesim’s Finance department (\naccounts@awesim.co.za)\n.\nAwesim will provide guidance on arranging necessary repairs or replacements. Once approved, repairs or replacements must be completed within one week of approval.\nRETURN\nAssets and equipment must be returned in the same condition as the employee received them, when they are no longer needed, or upon request by Awesim, or upon termination of employment.\nIf notified to return an asset/equipment, the employee must do so within:\n48 hours if located less than 4 hours from the Head Office (HO); or\n72 hours if located 4 hours or more from the HO.\nFor each hour that the equipment is overdue, a rental fee of R50 per hour will be charged until the asset or equipment is returned. Awesim reserves the right to deduct these fees from the employee’s salary or any other monies owed to the employee.\nSALARY DEDUCTION\nBy accepting the assets/equipment and signing the handover checklist, the Employee gives full consent to Awesim to deduct any maintenance, repair, or replacement costs from the employee’s salary in line with this policy.\nIf the loss, damage or theft is reported within the required 24-hour period, 50% of the total cost (e.g. the repair, replacement or insurance excess fee) will be deducted from the employees salary.\nIf the loss, damage or theft is not reported within the required 24-hour period, 100% of the total cost (e.g. the repair, replacement or insurance excess fee) will be deducted from the employees salary.\nAwesim reserves the right to deduct the total cost (100%) of the repair, replacement or insurance excess fee from the employee’s salary as a result of negligence or misuse.\nAwesim may also deduct the total cost (100%) of repair or replacement costs from the employees salary for any damages caused to third-party property, assets, or equipment as a result of negligence or misuse.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"89c7a681-f0c1-4105-b352-c68fd08201bf\",\"title\":\"Asset & Equipment Policy\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

This policy establishes the guidelines for the use, maintenance, and return of company assets and equipment. All employees are required to adhere to these guidelines to ensure the proper care, security, and longevity of company property.

USAGE

  • Company assets and equipment provided by Awesim are for business purposes only.
  • Employees must use issued assets and equipment safely, following all manufacturer instructions.
  • A checklist documenting the issuance and condition of the assets/equipment must be signed by both the employee and the issuer. Any faults or missing items will be noted.
  • Employees must store all assets and equipment securely, especially during transport or when off-site. If there are concerns about the safety or storage of these items, employees should consult the company for guidance in advance.
  • Awesim reserves the right to request the return of any issued equipment at any time without prior notice.

MAINTENANCE

  • Employees are responsible for keeping all assets and equipment clean and in good working condition.
  • Any maintenance needs must be reported within 24 hours by emailing accounts@awesim.co.za, with the employee's Line Manager copied on the email.
  • Maintenance costs are the employee’s responsibility, except for those due to normal wear and tear.

LOSS, DAMAGE, OR THEFT

  • In case of loss, damage, or theft, the employee must report it immediately by emailing accounts@awesim.co.za, with the employee's Line Manager copied on the email. If the employee is unable to report it immediately, then they must do so within 24 hours of the incident.
  • The employee is responsible for obtaining and submitting any necessary documentation (e.g., SAPS Case number) to Awesim’s Finance department (accounts@awesim.co.za).
  • Awesim will provide guidance on arranging necessary repairs or replacements. Once approved, repairs or replacements must be completed within one week of approval.

RETURN

  • Assets and equipment must be returned in the same condition as the employee received them, when they are no longer needed, or upon request by Awesim, or upon termination of employment.
  • If notified to return an asset/equipment, the employee must do so within:
    • 48 hours if located less than 4 hours from the Head Office (HO); or
    • 72 hours if located 4 hours or more from the HO.
  • For each hour that the equipment is overdue, a rental fee of R50 per hour will be charged until the asset or equipment is returned. Awesim reserves the right to deduct these fees from the employee’s salary or any other monies owed to the employee.

SALARY DEDUCTION

  • By accepting the assets/equipment and signing the handover checklist, the Employee gives full consent to Awesim to deduct any maintenance, repair, or replacement costs from the employee’s salary in line with this policy.
  • If the loss, damage or theft is reported within the required 24-hour period, 50% of the total cost (e.g. the repair, replacement or insurance excess fee) will be deducted from the employees salary.
  • If the loss, damage or theft is not reported within the required 24-hour period, 100% of the total cost (e.g. the repair, replacement or insurance excess fee) will be deducted from the employees salary.
  • Awesim reserves the right to deduct the total cost (100%) of the repair, replacement or insurance excess fee from the employee’s salary as a result of negligence or misuse.
  • Awesim may also deduct the total cost (100%) of repair or replacement costs from the employees salary for any damages caused to third-party property, assets, or equipment as a result of negligence or misuse.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "asset", "id": "1c50633f-14a8-436c-ba26-fc85b09edc0a", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "equipment", "id": "6cccb0a6-3c15-4ee8-80c0-68ce57cf61f8", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "policy", "id": "4b4e383d-ef16-43ea-8781-9877bf0601e9", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-09-11T09:50:16.345+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/89c7a681-f0c1-4105-b352-c68fd08201bf" }, { "id": "3aaac33b-c37c-4d96-ba39-683bf6318926", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"3aaac33b-c37c-4d96-ba39-683bf6318926\",\"title\":\"TEST\",\"content\":\"\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"3aaac33b-c37c-4d96-ba39-683bf6318926\",\"title\":\"TEST\",\"content\":\"

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-09-11T09:41:21.548+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/3aaac33b-c37c-4d96-ba39-683bf6318926" }, { "id": "a2294eb3-3391-493e-b06a-a67f70c4cd78", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"a2294eb3-3391-493e-b06a-a67f70c4cd78\",\"title\":\"Slack Channel #Help_Centre SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the purpose and use of the\n#help_centre\nSlack channel, which serves as a central space for accessing support resources and asking questions related to agent-facing issues and common queries.\nScope\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) applies to the use of the\n#help_centre\nSlack channel, which serves as a central support space for staff.\nThe\n#help_centre\nchannel is a dedicated Slack space designed to:\n💬 Allow staff to\nask questions\nand receive\ntimely support\n📚 Provide\nquick access to verified\n⚠️\nPlease note:\nThis channel does\nnot\ninclude internal Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) or confidential operational processes. For such queries, please refer to your team’s internal documentation or consult your team lead directly.\nHow-To Guide: Non-Guru Users\nStep 1: Ask Your Question in the Slack Channel\nStep 2: Answer Loading\nThe nominated GetGuru User,\nCustomer Care Administrator\nwill tag @Guru to assist with your query.\nIgnore\nthe Guru Auto Response: “Answer not available”\nCheck the thread for the response once it's posted.\nThe reply includes source links. See “Citations”.\nYou can click the link for more details and share it with your agents.\nAlways check for the verified badge ✅\nStep 3: Please provide us with feedback\nPlease use the Slack Emoji (and not the Guru Emojis)\n👍 = Accurate and helpful\n👎 = Needs work\n(Please share feedback: Reply to Thread)\nStep 3: Feedback Example\nHow to Guide: Guru Users\nStep 1:\nPost your question directly in the Slack channel.\n(As a licensed user, you do not need to tag @guru—just type your question.)\nStep 2: Answer Loading\nCheck the thread for the Guru response once it's posted.\nThe reply includes source links. See “Citations”\nClick the link for more details and share it with your agents.\nAlways check for the verified badge ✅\nStep 3: Please provide us with feedback\nPlease click on the\nGuru\nEmoji\n👍 = Accurate and helpful\n👎 = Needs work\nClick on the ellipses \\\"three dots\\\"\nClick on the ellipses \\\"three dots\\\" = Ask an Expert\nPlease share feedback\nStep 3: Feedback Example\nStep 3: Ask an Expert Example\nTag Expert:\nContent & Training Admin\n@Naadirah Valli\nwho will assist to co-ordinate the verified answer with the Knowledge Expert\nAdd a note:\nFeedback\nProcess: Handling a Request\n1. Receive & Acknowledge : Non-Guru Users\nCopy and paste the request exactly as received into the Slack thread.\nTag\n@Guru\nin the thread to flag the request for knowledge base inclusion.\nEncourage the requester to\nreact\nor\ncomment\non the card for future feedback.\n✅ Mark as complete once responded.\n3. Receive & Acknowledge : Guru Users\nGuru Users to post their question directly in the Slack channel.\n(As a licensed user, there is no need to tag @guru.)\nEncourage the requester to\nreact\nor\ncomment\non the card for future feedback.\n✅ Mark as complete once responded.\n3. If No Existing Guru Card Exists\nAlert the\nSupport Content Coordinator (SCC)\nto create a new card.\nCard must include:\n📝 A clear title\n🧩 Categorized tags (e.g. \\\"RICA\\\", \\\"Escalation\\\", \\\"Billing\\\")\n📖 Simple, easy-to-follow content:\nDescription of the issue\nStep-by-step solution\nLinks, screenshots, and contact info (if applicable)\nFeedback\nSuggested responses:\nWe’d love to know if this content helped you!\nHow helpful was this content?\nYour feedback helps us improve the Help Centre.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"a2294eb3-3391-493e-b06a-a67f70c4cd78\",\"title\":\"Slack Channel #Help_Centre SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.


PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the purpose and use of the #help_centre Slack channel, which serves as a central space for accessing support resources and asking questions related to agent-facing issues and common queries.

Scope

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) applies to the use of the #help_centre Slack channel, which serves as a central support space for staff.

The #help_centre channel is a dedicated Slack space designed to:

  • 💬 Allow staff to ask questions and receive timely support
  • 📚 Provide quick access to verified
⚠️ Please note: This channel does not include internal Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) or confidential operational processes. For such queries, please refer to your team’s internal documentation or consult your team lead directly.

How-To Guide: Non-Guru Users

Step 1: Ask Your Question in the Slack Channel

Step 2: Answer Loading

  • The nominated GetGuru User, Customer Care Administrator will tag @Guru to assist with your query.
  • Ignore the Guru Auto Response: “Answer not available”
  • Check the thread for the response once it's posted.
  • The reply includes source links. See “Citations”.
  • You can click the link for more details and share it with your agents.
  • Always check for the verified badge ✅

Step 3: Please provide us with feedback

  • Please use the Slack Emoji (and not the Guru Emojis)

👍 = Accurate and helpful
👎 = Needs work
(Please share feedback: Reply to Thread)


Step 3: Feedback Example

How to Guide: Guru Users

Step 1: Post your question directly in the Slack channel. (As a licensed user, you do not need to tag @guru—just type your question.)

Step 2: Answer Loading

  • Check the thread for the Guru response once it's posted.
  • The reply includes source links. See “Citations”
  • Click the link for more details and share it with your agents.
  • Always check for the verified badge ✅

Step 3: Please provide us with feedback

  • Please click on the Guru Emoji

👍 = Accurate and helpful
👎 = Needs work

Click on the ellipses \\\"three dots\\\"

Click on the ellipses \\\"three dots\\\" = Ask an Expert
Please share feedback

Step 3: Feedback Example

Step 3: Ask an Expert Example

  • Tag Expert: Content & Training Admin @Naadirah Valli who will assist to co-ordinate the verified answer with the Knowledge Expert
  • Add a note: Feedback

Process: Handling a Request

1. Receive & Acknowledge : Non-Guru Users

  • Copy and paste the request exactly as received into the Slack thread.
  • Tag @Guru in the thread to flag the request for knowledge base inclusion.
  • Encourage the requester to react or comment on the card for future feedback.
  • ✅ Mark as complete once responded.

3. Receive & Acknowledge : Guru Users

  • Guru Users to post their question directly in the Slack channel. (As a licensed user, there is no need to tag @guru.)
  • Encourage the requester to react or comment on the card for future feedback.
  • ✅ Mark as complete once responded.

3. If No Existing Guru Card Exists

  • Alert the Support Content Coordinator (SCC) to create a new card.

Card must include:

  • 📝 A clear title
  • 🧩 Categorized tags (e.g. \\\"RICA\\\", \\\"Escalation\\\", \\\"Billing\\\")
  • 📖 Simple, easy-to-follow content:
    • Description of the issue
    • Step-by-step solution
    • Links, screenshots, and contact info (if applicable)
Feedback

Suggested responses:

  1. We’d love to know if this content helped you!
  2. How helpful was this content? 
  3. Your feedback helps us improve the Help Centre.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "guru user", "id": "b9c8486f-f3fd-4269-bcb7-87c96e7074a8", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "Help_Centre", "id": "74bde7f7-b10f-4f0b-b19d-818ac541c4b5", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-09-10T07:41:52.301+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/a2294eb3-3391-493e-b06a-a67f70c4cd78" }, { "id": "e408ca0f-f514-4860-9b9d-7c887ed80ae1", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"e408ca0f-f514-4860-9b9d-7c887ed80ae1\",\"title\":\"What documents are required when a customer has forgotten their SmartLoad PIN?\",\"content\":\"\nTo unblock a SmartLoad account or have the PIN resent, the following documents are required:\nCertified copy of the customer’s ID\nAffidavit from a police station\nrequesting Smartcall to unblock the account and reset the SmartLoad PIN. The affidavit must include:\nFull name and surname\nPhysical address\nCell phone number linked to the SmartLoad account\nLast transaction details on the account\nOnce completed, the documents must be sent via email to\nsupport@smartcall.co.za\n.\nImportant notes:\nAll submitted documents must be\nclear\nand\nnot older than 3 months\n.\nAfter verification, Smartcall will\nunlock the account or send the PIN via SMS\n.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"e408ca0f-f514-4860-9b9d-7c887ed80ae1\",\"title\":\"What documents are required when a customer has forgotten their SmartLoad PIN?\",\"content\":\"

To unblock a SmartLoad account or have the PIN resent, the following documents are required:

  • Certified copy of the customer’s ID
  • Affidavit from a police station requesting Smartcall to unblock the account and reset the SmartLoad PIN. The affidavit must include:
    • Full name and surname
    • Physical address
    • Cell phone number linked to the SmartLoad account
    • Last transaction details on the account

Once completed, the documents must be sent via email to support@smartcall.co.za.

Important notes:

  • All submitted documents must be clear and not older than 3 months.
  • After verification, Smartcall will unlock the account or send the PIN via SMS.


\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-09-09T09:27:41.689+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/e408ca0f-f514-4860-9b9d-7c887ed80ae1" }, { "id": "b233043c-f90b-4e3b-9774-f8cdbb712e65", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"b233043c-f90b-4e3b-9774-f8cdbb712e65\",\"title\":\"How to Cash Out from SmartLoad? - From Nedbank\",\"content\":\"\nSummary\nIf your payment method is SmartLoad, you can easily cash out your money.\nHere’s a quick step-by-step guide on how to transfer money from your SmartLoad account to a Nedbank MobiMoney account:\nDial\n*130*678#\non your phone.\nSelect\nOption - Money Send\nSelect\nOption - Send Money\nSelect\nOption - Nedbank MobiMoney\nDetails\nIf your payment method is SmartLoad, you can transfer your money to an Nedbank MobiMoney account.\nHere’s a detailed guide how to do it step-by-step:\nNedbank MobiMoney Cash out\nDial\n*130*678#\nSelect\n\\\"Option - Money Send\\\"\nSelect\n\\\"Option - Send Money\\\"\nSelect\n\\\"Option - Nedbank MobiMoney\\\"\nEnter\nSmartLoad PIN:\nSelect\n\\\"Option - Continue\\\"\nEnter Cash Out Amount\n(note: a\nR10 fee\nwill be\ndeducted\n).\nEnter 1 to proceed.\nAfter confirming, you will see a pop-up confirmation.\nThe processing time with Nedbank is approximately 1-3 hours.\nKeep the reference number handy, contact SmartLoad at\n011 507 4789\nif you do not receive an SMS from Nedbank after 3 hours.\nReceive SMS Confirmation:\nOnce the transaction is complete, you will receive an\nSMS from Nedbank with the PIN and Voucher Number.\nTake this SMS to a Nedbank ATM, Pick n Pay, Shoprite,\nCheckers, Boxer Stores, or any other participating\nlocations to withdraw your money.\nKeep the SMS safe and do not share it with anyone.\nBy following these steps, you can easily transfer your money\nfrom SmartLoad to an Nedbank Mobimoney Account and access your funds.\nForgot Your PIN or Issues with Accessing Money?\nCall Nedbank MobiMoney support at\n0800 555 111\nfor assistance.\nTerms & Conditions Apply\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"b233043c-f90b-4e3b-9774-f8cdbb712e65\",\"title\":\"How to Cash Out from SmartLoad? - From Nedbank\",\"content\":\"

Summary

If your payment method is SmartLoad, you can easily cash out your money.
Here’s a quick step-by-step guide on how to transfer money from your SmartLoad account to a Nedbank MobiMoney account:

  1. Dial *130*678# on your phone.
  2. Select Option - Money Send
  3. Select Option - Send Money
  4. Select Option - Nedbank MobiMoney

Details
