If feasible and possible, the manager will coordinate and implement a seating adjustment within the designated open-plan areas and at their discretion.\nLOAD-SHEDDING\nDue to the amount of power air conditioners use and the strain/risks this places on the solar system/generator during times of load-shedding, all air conditioners have been set-up so that they will not operate when load-shedding/power failures are in effect.\nThe nominated responsible employee must be aware of loadshedding/power failure times to ensure that they are aware of the reasonings why the units will not work. This will not constitute a unit error/fault and does not need to be reported to the Procurement Officer. The nominated responsible employee must ensure that all air conditioners in their designated area are turned off at the end of the business day, when loadshedding is still in effect, to ensure that the air conditioners do not automatically continue running when the power is restored after business operating hours.\nADDITIONAL INFORMATION\nIf there are any issues/concerns in regard to the air conditioners or the management thereof, same can be reported to the nominated responsible person or to HR promptly so that the matter can be attended to.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"59837a81-fdae-40c5-8819-cec482914ee0\",\"title\":\"Air Conditioning & Heaters Policy\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

The purpose of this policy is to establish guidelines for the safe and effective management of air conditioners within Awesim’s offices, in compliance with South African health and safety legislation. This policy aims to create a comfortable working environment whilst optimising energy usage to manage operational costs.

OFFICE ENVIRONMENT OVERVIEW

There is a combination of private and shared/open-plan offices within Awesim’s office building. An office space is considered private when it has been allocated to one (1) employee as their dedicated space to conduct work. An office space is considered shared/open-plan when the space has been allocated to two (2) or more employees as their dedicated space to conduct work.

AIR CONDITIONER UNIT OVERVIEW

The below table indicates the different types of devices used to control the air conditioning units in the various areas/regions:

Device Type

Area/Region

Device Image

Wall-mounted panel

RAM, Invoicing & Sticking

Built-in panel on the wall-mounted unit

Various

Remote-controlled unit (Type 1)

Stores

Remote-controlled unit (Type 2)

Individual Offices

USE OF HEATERS & ADDITIONAL WARMING METHODS

The use of floor or oil heaters is not permitted in the office due to safety risks and energy inefficiency. Exceptions have only been made for specific areas approved by management, where open-plan areas (e.g. reception) have no carpeting and air conditioning units are ineffective or not available.

If the allocated air conditioning settings during cold seasons do not provide adequate warmth, staff are encouraged to:

  • Wear additional layers of clothing;
  • Wrap a blanket around their legs while seated;
  • Use a hot water bottle.

Hot Water Bottle Safety Precautions:

  • Use bottles that comply with BS1970:2012 standards or equivalent;
  • Fill the bottle with hot (not boiling) water to avoid splitting or burns;
  • Do not overfill – fill to about two-thirds capacity;
  • Expel excess air before sealing tightly;
  • Always use a cover or wrap the bottle in a towel to avoid direct contact with skin;
  • Never sit, lie on, or apply pressure to the hot water bottle;
  • Check the bottle regularly for signs of wear or leaks.

Employees are expected to take personal responsibility for using warming methods safely and appropriately. Misuse or unsafe practices may lead to disciplinary action.

NOMINATED RESPONSIBLE EMPLOYEE

The diverse preferences and comfort levels of individuals working in private and shared/open-plan offices make it essential to have a management process in place. Striking the right balance ensures that the office remains a welcoming and accommodating space for everyone, promoting a collaborative and harmonious work atmosphere.

As such, Awesim will nominate a responsible person per area/region of the office who will be responsible for the management and control of all the air conditioners allocated within their allocated area/region. The nominated responsible people will take on this responsibility for a period of 12 consecutive months. HR will reallocate this responsibility annually.

Responsibilities of each nominated employee:

  • Have full control over the remote/panel for your allocated section (No other employee is allowed to change the temperature or make use of the aircon);
  • Liaise with staff in private offices to manage those air conditioners within your area/region;
  • Control the temp of the air conditioning in shared/open-plan offices in accordance with this policy;
  • Ensure that private offices are using their air conditioners in accordance with this policy;
  • Safekeeping & care of the air conditioners remotes for your allocated area/region;
  • Turn off all air conditioners immediately if requested by management/IT;
  • Ensure all air conditioners are switched off at the end of the business day before leaving the premises;
  • If there was load shedding during the end of the business day and municipal power was not restored before the end of the day, ensure all air conditioners are switched off to ensure that they do not run unnecessarily through the evening when the power is restored after business closure;
  • Arrange with your line manager to nominate a temporary person to manage these responsibilities on days you are not in the office (Sick leave, annual leave, WFH, etc.);
  • Overall management of the air conditioners - report any issues & faults to the Procurement Officer immediately;
  • Ensure that no floor/oil heaters are being used in unauthorised area's;
  • Ensure the overall compliance with this policy witin their designated area's.

Nominated responsible employees' for 2025:

  • Tyra Daniels - Finance, Canteen/kitchen & Admin area's located around the canteen area
  • Liona Govender - HR, IT, admin area, Jo's Office & floating desk area's
  • Jabulile Dlamini - CC Department & reception area's
  • Bongani Moumakwe - RAM, Invoicing, Sticking & stores office area's

TEMPERATURE SETTINGS/CONTROL

The table below indicates the temperature ranges that all air-conditioning units must remain within during the different seasons. These temperatures are guided by health & safety legislation. The temperatures specified herein are applicable to both private and shared/open-plan offices.

Only the nominated responsible employee is permitted to change/adjust the air conditioners temperatures in shared/open-plan offices.

Temperature units:

Season

Months

Min Temp

Max Temp

Summer

December - February

21°C

24°C

Autumn

March - May

22°C

24°C

Winter

June - August

24°C

26°C

Spring

September - November

22°C

24°C

Wall mounted panel units:

Season

Months

Setting Gage

(Top rotation dial)

Temperature Gage (Bottom rotation dial)

Image/Diagram

Autumn & Winter

March -August

Heating

(See red star marking)

30 degree mark from far left following the blue indicator.

Spring & Summer

September - February

Cool Low

(See black star marking)

150 degree mark from far left following the blue indicator.

SHARED/OPEN-PLAN OFFICES

In shared and open-plan office spaces, employees must collectively agree on designated times for the air conditioning to be switched on or off, in line with the company's standardised temperature settings. If employees cannot reach an agreement, the manager responsible for the department will make a final decision and implement the decision accordingly.

Employees are encouraged to dress appropriately for varying office temperatures. This includes keeping suitable clothing, such as a jacket or extra layers, at the office to ensure personal comfort.

If an employee experiences discomfort due to their proximity to an air conditioning unit, they may request a seating change with their line manager. If feasible and possible, the manager will coordinate and implement a seating adjustment within the designated open-plan areas and at their discretion.

LOAD-SHEDDING

Due to the amount of power air conditioners use and the strain/risks this places on the solar system/generator during times of load-shedding, all air conditioners have been set-up so that they will not operate when load-shedding/power failures are in effect.

The nominated responsible employee must be aware of loadshedding/power failure times to ensure that they are aware of the reasonings why the units will not work. This will not constitute a unit error/fault and does not need to be reported to the Procurement Officer. The nominated responsible employee must ensure that all air conditioners in their designated area are turned off at the end of the business day, when loadshedding is still in effect, to ensure that the air conditioners do not automatically continue running when the power is restored after business operating hours.

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

If there are any issues/concerns in regard to the air conditioners or the management thereof, same can be reported to the nominated responsible person or to HR promptly so that the matter can be attended to.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "policy", "id": "4b4e383d-ef16-43ea-8781-9877bf0601e9", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-05-20T11:19:10.252+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/59837a81-fdae-40c5-8819-cec482914ee0" }, { "id": "40873a67-15c1-4f9f-9b46-44b150b69722", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"40873a67-15c1-4f9f-9b46-44b150b69722\",\"title\":\"#calling_problems - Tech Down Time SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines a standardized approach for managing both planned and unplanned system downtime. It ensures efficient handling of disruptions, minimizes service impact, and provides clear communication protocols for affected stakeholders, ensuring minimal disruption to business operations and the efficient resolution of issues.\nSYSTEMS & SERVICES COVERED BY THIS SOP\nThis SOP covers, but is not limited to, the following systems and services:\nEuphoria (Telephone Software) Tech Downtime\nEuphoria TMS\nIn-house Connectivity Issues\nTwilio (WhatsApp) Chat Tech Downtime\nAll Internal Systems Tech Downtime (CMS, Twilio, Internet)\nRaising Tasks / Issues:\nDuring any\ntechnical system downtime\n(planned or unplanned), follow these steps:\nCRITICAL ISSUES:\n⚠️\nCC logs issue on\n#calling_problems\nchannel\nOnce verified as critical, query will be logged on\n#critical_support\nby CCA / TL for IT to assist.\nCCA / TL can ask the developer questions via\nSlack\nor\nin person\n, provided they take\nno longer than 5 minutes\n.\nCRITICAL:\nNeeds to be addressed\nIMMEDIATELY\n🚨\nLogging Tasks:\n📌\nRequester\n:\nCCA / TL / LM\nNormal / Project Tasks:\nLog these as a\nJira ticket\nunder the relevant project.\nIf unsure, contact\nWerner\n.\nUrgent Tasks / Bugs (Must be resolved within this sprint - up to 2 weeks):\nLog these through the\nhelp desk system\n. These will be\nreviewed daily\nduring the IT stand-up meeting.\nCritical Tasks / Queries:\nRaise these\ndirectly in Slack\n—\nno need\nto log them in the help desk.\n#calling_problems\nOnly critical issues should be logged on\n#calling_problems\nslack channel and Once verified as critical, query will be logged on\n#critical_support\nChannel, for IT to assist.\nAny system issues (outside of critical), will be logged in line with the revised process, by CCA / TL.\n1st Point of Contact -\n@Omphile\n[CCA]\n2nd Point of Contact -\n@Tokelo\n[TL]\n3rd Point of Contact -\n@Bernadine Bothwell\n[LM]\nCRITICAL ISSUE:\n⚠️\nCRITICAL:\nNeeds to be addressed\nIMMEDIATELY\n🚨\nEuphoria Tech Downtime\nEuphoria (Telephone Software) Downtime\n– Disruptions in call center operations due to connectivity or software failures.\nStep 1:\nCC reports downtime on\n#calling_problems\n.\nStep 2:\nCCA Troubleshoot and establish the error.\nContact Euphoria Helpdesk for unknown issues (network, software, hardware, configuration, security, or integration).\nAlert In-house IT for app installations, PC settings adjustments, or hardware configurations.\nStep 3:\nInitiate IVR – System Downtime [Ensure IVR message alligns with system down time during office hours: 08:00 -17:00].\nStep 4:\nUpdate Customer Care Roles (set to CHAT only).\nStep 5:\nAlert CC team of system downtime via Slack channel\n#calling_problems\n.\nStep 6:\nInform Ops Team to update Social Media (SM) platforms and Update Out of Office on info@ and RTS inboxes if issue isn’t resolved within appropriate TAT [45 minutes].\nStep 7:\nOnce system is restored, return roles back to BOTH/SM, depending on the queue.\nStep 8:\nSend update on Slack (\n#calling_problems\n) indicating the system has been restored.\nIn-house Connectivity Issues wrt Euphoria\nAlert Inhouse IT for issues such as:\nApp updates\nPC / Audio settings\nHardware configurations\nEuphoria  Contact Details:\nCall:\n+27 87 231 0600\n/  Log:\nNew Support Ticket\nTwilio (WhatsApp) Chat Tech Downtime\nTwilio (WhatsApp) Chat Downtime\n– Inability to send or receive messages affecting customer communication.\nStep 1:\nCC reports downtime on\n#calling_problems\n.\nStep 2:\nCCA Troubleshoot and establish the error.\nAlert IT Helpdesk on\n#critical_support\n.\nStep 3:\nChange Customer Care Roles to CALLS ONLY.\nStep 4:\nInitiate IVR – System Downtime [Ensure IVR message alligns with system down time during office hours: 08:00 -17:00].\nStep 5:\nAlert CC team of system downtime via Slack channel\n#calling_problems\n.\nStep 6:\nInform Ops Team to update SM platforms and Update Out of Office on info@ and RTS inboxes if issue isn’t resolved within appropriate TAT [45 minutes].\nStep 7:\nOnce system is restored, return roles back to BOTH/SM, depending on the queue.\nStep 8:\nSend update on Slack (\n#calling_problems\n) indicating that the system has been restored.\nAll Internal Systems Tech Downtime\nInternal Systems Downtime (CMS, Internet, Twilio, etc.)\n– Failures in core business applications leading to operational inefficiencies.\nStep 1:\nCC reports downtime on\n#calling_problems\n.\nStep 2:\nCCA Troubleshoots and establish the error.\nAlert IT Helpdesk on\n#critical_support\n.\nStep 3:\nInitiate IVR – System Downtime [Ensure IVR message alligns with system down time during office hours: 08:00 -17:00].\nStep 4:\nAlert CC team of system downtime via Slack channel\n#calling_problems\nor walkin to Call Centre.\nStep 5:\nInform Ops Team to update SM platforms and CCA to update Out of Office on info@ and RTS inboxes if issue isn’t resolved within TAT [45 minutes].\nStep 6:\nOnce system is restored, return roles back to BOTH/SM, depending on the queue.\nStep 7:\nSend update on Slack (#calling_problems) indicating the system has been restored.\nPreventative Measures - IT Post-Incident Report:\nFollowing the resolution of downtime, IT will provide a\npost-incident report\ndetailing:\nThe issue encountered\nRoot cause analysis\nRecommended\npreventative measures\nto minimize the recurrence of similar incidents\nCommunication & Confirmation Process\nResolution Feedback:\nCCA\nto share resolution feedback on\n#calling_problems\n(\nSlack Channel\n) –\nReply to thread\n.\nConfirmation & Monitoring:\nCC\nto confirm resolution on\n#calling_problems\n(\nSlack Channel\n) –\nReply to thread\nor escalate if issues reoccur following the fix.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"40873a67-15c1-4f9f-9b46-44b150b69722\",\"title\":\"#calling_problems - Tech Down Time SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.


PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines a standardized approach for managing both planned and unplanned system downtime. It ensures efficient handling of disruptions, minimizes service impact, and provides clear communication protocols for affected stakeholders, ensuring minimal disruption to business operations and the efficient resolution of issues.

SYSTEMS & SERVICES COVERED BY THIS SOP

This SOP covers, but is not limited to, the following systems and services:

  1. Euphoria (Telephone Software) Tech Downtime
  2. Euphoria TMS
  3. In-house Connectivity Issues
  4. Twilio (WhatsApp) Chat Tech Downtime
  5. All Internal Systems Tech Downtime (CMS, Twilio, Internet)

Raising Tasks / Issues:

During any technical system downtime (planned or unplanned), follow these steps:

CRITICAL ISSUES: ⚠️

CC logs issue on #calling_problems channel

Once verified as critical, query will be logged on #critical_support by CCA / TL for IT to assist.

CCA / TL can ask the developer questions via Slack or in person, provided they take no longer than 5 minutes.

CRITICAL: Needs to be addressed IMMEDIATELY 🚨

Logging Tasks: 📌

Requester : CCA / TL / LM

      • Normal / Project Tasks: Log these as a Jira ticket under the relevant project. If unsure, contact Werner.
      • Urgent Tasks / Bugs (Must be resolved within this sprint - up to 2 weeks): Log these through the help desk system. These will be reviewed daily during the IT stand-up meeting.
      • Critical Tasks / Queries: Raise these directly in Slack—no need to log them in the help desk.

#calling_problems

    • Only critical issues should be logged on #calling_problems slack channel and Once verified as critical, query will be logged on #critical_support Channel, for IT to assist.
    • Any system issues (outside of critical), will be logged in line with the revised process, by CCA / TL.

  • 1st Point of Contact -  @Omphile [CCA]
  • 2nd Point of Contact - @Tokelo [TL]
  • 3rd Point of Contact - @Bernadine Bothwell [LM]

CRITICAL ISSUE: ⚠️

CRITICAL: Needs to be addressed IMMEDIATELY 🚨

Euphoria Tech Downtime

Euphoria (Telephone Software) Downtime – Disruptions in call center operations due to connectivity or software failures.

Step 1: CC reports downtime on #calling_problems.

Step 2: CCA Troubleshoot and establish the error.

      1. Contact Euphoria Helpdesk for unknown issues (network, software, hardware, configuration, security, or integration).
      2. Alert In-house IT for app installations, PC settings adjustments, or hardware configurations.

Step 3: Initiate IVR – System Downtime [Ensure IVR message alligns with system down time during office hours: 08:00 -17:00].

Step 4: Update Customer Care Roles (set to CHAT only).

Step 5: Alert CC team of system downtime via Slack channel #calling_problems.

Step 6: Inform Ops Team to update Social Media (SM) platforms and Update Out of Office on info@ and RTS inboxes if issue isn’t resolved within appropriate TAT [45 minutes].

Step 7: Once system is restored, return roles back to BOTH/SM, depending on the queue.

Step 8: Send update on Slack (#calling_problems) indicating the system has been restored.

In-house Connectivity Issues wrt Euphoria

Alert Inhouse IT for issues such as:

  • App updates
  • PC / Audio settings
  • Hardware configurations

Euphoria  Contact Details:

Call:   +27 87 231 0600 /  Log:  New Support Ticket 

Twilio (WhatsApp) Chat Tech Downtime

Twilio (WhatsApp) Chat Downtime – Inability to send or receive messages affecting customer communication.

Step 1: CC reports downtime on #calling_problems.

Step 2: CCA Troubleshoot and establish the error.

  • Alert IT Helpdesk on #critical_support.

Step 3: Change Customer Care Roles to CALLS ONLY.

Step 4: Initiate IVR – System Downtime [Ensure IVR message alligns with system down time during office hours: 08:00 -17:00].

Step 5: Alert CC team of system downtime via Slack channel #calling_problems.

Step 6: Inform Ops Team to update SM platforms and Update Out of Office on info@ and RTS inboxes if issue isn’t resolved within appropriate TAT [45 minutes].

Step 7: Once system is restored, return roles back to BOTH/SM, depending on the queue.

Step 8: Send update on Slack (#calling_problems) indicating that the system has been restored.

All Internal Systems Tech Downtime

Internal Systems Downtime (CMS, Internet, Twilio, etc.) – Failures in core business applications leading to operational inefficiencies.

Step 1: CC reports downtime on #calling_problems.

Step 2: CCA Troubleshoots and establish the error.

  • Alert IT Helpdesk on #critical_support.

Step 3: Initiate IVR – System Downtime [Ensure IVR message alligns with system down time during office hours: 08:00 -17:00].

Step 4: Alert CC team of system downtime via Slack channel #calling_problems or walkin to Call Centre.

Step 5: Inform Ops Team to update SM platforms and CCA to update Out of Office on info@ and RTS inboxes if issue isn’t resolved within TAT [45 minutes].

Step 6: Once system is restored, return roles back to BOTH/SM, depending on the queue.

Step 7: Send update on Slack (#calling_problems) indicating the system has been restored.


Preventative Measures - IT Post-Incident Report:

Following the resolution of downtime, IT will provide a post-incident report detailing:

  • The issue encountered
  • Root cause analysis
  • Recommended preventative measures to minimize the recurrence of similar incidents

Communication & Confirmation Process

  1. Resolution Feedback:
      • CCA to share resolution feedback on #calling_problems (Slack Channel) – Reply to thread.
  2. Confirmation & Monitoring:
      • CC to confirm resolution on #calling_problems (Slack Channel) – Reply to thread or escalate if issues reoccur following the fix.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Calling Problems", "id": "b4c19566-b0c9-441c-bceb-f39b46f95edc", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "Tech", "id": "081de712-0bc6-4aa5-a1c1-0c52967d5eaf", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "Tech down Time", "id": "5d6342d1-7625-4eb0-a278-a79c97a9b931", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-05-15T08:08:28.250+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/40873a67-15c1-4f9f-9b46-44b150b69722" }, { "id": "184c80fb-66b4-437c-92ab-fdbadddfc3e5", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"184c80fb-66b4-437c-92ab-fdbadddfc3e5\",\"title\":\"Invoicing Walk-In SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to streamlines the handling of walk-in orders by providing clear steps for agents, customer care, and FOH staff to capture, prioritize, and invoice orders efficiently. It aims to avoid delays, ensure consistency, and enhance the customer's experience by ensuring prompt processing of walk-in orders in the appropriate queue.\nINVOICING WALK-IN SOP\nWALK-IN ALERT\nAgent (GEN / SALES CODE)\nWalks In\nat\nFOH\n(Front of House / Reception)\nNo Order Captured:\nFOH to capture the order in-house.\nFast Track Order Captured:\nIf a Fast Track order is captured by the agent (via Our Awesim App, USSD, or My.Awesim), the agent must contact the Customer Care (CC) team via WhatsApp or Call to capture the Walk-In Alert for collection.\nCapture Walk-In Workflow on Slack:\nCC / FOH / AMB must capture the Walk-In Alert Workflow on the\n#invoicing_walk-in\n(\nSlack Channel\n)\nReprioritize Queue\nAgent Walk-In Order Handling:\nWhen an agent walks in, their order must be processed efficiently.\nWorkflow Submission & Order Collection:\nIf a submission appears in\n#invoicing_walk-in\n, it indicates that the agent is scheduled to collect the order from FOH.\nWalk-In Order Assistance:\nIf an agent requests assistance in placing an order for\nWalk-In Delivery\n, CC must select the\nWALK IN INVOICE\ndropdown.\nWorkflow Completion & Team Notification:\nCC must complete the\n#invoicing_walk-in\nworkflow to alert the team for further processing.\nHandling Escalations:\nIf the order (SR) escalates due to an\nInflated System Rule\n, the\nCC Supervisor\nmust\naction the Service Request as a priority\nto avoid delays for the agent.\nUse\nEscalation DropDown: WALK IN APPROVED\n.\nOrder Prioritization in the Queue:\nIf an order has already been placed, the\n#invoicing_walk-in\nworkflow serves as a request to\nprioritize the order\nin the invoicing queue.\nThe order details (\nCust Code & SR #\n) will be move to the top of the\nTO INVOICE\nqueue on CMS.\nOrder Processing - Invoicing Team Alerted:\nInvoicers are notified to process the order.\nAction Required:\nFill the parcel and click\n[INVOICED]\nto complete the process.\nSlack Workflow Notification\n(#invoicing_walk-in)\nAssign to Relevant Team Members:\n#invoicing_walk-in workflow will tag @Shanelle Redman or @Omphile for further action.\nReprioritize Queue\n:\nCMS\nSearch for the customer code (Code) or Service Request number (SR #) using\nCtrl + F\n.\nTick the box next to the order.\nSelect\nPrioritize\n(On the top right hand corner of your screen) to move the order to the top of the queue.\n﻿\nRespond to the Thread\n: Update\n#invoicing_walk-in\n(\nSlack Channel\n):\nUpdate the Slack thread to indicate which queue the order is in, the\nNormal Queue (\nTO INVOICE\n)\nor\nSpecial Invoicing\n.\nIf required, alert\nFinance\nto prioritize the parcel (Reply to thread)\n.\nSelect ✅ to confirm actioning/prioritization of the Walk-In.\nOrder Processing: Stores Team\nInvoicers are notified to process the order.\nAction Required:\nFill the parcel and click\n[INVOICED]\nto complete the process.\nTeam Responsibilities\nIf a workflow appears in\n#invoicing_walk-in\n, check whether the order has escalated to your queue to avoid delays.\nSOP Adherence & Review\nEnsure that the correct\nDropDown\nis used.\nCorrect Workflow:\nDropDown: WALK IN INVOICE\nEscalation DropDown: WALK IN APPROVED\n.\nConfirm that the invoice appears at the\nTop of the Queue\n.\nUpdate\n#invoicing_walk-in\nusing the ✅ emoji to confirm prioritization.\nNote:\nPrioritization of Orders is\nonly\napplicable to Walk-Ins.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"184c80fb-66b4-437c-92ab-fdbadddfc3e5\",\"title\":\"Invoicing Walk-In SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.


PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to streamlines the handling of walk-in orders by providing clear steps for agents, customer care, and FOH staff to capture, prioritize, and invoice orders efficiently. It aims to avoid delays, ensure consistency, and enhance the customer's experience by ensuring prompt processing of walk-in orders in the appropriate queue.

INVOICING WALK-IN SOP

WALK-IN ALERT

Agent (GEN / SALES CODE) Walks In at FOH (Front of House / Reception)

  • No Order Captured: FOH to capture the order in-house.

  • Fast Track Order Captured: If a Fast Track order is captured by the agent (via Our Awesim App, USSD, or My.Awesim), the agent must contact the Customer Care (CC) team via WhatsApp or Call to capture the Walk-In Alert for collection.

  • Capture Walk-In Workflow on Slack: CC / FOH / AMB must capture the Walk-In Alert Workflow on the #invoicing_walk-in (Slack Channel)

Reprioritize Queue

Agent Walk-In Order Handling:

  • When an agent walks in, their order must be processed efficiently.

Workflow Submission & Order Collection:

  • If a submission appears in #invoicing_walk-in, it indicates that the agent is scheduled to collect the order from FOH.

Walk-In Order Assistance:

  • If an agent requests assistance in placing an order for Walk-In Delivery, CC must select the WALK IN INVOICE dropdown.

Workflow Completion & Team Notification:

  • CC must complete the #invoicing_walk-in workflow to alert the team for further processing.

Handling Escalations:

  • If the order (SR) escalates due to an Inflated System Rule, the CC Supervisor must action the Service Request as a priority to avoid delays for the agent.
  • Use Escalation DropDown: WALK IN APPROVED.

Order Prioritization in the Queue:

  • If an order has already been placed, the #invoicing_walk-in workflow serves as a request to prioritize the order in the invoicing queue.
  • The order details (Cust Code & SR #) will be move to the top of the TO INVOICE queue on CMS.

Order Processing - Invoicing Team Alerted:

  • Invoicers are notified to process the order.
  • Action Required: Fill the parcel and click [INVOICED] to complete the process.

Slack Workflow Notification (#invoicing_walk-in)

Assign to Relevant Team Members:

  • #invoicing_walk-in workflow will tag @Shanelle Redman or @Omphile for further action.

Reprioritize Queue: CMS

  • Search for the customer code (Code) or Service Request number (SR #) using Ctrl + F.
  • Tick the box next to the order.
  • Select Prioritize (On the top right hand corner of your screen) to move the order to the top of the queue.

﻿

Respond to the Thread: Update #invoicing_walk-in (Slack Channel):

  • Update the Slack thread to indicate which queue the order is in, the Normal Queue (TO INVOICE) or Special Invoicing.
  • If required, alert Finance to prioritize the parcel (Reply to thread).
  • Select ✅ to confirm actioning/prioritization of the Walk-In.

Order Processing: Stores Team

  • Invoicers are notified to process the order.
  • Action Required: Fill the parcel and click [INVOICED] to complete the process.

Team Responsibilities

  • If a workflow appears in #invoicing_walk-in, check whether the order has escalated to your queue to avoid delays.

SOP Adherence & Review

  • Ensure that the correct DropDown is used.

Correct Workflow:

      • DropDown: WALK IN INVOICE
      • Escalation DropDown: WALK IN APPROVED.
  • Confirm that the invoice appears at the Top of the Queue.
  • Update #invoicing_walk-in using the ✅ emoji to confirm prioritization.

Note: Prioritization of Orders is only applicable to Walk-Ins.


\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "invoicing", "id": "22c77f1d-c9b8-4e22-b9f3-9a78d6e53c8e", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "walk in", "id": "2442f60f-987d-4cb6-ad87-16c2b4c3745a", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-05-15T08:07:03.177+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/184c80fb-66b4-437c-92ab-fdbadddfc3e5" }, { "id": "7a75784f-0618-4f9d-8119-c9bc1c19c3e9", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"7a75784f-0618-4f9d-8119-c9bc1c19c3e9\",\"title\":\"MISReport SMS: 43203 Last SMS Received SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the steps to check and verify the last SMS received on MISReport for SMS: 43203, ensuring proper testing and troubleshooting.\nProcess to Check SMS: 43203 Last SMS Received\nAccess the\nMISReport\n.\nSearch for\nSMS: 43203 Last SMS Received\non the dropdown menu and click on view.\nTake a screenshot of the report showing date last received.\nShare the screenshot with\nCC AMB\non #\ncc_ambassadors\nslack channel for the CC AMB on rotation to test the oldeset date.\nOnce the completion status is shared by CC AMB, recheck\nMISReport >>> SMS: 43203 Last SMS Received\nto verify if the test was successful.\nUpdate\nCC AMB\non #\ncc_ambassadors\n(slack channel) on the status and if any further actions required.\nTroubleshooting - If Error / Not Received\nTest the line using a different SIM card.\nIf the issue persists, notify IT on #critical_support to investigate and determine whether the error is internal or external.\nIf the error is from our service provide at Smart Tech, escalate the matter by emailing\nLorinda Wepener\nat\nLorinda@smartcalltech.co.za\nfor support.\nUpdate the team on #critical_support and #\ncc_ambassadors\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"7a75784f-0618-4f9d-8119-c9bc1c19c3e9\",\"title\":\"MISReport SMS: 43203 Last SMS Received SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.


PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the steps to check and verify the last SMS received on MISReport for SMS: 43203, ensuring proper testing and troubleshooting.

Process to Check SMS: 43203 Last SMS Received

  1. Access the MISReport.
  2. Search for SMS: 43203 Last SMS Received on the dropdown menu and click on view.
  3. Take a screenshot of the report showing date last received.
  4. Share the screenshot with CC AMB on #cc_ambassadors slack channel for the CC AMB on rotation to test the oldeset date.
  5. Once the completion status is shared by CC AMB, recheck MISReport >>> SMS: 43203 Last SMS Received to verify if the test was successful.
  6. Update CC AMB on #cc_ambassadors (slack channel) on the status and if any further actions required.

Troubleshooting - If Error / Not Received

  1. Test the line using a different SIM card.
  2. If the issue persists, notify IT on #critical_support to investigate and determine whether the error is internal or external.
  3. If the error is from our service provide at Smart Tech, escalate the matter by emailing Lorinda Wepener at Lorinda@smartcalltech.co.za for support.
  4. Update the team on #critical_support and #cc_ambassadors


\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "43203", "id": "c70af3ec-07ff-490d-b817-56ad2bde3f8d", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "last sms", "id": "d2e97e61-a853-4dfc-b208-205f00dcdd02", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "misreport", "id": "8ab057d0-1649-4c78-af01-3b21e8529645", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "misreport sm", "id": "084a7736-39bc-46f2-b21e-2f5273c031ca", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "SMS", "id": "647e2f6e-00af-4132-9cbd-b737d9fe421e", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-05-15T08:05:30.604+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/7a75784f-0618-4f9d-8119-c9bc1c19c3e9" }, { "id": "60816788-6e64-48e8-afac-8d0dc5db604b", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"60816788-6e64-48e8-afac-8d0dc5db604b\",\"title\":\"CRM Email Reports - Changed Alerts SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to outline the process for reviewing and validating alerts generated in CRM Email Reports for changes made to customer data. This ensures accuracy, prevents data inconsistencies, and upholds data integrity in CRM systems. The procedure applies to changes in telephone numbers, sales representatives, delivery methods, and ID/foreign numbers.\nCRM Email Reports - Changed Alerts (High Level Review)\nThis SOP applies to all customer care representatives, sales representatives, and quality assurance (QA) personnel responsible for verifying and validating CRM changes. It covers the following alerts:\nTelephone Number Changed Alert\nSales Person Changed Alert\nDelivery Method Changed Alert\nID/Foreign Number Changed Alert\nTelephone Number Changed Alert\nSteps:\nCopy the customer code from the report and search for the customer code on CMS.\nReview customer history to validate the number change request.\nVerify the process trigger:\nThe request could be actioned via Call Centre, WhatsApp, or self-submission on\nmy.awesim\nor\nOur Awesim App\n(OTP verification process applies).\nManual number change requests come via the Call Centre or Sales Rep and are actioned as per the\nManual Number Change SOP\n.\nEnsure that the telephone number has been updated correctly as per the Service Request (SR).\nConfirm that the new telephone number is\nSmartRICA registered\n:\nIf not registered, reinstate CC or Sales Rep to assist in completing SmartRICA registration.\nCheck CRM to verify if the number is linked to any other customer codes:\nIf the search returns\nonly one\nupdated code, validation is complete.\nIf the search returns\nmultiple\ncodes, investigate the reason for duplication, raise a\nQA alert\n, and initiate corrective action if CC initiated the SR. If it requires Sales Rep intervention, escalate to Sales or\nRTL\n.\nSales Person Changed Alert & Delivery Method Changed Alert\nSteps:\nCopy the customer code from the report and search for the customer code on CMS.\nFor bulk system changes due to a process (proc) update, see Audit history on CRM.\nReview customer history to validate the Sales Person and/or Delivery Method change.\nEnsure that the Sales Person and/or Delivery Method has been updated correctly as per the SR.\nProcess triggers:\nBase change requests from\nGEN\nto\nSales Rep or vise versa\n.\nID/Foreign Number Changed Alert\nSteps:\nCopy the customer code from the report and search for the customer code on CMS.\nReview customer history to validate the ID/Foreign Passport Number change.\nEnsure that the ID/Foreign Passport Number has been updated correctly as per the SR and\nFile Upload Verification Checks SOP\n.\nProcess triggers:\nChanges made to\nID or Foreign Passport Number\n.\nResponsibilities\nCustomer Care Representatives (CC):\nEnsure changes are correctly verified and documented.\nSales Representatives (Sales Rep):\nAction manual number changes and assist customers with SmartRICA registration when necessary.\nQuality Assurance (QA) Team:\nMonitor duplicate number occurrences, raise alerts, and ensure corrective actions are taken.\nRTL (Regional Team Lead):\nInvestigate and resolve duplicate number issues that require sales rep intervention.\nCompliance & Reporting\nAll updates should align with the respective SOPs (e.g., Manual Number Change SOP).\nAny discrepancies or duplicate entries must be escalated promptly.\nA record of all validated changes should be maintained for audit and compliance purposes.\nReferences\nManual Number Change SOP\nFile Upload Verification Checks SOP\nSmartRICA Registration Guidelines\nCRM / CMS System User Guide\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"60816788-6e64-48e8-afac-8d0dc5db604b\",\"title\":\"CRM Email Reports - Changed Alerts SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.


PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to outline the process for reviewing and validating alerts generated in CRM Email Reports for changes made to customer data. This ensures accuracy, prevents data inconsistencies, and upholds data integrity in CRM systems. The procedure applies to changes in telephone numbers, sales representatives, delivery methods, and ID/foreign numbers.

CRM Email Reports - Changed Alerts (High Level Review)

This SOP applies to all customer care representatives, sales representatives, and quality assurance (QA) personnel responsible for verifying and validating CRM changes. It covers the following alerts:

  • Telephone Number Changed Alert
  • Sales Person Changed Alert
  • Delivery Method Changed Alert
  • ID/Foreign Number Changed Alert

Telephone Number Changed Alert

Steps:

  1. Copy the customer code from the report and search for the customer code on CMS.

  1. Review customer history to validate the number change request.
  2. Verify the process trigger:
  3. The request could be actioned via Call Centre, WhatsApp, or self-submission on my.awesim or Our Awesim App (OTP verification process applies).
  4. Manual number change requests come via the Call Centre or Sales Rep and are actioned as per the Manual Number Change SOP.
  5. Ensure that the telephone number has been updated correctly as per the Service Request (SR).
  6. Confirm that the new telephone number is SmartRICA registered:
  7. If not registered, reinstate CC or Sales Rep to assist in completing SmartRICA registration.
  8. Check CRM to verify if the number is linked to any other customer codes:
  9. If the search returns only one updated code, validation is complete.
  10. If the search returns multiple codes, investigate the reason for duplication, raise a QA alert, and initiate corrective action if CC initiated the SR. If it requires Sales Rep intervention, escalate to Sales or RTL.

Sales Person Changed Alert & Delivery Method Changed Alert

Steps:

  1. Copy the customer code from the report and search for the customer code on CMS.
  2. For bulk system changes due to a process (proc) update, see Audit history on CRM.
  3. Review customer history to validate the Sales Person and/or Delivery Method change.
  4. Ensure that the Sales Person and/or Delivery Method has been updated correctly as per the SR.
  5. Process triggers:
  6. Base change requests from GEN to Sales Rep or vise versa.

ID/Foreign Number Changed Alert

Steps:

  1. Copy the customer code from the report and search for the customer code on CMS.
  2. Review customer history to validate the ID/Foreign Passport Number change.
  3. Ensure that the ID/Foreign Passport Number has been updated correctly as per the SR and File Upload Verification Checks SOP.
  4. Process triggers:
  5. Changes made to ID or Foreign Passport Number.

Responsibilities

  • Customer Care Representatives (CC): Ensure changes are correctly verified and documented.
  • Sales Representatives (Sales Rep): Action manual number changes and assist customers with SmartRICA registration when necessary.
  • Quality Assurance (QA) Team: Monitor duplicate number occurrences, raise alerts, and ensure corrective actions are taken.
  • RTL (Regional Team Lead): Investigate and resolve duplicate number issues that require sales rep intervention.

Compliance & Reporting

  • All updates should align with the respective SOPs (e.g., Manual Number Change SOP).
  • Any discrepancies or duplicate entries must be escalated promptly.
  • A record of all validated changes should be maintained for audit and compliance purposes.

References

  • Manual Number Change SOP
  • File Upload Verification Checks SOP
  • SmartRICA Registration Guidelines
  • CRM / CMS System User Guide

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "alerts", "id": "480b2597-21c1-48ba-9605-9abb898212ac", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "changed", "id": "ebf77b31-e5bf-4a69-a755-2ac229d61d84", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "crm change", "id": "6a4d3988-238d-4f39-891e-a1948253b0a1", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-05-15T08:04:05.469+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/60816788-6e64-48e8-afac-8d0dc5db604b" }, { "id": "979272c9-b2de-44fc-a14c-edbf77e3f141", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"979272c9-b2de-44fc-a14c-edbf77e3f141\",\"title\":\"Euphoria Abandoned Call Management SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the process to monitor, manage, and act on abandoned calls on the Euphoria Call Centre platform. The goal is to ensure prompt customer follow-up and maintain high service standards.\nResponsibilities\nCustomer Care Administrator (CCA):\nActively monitor real-time agent statuses and abandoned call rates on the Euphoria portal.\nCustomer Care Agents (CC):\nResponsible for returning customer calls as instructed via Service Requests (SR).\nSupervisors / Line Manager:\nMust be alerted when the abandoned call threshold is reached or exceeded.\nProcedure\nMonitoring Calls\nLog in to the\nEuphoria Management Portal\n.\nNavigate to the\nDashboard\n.\nMonitor the following tabs to view unanswered/abandoned calls:\nRealtime Queues\nQueue An alytics\nFocus on the\nAbandoned Call Rate\n:\nThreshold:\nNo more than\n5\nabandoned calls at a time.\nIf the. number of abandoned calls reaches\n5 or more\n,\nimmediately flag\nthis with the\nSupervisor / Line Manager\n.\nAction on Abandoned Calls\nIdentify the agent(s) associated with the abandoned call(s).\nSearch the\ntelephone number\nin\nCRM / CMS\n.\nIf the number exists in our records:\nLog a\nService Request (SR)\non CMS for CC to contact the customer.\nIn the\nDropdown\n, select:\nReinstate call centre\n[SR logged in real time]\nIf the number is\nnot yet linked\nto a customer code:\nSend a\nstandard email\nto notify CC and prompt agent follow-up.\n[10 TO 15 Minutes wait for SR to be generated]\nEmail With Number Instructions\nTo:\nsms@awesim.co.za\nSubject Line:\n(Insert customer telephone number e.g. 27793563189)\nBody\n(exact text, no edits):\nEuphoria Abandoned Call - 1) Please return the customer's call, and assist 2) We apologize as all our agents were currently busy at the time of the call.\nImportant:\nDo\nnot\ninclude any signatures.\nEnsure the email body is\nclear\nwith\nno spaces\nbefore or after the message.\nUse a\nblank email template\n.\nNotes\nMonitor and action abandoned calls in\nreal-time\n.\nRun spot checks on SR's to confirm customer callbacks have been completed.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"979272c9-b2de-44fc-a14c-edbf77e3f141\",\"title\":\"Euphoria Abandoned Call Management SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.


PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the process to monitor, manage, and act on abandoned calls on the Euphoria Call Centre platform. The goal is to ensure prompt customer follow-up and maintain high service standards.

Responsibilities

  • Customer Care Administrator (CCA):
    Actively monitor real-time agent statuses and abandoned call rates on the Euphoria portal.
  • Customer Care Agents (CC):
    Responsible for returning customer calls as instructed via Service Requests (SR).
  • Supervisors / Line Manager:
    Must be alerted when the abandoned call threshold is reached or exceeded.

Procedure

Monitoring Calls

  1. Log in to the Euphoria Management Portal.
  2. Navigate to the Dashboard.
  3. Monitor the following tabs to view unanswered/abandoned calls:
  4. Realtime Queues
  5. Queue An alytics
  6. Focus on the Abandoned Call Rate:
  7. Threshold: No more than 5 abandoned calls at a time.
  8. If the. number of abandoned calls reaches 5 or more, immediately flag this with the Supervisor / Line Manager.

Action on Abandoned Calls

  1. Identify the agent(s) associated with the abandoned call(s).
  2. Search the telephone number in CRM / CMS.

If the number exists in our records:

    • Log a Service Request (SR) on CMS for CC to contact the customer.
    • In the Dropdown, select: Reinstate call centre

[SR logged in real time]

If the number is not yet linked to a customer code:

    • Send a standard email to notify CC and prompt agent follow-up.

[10 TO 15 Minutes wait for SR to be generated]

Email With Number Instructions

To: sms@awesim.co.za

Subject Line: (Insert customer telephone number e.g. 27793563189)

Body (exact text, no edits):

Euphoria Abandoned Call - 1) Please return the customer's call, and assist 2) We apologize as all our agents were currently busy at the time of the call.

Important:

  • Do not include any signatures.
  • Ensure the email body is clear with no spaces before or after the message.
  • Use a blank email template.

Notes

  • Monitor and action abandoned calls in real-time.
  • Run spot checks on SR's to confirm customer callbacks have been completed.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "abandoned", "id": "8fa731ce-6b4d-4f01-b3cf-2350668d20b1", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "euphoria abandon", "id": "6fda623d-a276-4c70-bc85-58db2ba9d247", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-05-15T08:03:28.572+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/979272c9-b2de-44fc-a14c-edbf77e3f141" }, { "id": "8e283e11-09c6-4870-ac02-f300bb5a006c", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"8e283e11-09c6-4870-ac02-f300bb5a006c\",\"title\":\"UNREACHABLE ON HOLD - 3 distinct SR attempts\",\"content\":\"\nSMSes\nAWESIM AGENT: We have tried to contact you numerous times. Your account has been placed On Hold. To reactivate, SMS 43203 or WhatsApp 0600702666 for assistance.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"8e283e11-09c6-4870-ac02-f300bb5a006c\",\"title\":\"UNREACHABLE ON HOLD - 3 distinct SR attempts\",\"content\":\"

SMSes

AWESIM AGENT: We have tried to contact you numerous times. Your account has been placed On Hold. To reactivate, SMS 43203 or WhatsApp 0600702666 for assistance.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-05-13T12:47:48.692+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/8e283e11-09c6-4870-ac02-f300bb5a006c" }, { "id": "e4aba217-1823-4d3d-9bed-eb7e7ee4c993", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"e4aba217-1823-4d3d-9bed-eb7e7ee4c993\",\"title\":\"Base Change Request: form Sales to Sales\",\"content\":\"\nPlease note that there is an available Sales Rep that is based in your new address who's name is\n[provide Sales Rep Name].\nWould you like to be serviced by Him/ Her instead?\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"e4aba217-1823-4d3d-9bed-eb7e7ee4c993\",\"title\":\"Base Change Request: form Sales to Sales\",\"content\":\"

Please note that there is an available Sales Rep that is based in your new address who's name is [provide Sales Rep Name].Would you like to be serviced by Him/ Her instead?

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-04-16T08:17:59.603+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/e4aba217-1823-4d3d-9bed-eb7e7ee4c993" }, { "id": "f5e3dad7-e90c-4c16-a895-e3ddf5dc2df0", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"f5e3dad7-e90c-4c16-a895-e3ddf5dc2df0\",\"title\":\"Account Creation\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nLegend\nCMS\nCustomer Management System\nCRM\nCustomer Relationship Management\nSR\nService Request\nPURPOSE\nTo outline the process for creating agent accounts in internal systems via cross functional integration of the Customer Relationship System and Customer Management System.\nProcedure and Responsibilities:\nInitiate Account Request:\nTrigger:\nThe customer care consultant submits an account creation request via the Customer Management System for Account Creation.\nOutcome:\nThe customer care consultant will know how to create an account from inception until its ready to queue for invoicing.\nPrerequisite\nFull agent account information (ID/Passport, Contacts Name & Surname, Address etc.)\nDelivery method\nSales person\nVerify Request Details:\nThe Operations, Call Centre unit is the first in contacted for clarification if necessary.\nThe customer care consultant reviews the submitted details for accuracy.\nApproval Process:\nUpon successful creation the account is routed to invoicing for processing of orders and invoicing checks to be conducted, follow invoicing processes.\nApproval or Denial:\nIf approved, proceed to the next account creation.\nIf denied - 1) call the agent for rectification of the error.\n2) Ask the agent to initiate a chat to correct the issue by requesting the agent to send the relevant document or address, route to drop down “info  SMS”.\nCreate User account\nClick on the link that is pending account generation \\\"New ID105129\\\".\n1.1. The system will automatically open CRM upon clicking of the Syspro Customer Code consisting the\nagents information preloaded from CMS.\nClick on the Syspro customer code to generate customer code.\nAfter the customer code has been generated, click the blue box to create customer.\nCopy the newly generated code and revert CMS to paste the code in the box next to the waybill and click search option to populate customer information on CMS.\n4.1 Confirm if the agents address is pinned accurately and corresponds with Google maps.\nCopy the customer code and access the my.awsim application to save the agents documents.\n5.1 Ensure that the information reflected on the agents account corresponds with CMS.\nConfirm the agents requested networks and the number of sim cards that are pending invoicing under \\\"comments\\\"\n6.1  In an instance where the number of sim cards and networks are not indicated, confirm or tick all\nNormal\nsim cards for all networks and confirm only 10 sim cards per network under \\\"Stock Order\\\". Set\nas Default.\nUpdate comments section \\\"Welcome pack\\\" and route the account SR to \\\"TO INVOICE\\\".\nSystem Issues:\nAlways raise system issues experienced as per the system escalation process “calling_problems”.\nTraining (if required):\nAlways request assistance from the customer care supervisor for clarity and rectification of the process as and when required.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"f5e3dad7-e90c-4c16-a895-e3ddf5dc2df0\",\"title\":\"Account Creation\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.

Legend

CMS

Customer Management System

CRM

Customer Relationship Management

SR

Service Request


PURPOSE

To outline the process for creating agent accounts in internal systems via cross functional integration of the Customer Relationship System and Customer Management System.

Procedure and Responsibilities:
  1. Initiate Account Request:
    • Trigger: The customer care consultant submits an account creation request via the Customer Management System for Account Creation.
    • Outcome: The customer care consultant will know how to create an account from inception until its ready to queue for invoicing.
    • Prerequisite
      • Full agent account information (ID/Passport, Contacts Name & Surname, Address etc.)
      • Delivery method
      • Sales person
  2. Verify Request Details:
    • The Operations, Call Centre unit is the first in contacted for clarification if necessary.
    • The customer care consultant reviews the submitted details for accuracy.
  3. Approval Process:
    • Upon successful creation the account is routed to invoicing for processing of orders and invoicing checks to be conducted, follow invoicing processes.
    • Approval or Denial:
      • If approved, proceed to the next account creation.
      • If denied - 1) call the agent for rectification of the error.
      • 2) Ask the agent to initiate a chat to correct the issue by requesting the agent to send the relevant document or address, route to drop down “info  SMS”.

Create User account

  1. Click on the link that is pending account generation \\\"New ID105129\\\".

1.1. The system will automatically open CRM upon clicking of the Syspro Customer Code consisting the

agents information preloaded from CMS.

  1. Click on the Syspro customer code to generate customer code.

  1. After the customer code has been generated, click the blue box to create customer.
  1. Copy the newly generated code and revert CMS to paste the code in the box next to the waybill and click search option to populate customer information on CMS.

4.1 Confirm if the agents address is pinned accurately and corresponds with Google maps.

  1. Copy the customer code and access the my.awsim application to save the agents documents.

5.1 Ensure that the information reflected on the agents account corresponds with CMS.

  1. Confirm the agents requested networks and the number of sim cards that are pending invoicing under \\\"comments\\\"

6.1  In an instance where the number of sim cards and networks are not indicated, confirm or tick all

Normal sim cards for all networks and confirm only 10 sim cards per network under \\\"Stock Order\\\". Set

as Default.

  1. Update comments section \\\"Welcome pack\\\" and route the account SR to \\\"TO INVOICE\\\".

System Issues:

    • Always raise system issues experienced as per the system escalation process “calling_problems”.

Training (if required):

    • Always request assistance from the customer care supervisor for clarity and rectification of the process as and when required.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-04-07T14:24:56.806+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/f5e3dad7-e90c-4c16-a895-e3ddf5dc2df0" }, { "id": "5e036cb3-66d1-40b3-ad23-c4ec1fba715a", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"5e036cb3-66d1-40b3-ad23-c4ec1fba715a\",\"title\":\"Registering a new Awesim RICA Agent\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nLEGEND\nCC\nCustomer Care\nCMS\nCustomer Management System\nCRM\nCustomer Relationship Management\nSR\nService Requests\nPURPOSE\nTo outline the process of registering a new Awesim RICA agent.\nProcedure and Responsibilities:\nNew agent request synopsis:\nTrigger:\nAn individual wants to become a registered Awesim RICA agent.\nOutcome:\nThe customer care consultant will know how to register a New Awesim RICA agent from inception until closure of the SR.\nCustomer Care Consultant Procedure Methodology / Quality fundamentals.\nMonitoring\n: Check the SR's history occurrences to avoid repetition and oversights to ensure quality within the process of providing accurate responses directed to the query at hand.\nVerification\n: Confirm the prospect agents identity and verify that they meet all necessary requirements to register as an Awesim RICA agent.\nDocumentation\n: Ensure all required documentation is sent, including proof of address, identification document (ID/Passport), cell phone number and a picture of the agent holding their ID/Passport next to their face.\nSystem Entry\n: Accurately input the prospect agent's details into the system, ensuring all fields are properly completed.\nConfirmation\n: Provide the agent with a confirmation of their registration and the next steps in the process.\nVerify Request Details:\nThe Operations, Call Centre unit is the first in contacted for clarification if necessary.\nThe customer care consultant reviews the submitted details for accuracy.\nApproval Process:\nUpon successful documentation, the account is routed to account creation with drop down “To Create”, follow account processes.\nSuccessful or Unsuccessful:\nIf Successful, proceed to submitting the SmartRICA registration and proceed to the next service request.\nIf Unsuccessful - Inform the agent for rectification of the error documents.\nNew agent workflow table:\nProcess Steps\nCustomer Care Consultant Actionable(s)\nGet Guru Canned Responses\nSR Comment\nImages\nSR Drop Down Routing\n1.\nCMS\n“New” / “Next”\nService Request is initiated.\nNull\nNull\nNull\nNull\n2.\nSR query Identification:\nA new agent join request linked to Reply \\\"Number 1\\\".\nA request to join Awesim as a RICA Agent is initiated.\nChat (Twilio) Auto Response:\nChat (Twilio) Auto Reponse\nNull\nNew agent\nNull\nChat Waiting\n3.\nNew Agent to share required documentation:\nThe cell phone number for RICA registration.\nA clear photo of the agents ID/Passport.\nA photo of the agent holding their ID/Passport next to their face.\nProof of address which must display the agents name, back dated to no more than 3 months old.\nNB:\nApply the procedure & Methodology points / Quality Fundamentals.\nAWESIM: RICA Agent - Getting Started!\nNew agent: All documents and photos provided.\nNull\nChat Waiting\n4.\nSearch the provided agents ID/Passport number and cellphone number in CMS/CRM to check if the agent is already in our database.\nNB:\nDuplicate agent accounts are not allowed.\nNull\nNull\nCMS/CRM CHECKS\nChat Waiting\n5.\nIf the agent is existing - proceed with\nReturning Agent SOP.\nNull\nNull\nExisting Account\nNull\n6.\nSearch the agent's confirmed cellphone number to verify if it is SmartRICA registered.\nIf No - proceed with SmartRICA Registering the agent via Smartcall Online:\nClick here\n-\nSmartcall Online\nSmartcall RICA Registration Submission\nRegistered Number\n﻿\nUnregistered Number\n﻿\nNew RICA agent captured\nChat Waiting\n6.1\nFor successful SmartRICA Registration status: Access Slack \\\"\ncalling_updates\\\"\nand complete the workflow as stipulated per below;\n6.1.1 Date of registration - 2025/02/28\n6.1.2 The CC user ID - TokeloM\n6.1.3 The registered number - 27790761377\n6.1.4 Status - Successful\n6.1.5 One Time Pin optional - This option is done only if the workflow submission is completed by a teammate.\n6.1.6 Reason if Error optional - When there's an error dictated leave the status under Pending with the full description of the error.\nOnce completed click on submit.\nNull\nNull\nSmartRICA Workflow\nNull\n6.2\nFor unsuccessful SmartRICA Registration status - proceed to Steps 3 & 6 and resubmit the SmartRICA Registration.\nNull\nNull\nNull\nNull\nInbound:\nchecks performed by CC consultant\nThe agent replies SMS has been received - the CC will use PBVerify to Confirm.\nIf Successful - Proceed to Step 7.\nIf Unsuccessful - Proceed to Step 6.2.\nNull\nNull\nNull\nNull\n7.\nConfirmation Delivery Address.\nDelivery Address - Home or Work\nPending Delivery Address\nNull\nChat Waiting\n7.1\nBased on agent response - send the relevant Canned Response\nHome address\nWork address\nPending Delivery Address\nNull\nChat Waiting\nInbound:\nchecks performed by CC consultant\nSearch the delivery address and check if the location can be found on\nGoogle Maps\n.\nNull\nNull\nNull\nNull\n8.\nQuestion: Is the provided address feasible and attainable on Google Maps?\nYes - proceed to step 9\nNo - proceed with\nGeo Location SOP\nDelivery Address - Google Co-Ordinates ++ Geo Location Sharing\nNull\nNull\nNull\nInbound:\nchecks performed by CC consultant\nIf successful - proceed to Step 9\nPartial - update CMS Customer Info Table Fields\nIf Unsuccessful - follow Trouble Shooting Guide\nDelivery Address - Google Co-Ordinates ++ Geo Location Sharing\nNull\nAddress Field\nNull\n9.\nOrder (Networks) Confirmation\nStock Order (Networks) request\nPending Network Order\nNull\nChat Waiting\n10.\nSend the order confirmation.\nNumber of sim card allocation per Network Request:\n•        4 = 10 sims for each network (Default Starter Package).\n•        3 = 15 sims for each network.\n•        2 = 20 sims for each network.\n•        1 = 30 sims\nShare the relevant Promotional Information respective to the requested networks, ( e.g\nTelkom\n,\nMTN\n,\nCellC\n).\nOnce Canned Response is sent, \\\"End Chat\\\" to initiate the SurveyMonkey Rating Link.\nOrder Confirmation\nUpdate with the relevant network requests.\nExample(s):\n- All Networks. CMS Confirmed. DOT new Account Agent.\n- Vodacom Only. CMS Confirmed.  DOT new Account Agent.\nStock Order Table\nTo create - [End Request] Button\nSystem Issues:\nAlways raise system issues experienced as per the system escalation process “calling_problems”.\nTraining (if required):\nAlways request assistance from the customer care supervisor for clarity and rectification of the process as and when required.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"5e036cb3-66d1-40b3-ad23-c4ec1fba715a\",\"title\":\"Registering a new Awesim RICA Agent\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.

LEGEND

CC

Customer Care

CMS

Customer Management System

CRM

Customer Relationship Management

SR

Service Requests


PURPOSE

To outline the process of registering a new Awesim RICA agent.

Procedure and Responsibilities:

New agent request synopsis:

    • Trigger: An individual wants to become a registered Awesim RICA agent.
    • Outcome: The customer care consultant will know how to register a New Awesim RICA agent from inception until closure of the SR.
    • Customer Care Consultant Procedure Methodology / Quality fundamentals.
      • Monitoring: Check the SR's history occurrences to avoid repetition and oversights to ensure quality within the process of providing accurate responses directed to the query at hand.
      • Verification: Confirm the prospect agents identity and verify that they meet all necessary requirements to register as an Awesim RICA agent.
      • Documentation: Ensure all required documentation is sent, including proof of address, identification document (ID/Passport), cell phone number and a picture of the agent holding their ID/Passport next to their face.
      • System Entry: Accurately input the prospect agent's details into the system, ensuring all fields are properly completed.
      • Confirmation: Provide the agent with a confirmation of their registration and the next steps in the process.
  1. Verify Request Details:
    • The Operations, Call Centre unit is the first in contacted for clarification if necessary.
    • The customer care consultant reviews the submitted details for accuracy.
  2. Approval Process:
    • Upon successful documentation, the account is routed to account creation with drop down “To Create”, follow account processes.
    • Successful or Unsuccessful:
      • If Successful, proceed to submitting the SmartRICA registration and proceed to the next service request.

If Unsuccessful - Inform the agent for rectification of the error documents.

New agent workflow table:

Process Steps

Customer Care Consultant Actionable(s)

Get Guru Canned Responses

SR Comment

Images

SR Drop Down Routing 

1.

CMS “New” / “Next” Service Request is initiated.

Null

Null

Null 

Null

2.

SR query Identification:

  • A new agent join request linked to Reply \\\"Number 1\\\".
  • A request to join Awesim as a RICA Agent is initiated.

Chat (Twilio) Auto Response: Chat (Twilio) Auto Reponse

Null

New agent

Null

Chat Waiting

3.

New Agent to share required documentation:

  • The cell phone number for RICA registration.
  • A clear photo of the agents ID/Passport.
  • A photo of the agent holding their ID/Passport next to their face.
  • Proof of address which must display the agents name, back dated to no more than 3 months old.

NB: Apply the procedure & Methodology points / Quality Fundamentals.

AWESIM: RICA Agent - Getting Started! 

New agent: All documents and photos provided.

Null

Chat Waiting

4.

Search the provided agents ID/Passport number and cellphone number in CMS/CRM to check if the agent is already in our database.

NB: Duplicate agent accounts are not allowed.

Null

Null

CMS/CRM CHECKS

Chat Waiting

5.

If the agent is existing - proceed with Returning Agent SOP.

Null 

Null

Existing Account

Null

6.

Search the agent's confirmed cellphone number to verify if it is SmartRICA registered.


If No - proceed with SmartRICA Registering the agent via Smartcall Online: Click here - Smartcall Online


Smartcall RICA Registration Submission

  • Registered Number﻿
  • Unregistered Number
  • ﻿New RICA agent captured

Chat Waiting

6.1

For successful SmartRICA Registration status: Access Slack \\\"calling_updates\\\"and complete the workflow as stipulated per below;

  • 6.1.1 Date of registration - 2025/02/28
  • 6.1.2 The CC user ID - TokeloM
  • 6.1.3 The registered number - 27790761377
  • 6.1.4 Status - Successful
  • 6.1.5 One Time Pin optional - This option is done only if the workflow submission is completed by a teammate.
  • 6.1.6 Reason if Error optional - When there's an error dictated leave the status under Pending with the full description of the error.

Once completed click on submit.

Null

Null

SmartRICA Workflow

Null

6.2

For unsuccessful SmartRICA Registration status - proceed to Steps 3 & 6 and resubmit the SmartRICA Registration.

Null

Null

Null

Null

Inbound: checks performed by CC consultant

The agent replies SMS has been received - the CC will use PBVerify to Confirm.

  • If Successful - Proceed to Step 7.
  • If Unsuccessful - Proceed to Step 6.2.

Null

Null

Null

Null

7.

Confirmation Delivery Address.

Delivery Address - Home or Work

Pending Delivery Address

Null

Chat Waiting

7.1

Based on agent response - send the relevant Canned Response 

  • Home address
  • Work address 

Pending Delivery Address

Null

Chat Waiting

Inbound: checks performed by CC consultant 

Search the delivery address and check if the location can be found on Google Maps.

Null

Null

Null

Null

8.

Question: Is the provided address feasible and attainable on Google Maps?

Yes - proceed to step 9
No - proceed with Geo Location SOP 

Delivery Address - Google Co-Ordinates ++ Geo Location Sharing

Null

Null

Null

Inbound: checks performed by CC consultant 

If successful - proceed to Step 9
Partial - update CMS Customer Info Table Fields
If Unsuccessful - follow Trouble Shooting Guide 

Delivery Address - Google Co-Ordinates ++ Geo Location Sharing

Null

Address Field

Null

9.

Order (Networks) Confirmation

Stock Order (Networks) request

Pending Network Order

Null

Chat Waiting

10.

Send the order confirmation.
Number of sim card allocation per Network Request:


•        4 = 10 sims for each network (Default Starter Package).
•        3 = 15 sims for each network.
•        2 = 20 sims for each network.
•        1 = 30 sims

  • Share the relevant Promotional Information respective to the requested networks, ( e.g Telkom, MTN, CellC).
  • Once Canned Response is sent, \\\"End Chat\\\" to initiate the SurveyMonkey Rating Link.

Order Confirmation

Update with the relevant network requests.

Example(s):


- All Networks. CMS Confirmed. DOT new Account Agent.
- Vodacom Only. CMS Confirmed.  DOT new Account Agent. 

Stock Order Table

To create - [End Request] Button

System Issues:

      • Always raise system issues experienced as per the system escalation process “calling_problems”.

Training (if required):

      • Always request assistance from the customer care supervisor for clarity and rectification of the process as and when required.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-03-28T08:36:45.062+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/5e036cb3-66d1-40b3-ad23-c4ec1fba715a" }, { "id": "eb0a9e9f-0ef9-42d9-b99f-5f7c2965305d", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"eb0a9e9f-0ef9-42d9-b99f-5f7c2965305d\",\"title\":\"RICA Right & Recharge \",\"content\":\"\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"eb0a9e9f-0ef9-42d9-b99f-5f7c2965305d\",\"title\":\"RICA Right & Recharge \",\"content\":\"

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "networks", "id": "35b7376a-7f87-4e76-bae9-96aee3924e72", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "recharge", "id": "926d70ca-0c3a-487e-8f04-597a6816b575", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "RICA", "id": "cf03fec7-cd13-4081-9ace-b67fe64d147a", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "ussd", "id": "415374f7-055c-474d-9a2a-5eecdd8bee0b", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-03-18T12:12:53.135+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/eb0a9e9f-0ef9-42d9-b99f-5f7c2965305d" }, { "id": "718f9cb7-12df-43ed-89c8-0a7098fff6f7", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"718f9cb7-12df-43ed-89c8-0a7098fff6f7\",\"title\":\"Recruitment Referral Fee Policy \",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nAwesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd has implemented this recruitment referral fee policy & procedure to incentivise and reward employees for actively participating in the recruitment process by referring qualified candidates. This policy encourages employees to tap into their networks and recommend individuals who possess the skills, experience, and cultural fit necessary for the position advertised & for Awesim’s success overall.\nBy offering a financial incentive, such as a referral fee, the company promotes employee engagement and involvement in talent acquisition, leading to a broader candidate pool and increased chances of hiring top-notch personnel.\nFurthermore, this policy fosters a culture of collaboration, as employees become more invested in the success of their colleagues and the overall growth of Awesim.\nAll full-time employees of Awesim are eligible to participate in the recruitment referral fee program, subject to the terms and conditions outlined in this policy & procedure.\nDEFINITIONS\n\\\"\nReferral\\\"\nrefers to the act of recommending or suggesting a candidate for a specific job vacancy advertised within Awesim that qualifies.\n\\\"\nReferral Fee\\\"\nis a monetary reward or incentive provided to the employee for successfully referring a candidate who is subsequently hired by Awesim and successfully completes their probationary period and any extensions thereafter.\n\\\"\nReferring Employee\\\"\nrefers to an individual who works for Awesim and recommends/sends the CV and details of potential candidate/s for qualifying vacant positions within Awesim. This refers to an existing Awesim employee already working for the company. May be referred to as “Awesim employee”.\n\\\"\nReferred candidate\\\"\nrefers to an external individual who has been recommended or referred for a position vacancy by the referring employee.\n\\\"\nProbationary period\\\"\nrefers to a specific duration of time during which an employee's performance and suitability for a job & fit within Awesim are evaluated.\nELIGIBILITY CRITERIA FOR REFERRALS\nTo be eligible for the referral fee, the referred candidate must meet the following criteria:\nThe employee referring the candidate must complete the\nCandidate Referral Motivation\nslack workflow before the deadline date stipulated on the advert. This workflow can be found on the #general channel in slack.\nThe candidate must be an external candidate/individual who is not currently employed by Awesim.\nThe CV must meet the necessary requirements of the position, including: education, skills, experience, etc.\nThe referred candidate must be hired within the position that they were referred for and if that vacant position qualifies for the referral fee.\nThe candidate must successfully complete their probationary period and any other extension periods that may be applied as determined by Awesim in order to qualify for the referral fee.\nREFERRAL PROCESS\nTo be eligible for the referral fee, the Awesim employee must ensure that:\nThey complete the Candidate Referral Motivation slack workflow.\nPlease note\n: Applications received after the deadline date stipulated in the Job Advert, will not qualify for the referral fee.\nThe referred candidate must complete the Wamly video interview as part of the application process.\nWithin the form in the Wamly submission, the external candidate/individual must state the name and surname of the Awesim employee who referred them.  Please note: If the Awesim Employee name & surname is not included in the candidate's Wamly form submission, the employee will not qualify for the referral fee.\nThe referred candidate must be aware of and have given the Awesim employee consent to share his/her details for consideration.\nThe referred candidate must not have been previously referred by another Awesim employee for the same position.\nThe Awesim employee may be required to participate in the recruitment process, such as providing additional information or participating in an interview, if deemed necessary by the hiring team.\nNote: If candidates or Awesim employees do not follow the above process, applications will unfortunately not be considered for the referral fee.\nREFERRAL FEE AMOUNT\nShould all criteria within this policy/procedure be met, the referring employee will be eligible to receive a referral fee of R1 000.00 (One thousand rand) for each successful appointment/placement.\nPAYMENT PROCESS\nReferral fees will only be paid to the Awesim employee once the referred candidate has successfully completed their probationary period and any other extension periods that may apply as determined by Awesim.\nShould the referred candidate
