successfully complete their probationary period, the referral fee will be paid via EFT into the Awesim employees bank account in the month following the probationary period completion period with the relevant payroll run.\nEMPLOYEE EXCLUSIONS / DISQUALIYING CRITERIA\nHuman Resources Staff: HR personnel are excluded as a result of their involvement and responsibilities within the recruitment process. This prevents any conflict of interest, as well as biased decision-making.\nExecutives: Executive team members are excluded as a result of their involvement in the final appointment decision of all positions within Awesim. This prevents any conflict of interest, as well as biased decision-making.\nManagement: Department Managers, Regional Team Leaders and other Line Manager for which the position will be reporting to and/or associated with will not qualify for the referral fee to prevent biased decision-making and to ensure that the hiring process remains fair and objective.\nEmployees involved in the recruitment process: any employee directly involved in the recruitment process, such as participating in the candidate selection or interview panels, will not qualify for the referral fee. This helps ensure objectivity and prevents any biased decision-making.\nGENERAL GUIDELINES\nNo Guarantee: Submission of a candidate for consideration of a vacant position does not guarantee that they will be appointed/hired by Awesim.\nCommunication: all communication with the referred candidate will be done directly with the candidate. The employee referring the candidate will not be included in this communication. Should the candidate be successfully appointed in the vacant position, HR will communicate this to the employee who referred the candidate.\nPOLICY COMPLIANCE\nFailure to comply with the Recruitment Referral Fee Policy may result in disqualification from the referral program.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"718f9cb7-12df-43ed-89c8-0a7098fff6f7\",\"title\":\"Recruitment Referral Fee Policy \",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd has implemented this recruitment referral fee policy & procedure to incentivise and reward employees for actively participating in the recruitment process by referring qualified candidates. This policy encourages employees to tap into their networks and recommend individuals who possess the skills, experience, and cultural fit necessary for the position advertised & for Awesim’s success overall.

By offering a financial incentive, such as a referral fee, the company promotes employee engagement and involvement in talent acquisition, leading to a broader candidate pool and increased chances of hiring top-notch personnel.

Furthermore, this policy fosters a culture of collaboration, as employees become more invested in the success of their colleagues and the overall growth of Awesim.

All full-time employees of Awesim are eligible to participate in the recruitment referral fee program, subject to the terms and conditions outlined in this policy & procedure.

DEFINITIONS

  1. \\\"Referral\\\" refers to the act of recommending or suggesting a candidate for a specific job vacancy advertised within Awesim that qualifies.
  2. \\\"Referral Fee\\\" is a monetary reward or incentive provided to the employee for successfully referring a candidate who is subsequently hired by Awesim and successfully completes their probationary period and any extensions thereafter.
  3. \\\"Referring Employee\\\" refers to an individual who works for Awesim and recommends/sends the CV and details of potential candidate/s for qualifying vacant positions within Awesim. This refers to an existing Awesim employee already working for the company. May be referred to as “Awesim employee”.
  4. \\\"Referred candidate\\\" refers to an external individual who has been recommended or referred for a position vacancy by the referring employee.
  5. \\\"Probationary period\\\" refers to a specific duration of time during which an employee's performance and suitability for a job & fit within Awesim are evaluated.

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA FOR REFERRALS

To be eligible for the referral fee, the referred candidate must meet the following criteria:

  1. The employee referring the candidate must complete the Candidate Referral Motivation slack workflow before the deadline date stipulated on the advert. This workflow can be found on the #general channel in slack.
  2. The candidate must be an external candidate/individual who is not currently employed by Awesim.
  3. The CV must meet the necessary requirements of the position, including: education, skills, experience, etc.
  4. The referred candidate must be hired within the position that they were referred for and if that vacant position qualifies for the referral fee.
  5. The candidate must successfully complete their probationary period and any other extension periods that may be applied as determined by Awesim in order to qualify for the referral fee.

REFERRAL PROCESS

To be eligible for the referral fee, the Awesim employee must ensure that:

  1. They complete the Candidate Referral Motivation slack workflow. Please note: Applications received after the deadline date stipulated in the Job Advert, will not qualify for the referral fee.
  2. The referred candidate must complete the Wamly video interview as part of the application process.
  3. Within the form in the Wamly submission, the external candidate/individual must state the name and surname of the Awesim employee who referred them.  Please note: If the Awesim Employee name & surname is not included in the candidate's Wamly form submission, the employee will not qualify for the referral fee.
  4. The referred candidate must be aware of and have given the Awesim employee consent to share his/her details for consideration.
  5. The referred candidate must not have been previously referred by another Awesim employee for the same position.
  6. The Awesim employee may be required to participate in the recruitment process, such as providing additional information or participating in an interview, if deemed necessary by the hiring team.

Note: If candidates or Awesim employees do not follow the above process, applications will unfortunately not be considered for the referral fee.

REFERRAL FEE AMOUNT

Should all criteria within this policy/procedure be met, the referring employee will be eligible to receive a referral fee of R1 000.00 (One thousand rand) for each successful appointment/placement.

PAYMENT PROCESS

Referral fees will only be paid to the Awesim employee once the referred candidate has successfully completed their probationary period and any other extension periods that may apply as determined by Awesim.

Should the referred candidate successfully complete their probationary period, the referral fee will be paid via EFT into the Awesim employees bank account in the month following the probationary period completion period with the relevant payroll run.

EMPLOYEE EXCLUSIONS / DISQUALIYING CRITERIA

  1. Human Resources Staff: HR personnel are excluded as a result of their involvement and responsibilities within the recruitment process. This prevents any conflict of interest, as well as biased decision-making.
  2. Executives: Executive team members are excluded as a result of their involvement in the final appointment decision of all positions within Awesim. This prevents any conflict of interest, as well as biased decision-making.
  3. Management: Department Managers, Regional Team Leaders and other Line Manager for which the position will be reporting to and/or associated with will not qualify for the referral fee to prevent biased decision-making and to ensure that the hiring process remains fair and objective.
  4. Employees involved in the recruitment process: any employee directly involved in the recruitment process, such as participating in the candidate selection or interview panels, will not qualify for the referral fee. This helps ensure objectivity and prevents any biased decision-making.

GENERAL GUIDELINES

  1. No Guarantee: Submission of a candidate for consideration of a vacant position does not guarantee that they will be appointed/hired by Awesim.
  2. Communication: all communication with the referred candidate will be done directly with the candidate. The employee referring the candidate will not be included in this communication. Should the candidate be successfully appointed in the vacant position, HR will communicate this to the employee who referred the candidate.

POLICY COMPLIANCE

Failure to comply with the Recruitment Referral Fee Policy may result in disqualification from the referral program.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "policy", "id": "4b4e383d-ef16-43ea-8781-9877bf0601e9", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "recruitment", "id": "dd1072f2-f252-43d2-8aa8-81fc25402f07", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "referral fee", "id": "b64b02e4-a76b-4efa-8254-b6f899d897ba", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-03-17T08:09:34.686+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/718f9cb7-12df-43ed-89c8-0a7098fff6f7" }, { "id": "76487597-3569-4854-8921-cd1205e7da85", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"76487597-3569-4854-8921-cd1205e7da85\",\"title\":\"Buy Reward Points - Awesim App\",\"content\":\"\nLegend\nRP / RPs\nReward Points\nWhat are Reward Points?\nThis is an enhanced incentive program provided to Awesim agents, enabling them to purchase Reward Points at rand value. These points can then be used to redeem various items (sims) or rewards in the Rewards Store.\nThere are four methods of purchasing RPs on the Awesim App:\nawesim App Standard login procedure & Navigation to Reward points purchase\nLog into the app using the Awesim RICA registered cell phone number and password.\nClick on Your Rewards option.\nClick on Buy Points option.\nEnter the number of Reward Points you wish to purchase. (Note: 1 Reward Point = R1, and the minimum order is 20 points.)\nCredit/Debit Card\nBelow is how an agent can buy RPs using credit/Debit card and receive RPs instantly:\nChoose the Credit or Debit Card payment method.\nEnter the card details and click on Pay now.\nAuthorize the transaction by logging on to your banking banking app and confirming OTP.\nConfirm the purchase by approving the payment.\n3.1. 'PAYMENT FAILED' & TRANSACTION UNSUCCESSUL means the banking OTP was not successfully confirmed.\n3.1.1 Ensure that the banking app setting allows internet transactions and try again.\nAfter confirmation, the payment will be processed successfully.\nThe updated amount will be reflected under Rewards Points balance.\nThe agent will see the successful transaction update in your Rewards History, including the date, reason, reward point amount purchased, and the updated balance.\nSmartload\nBelow is how an agent can buy RPs using Smartload and receive RPs instantly:\nSelect Smartload as the payment method.\n1.1 Agents must use their main smartload payment number.\nThe agent will dial USSD *130\n*\n678*150#\nAn OTP pin landing page will be prompted on screen requesting the agent to dial the USSD string *130\n*\n678*150# and enter the pin.\nThe agent will navigate to the Awesim App, and enter the OTP.\nThe agent will purchase the reward points of up to a minimum of 20 points.\nA RPs landing page will be prompted on screen confirming the purchase.\nThe Rewards balance and history will be updated and reflect the recent purchase.\nEFT\nBelow is how an agent can buy RPs using EFT payment and receive RPs within 2 to 3 working days:\nSelect EFT as the payment method.\n1.1 Agents must take note of the banking details and read the T&Cs.\nAgents will log into their banking app or make use of internet banking and register Awesim Telecoms as a beneficiary with all the details mentioned in step 1.1.\nThe finance unit will track and allocate the received funds as per the Awesim buying points EFT process.\nCash\nBelow is how an agent can buy RPs using cash payment and receive RPs within 2 to 3 working days:\nSelect cash as the payment method.\n1.1 Agents must take note of the banking details and read the T&Cs.\nAgents will access a deposit ATM machine or Bank branch and make a cash deposit with all the details mentioned in step 1.1.\nThe finance unit will track and allocate the received funds as per the Awesim buying points cash process.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"76487597-3569-4854-8921-cd1205e7da85\",\"title\":\"Buy Reward Points - Awesim App\",\"content\":\"

Legend

RP / RPs

Reward Points

What are Reward Points?

This is an enhanced incentive program provided to Awesim agents, enabling them to purchase Reward Points at rand value. These points can then be used to redeem various items (sims) or rewards in the Rewards Store.

There are four methods of purchasing RPs on the Awesim App:

awesim App Standard login procedure & Navigation to Reward points purchase

  1. Log into the app using the Awesim RICA registered cell phone number and password.

  1. Click on Your Rewards option.

  1. Click on Buy Points option.

  1. Enter the number of Reward Points you wish to purchase. (Note: 1 Reward Point = R1, and the minimum order is 20 points.)

Credit/Debit Card

    • Below is how an agent can buy RPs using credit/Debit card and receive RPs instantly:
  1. Choose the Credit or Debit Card payment method.

  1. Enter the card details and click on Pay now.
    1. Authorize the transaction by logging on to your banking banking app and confirming OTP.

  1. Confirm the purchase by approving the payment.

3.1. 'PAYMENT FAILED' & TRANSACTION UNSUCCESSUL means the banking OTP was not successfully confirmed.

3.1.1 Ensure that the banking app setting allows internet transactions and try again.

  1. After confirmation, the payment will be processed successfully.

  1. The updated amount will be reflected under Rewards Points balance.

  1. The agent will see the successful transaction update in your Rewards History, including the date, reason, reward point amount purchased, and the updated balance.

Smartload

    • Below is how an agent can buy RPs using Smartload and receive RPs instantly:
  1. Select Smartload as the payment method.

1.1 Agents must use their main smartload payment number.

  1. The agent will dial USSD *130*678*150#

  1. An OTP pin landing page will be prompted on screen requesting the agent to dial the USSD string *130*678*150# and enter the pin.

  1. The agent will navigate to the Awesim App, and enter the OTP.

  1. The agent will purchase the reward points of up to a minimum of 20 points.

  1. A RPs landing page will be prompted on screen confirming the purchase.

  1. The Rewards balance and history will be updated and reflect the recent purchase.

EFT

    • Below is how an agent can buy RPs using EFT payment and receive RPs within 2 to 3 working days:
  1. Select EFT as the payment method.

1.1 Agents must take note of the banking details and read the T&Cs.

  1. Agents will log into their banking app or make use of internet banking and register Awesim Telecoms as a beneficiary with all the details mentioned in step 1.1.
  2. The finance unit will track and allocate the received funds as per the Awesim buying points EFT process.

Cash

    • Below is how an agent can buy RPs using cash payment and receive RPs within 2 to 3 working days:
  1. Select cash as the payment method.

1.1 Agents must take note of the banking details and read the T&Cs.

  1. Agents will access a deposit ATM machine or Bank branch and make a cash deposit with all the details mentioned in step 1.1.
  2. The finance unit will track and allocate the received funds as per the Awesim buying points cash process.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2025-03-13T06:52:01.759+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/76487597-3569-4854-8921-cd1205e7da85" }, { "id": "da0a5a63-f7e0-4364-aa38-7b7261289644", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"da0a5a63-f7e0-4364-aa38-7b7261289644\",\"title\":\"Delivery Address - Home or Work\",\"content\":\"\nPlease respond if we must deliver to your home or work address?\nYou must be available to take delivery between 08h00 and 17h00 during working days (Monday to Friday). Our courier company, RAM does not deliver on Public Holiday(s).\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"da0a5a63-f7e0-4364-aa38-7b7261289644\",\"title\":\"Delivery Address - Home or Work\",\"content\":\"

Please respond if we must deliver to your home or work address?

You must be available to take delivery between 08h00 and 17h00 during working days (Monday to Friday). Our courier company, RAM does not deliver on Public Holiday(s).

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "Address", "id": "357714c4-95dd-41a0-b0da-83a6b23daee8", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "delivery", "id": "379ef994-09e5-4513-968d-9bc434c836d5", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-02-26T07:09:13.652+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/da0a5a63-f7e0-4364-aa38-7b7261289644" }, { "id": "f5d146ec-7e19-42f1-bdc5-09efc8a67ba9", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"f5d146ec-7e19-42f1-bdc5-09efc8a67ba9\",\"title\":\"SalesPerson List by Area and Line Manager (LM) SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThe following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.\nPURPOSE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) provides viewer access to a list of salespersons, their codes, line managers (RTL), and the areas they cover.\nIt supports efficient handling of agent queries by location and ensures accurate 2nd level escalation routing using the salesperson field in the CMS Customer Info Table.\nLINK & USER ACCESS\nGoogle Sheet\nLink\nIf you do not have user access, please contact your Line Manager to request access.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"f5d146ec-7e19-42f1-bdc5-09efc8a67ba9\",\"title\":\"SalesPerson List by Area and Line Manager (LM) SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

The following is a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that must be followed as of the date of issue. Non-compliance to this document and/or instruction may result in disciplinary action. Should you have any queries in regard to this SOP, you are required to address same with management immediately.

PURPOSE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) provides viewer access to a list of salespersons, their codes, line managers (RTL), and the areas they cover.

It supports efficient handling of agent queries by location and ensures accurate 2nd level escalation routing using the salesperson field in the CMS Customer Info Table.


LINK & USER ACCESS

Google Sheet Link

If you do not have user access, please contact your Line Manager to request access.



\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "list", "id": "5a1a9644-a726-4a24-bff2-91937ef6d78a", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "salesperson", "id": "f26982b2-09b6-4e1e-aac3-6383813b50a7", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-02-21T08:16:28.553+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/f5d146ec-7e19-42f1-bdc5-09efc8a67ba9" }, { "id": "93267b3b-dc11-4f4b-a5c1-9e513951bbf1", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"93267b3b-dc11-4f4b-a5c1-9e513951bbf1\",\"title\":\"Verification Documents (On Hold) \",\"content\":\"\nIn order for us to reactivate your account and place an order for you, please send us the following docs for verification:\nSee\nReturning Agent (RA) Verification Documents\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"93267b3b-dc11-4f4b-a5c1-9e513951bbf1\",\"title\":\"Verification Documents (On Hold) \",\"content\":\"

In order for us to reactivate your account and place an order for you, please send us the following docs for verification:

See Returning Agent (RA) Verification Documents

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "On Hold", "id": "4797f3a2-73ab-4aaf-acd7-688a7f1497eb", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "Verification", "id": "83e081f5-8f31-4aab-aa4a-9d27c81a1deb", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-02-18T05:51:42.301+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/93267b3b-dc11-4f4b-a5c1-9e513951bbf1" }, { "id": "7725674d-fe38-49e5-a9c3-7aa3de2418fd", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"7725674d-fe38-49e5-a9c3-7aa3de2418fd\",\"title\":\"Returning Agent (RA) Verification Documents \",\"content\":\"\nIs agent already Verified - see CMS Customer Verified field?\nCustmer Verified: No\nIn order for us to place an order for you, we need to verify your account as it has been over a year since we last sent you sim cards.\nPlease send us the following docs for verification:\nA clear photo of your ID / Passport (please make sure that the important details, is clear and readable)\nA picture of you holding your ID / Passport next to your face.\nCustmer Verified:\nYes [Green Tick]\nIn order for us to place an order for you, we need to reverify your account as it has been over a year since we last sent you sim cards.\nPlease send us the following docs for verification:\nA picture of you holding your ID / Passport next to your face.\nExample:\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"7725674d-fe38-49e5-a9c3-7aa3de2418fd\",\"title\":\"Returning Agent (RA) Verification Documents \",\"content\":\"

Is agent already Verified - see CMS Customer Verified field?

Custmer Verified: No

In order for us to place an order for you, we need to verify your account as it has been over a year since we last sent you sim cards.

Please send us the following docs for verification:

  • A clear photo of your ID / Passport (please make sure that the important details, is clear and readable)
  • A picture of you holding your ID / Passport next to your face.


Custmer Verified: Yes [Green Tick]


In order for us to place an order for you, we need to reverify your account as it has been over a year since we last sent you sim cards.

Please send us the following docs for verification:

  • A picture of you holding your ID / Passport next to your face.



Example:



\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "ID Docs", "id": "2c8a29d1-0af9-4307-9625-fe5b6d25daba", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "Lost Agent", "id": "73f89280-9f43-4951-bcf4-71003ee610ad", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "photo", "id": "e2dd4ca3-d171-423e-8349-6529d51ccfab", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "Returning Agent", "id": "59c33176-575e-4fbb-9111-ba453c1dc8b9", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "Verification", "id": "83e081f5-8f31-4aab-aa4a-9d27c81a1deb", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-02-18T05:50:01.021+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/7725674d-fe38-49e5-a9c3-7aa3de2418fd" }, { "id": "554c940b-2c84-43ed-b37a-cbd013c976b5", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"554c940b-2c84-43ed-b37a-cbd013c976b5\",\"title\":\"Awesim MTN Social Starter\",\"content\":\"\nAwesim MTN Social Starters are now available!!\nWhat is on the Social sim?\nSims come with FREE value of\n1GB\nAnytime Data +\n1GB\nWhatsApp Data +\n10\nAnytime minutes\nvalid for 7 days\nSims can get the same bundle at anytime by dialing *142# / Combo / Weekly to get:\nR 20:\nsame bundle as above\nR 10:\n1GB\nWhatsApp Data +\n10\nAnytime minutes\nvalid for 7 days\nHow do I get the sim?\nSims will only be available to agents who are performing on normal MTN sims.\nSims will be issued as prizes to qualifying agents, this means:\nYou will receive them in their order over and above their normal MTN sims.\nIf you use your first allocation of sims properly they will start qualifying for more and can order them as usual.\nIf you waste your first allocation (use the free value & nothing else)\nthe agent will not receive again.\nPlease note:\nthat these Sims can only be distributed in Gauteng / Mpumalanga / Limpopo for now (we are working on getting this for the rest of the country).\nHow much\nI make?\nYou can sell the sims for up to R20.\nThese Sims still earn R30 when they activate like normal MTN sims\nSims still count towards Month End Bonuses like normal MTN sims\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"554c940b-2c84-43ed-b37a-cbd013c976b5\",\"title\":\"Awesim MTN Social Starter\",\"content\":\"

Awesim MTN Social Starters are now available!!

What is on the Social sim?

  • Sims come with FREE value of
    • 1GB Anytime Data +
    • 1GB WhatsApp Data +
    • 10 Anytime minutes
    • valid for 7 days
  • Sims can get the same bundle at anytime by dialing *142# / Combo / Weekly to get:
    • R 20:
      • same bundle as above
    • R 10:
      • 1GB  WhatsApp Data +
      • 10 Anytime minutes
      • valid for 7 days

How do I get the sim?

  • Sims will only be available to agents who are performing on normal MTN sims.
  • Sims will be issued as prizes to qualifying agents, this means:
    • You will receive them in their order over and above their normal MTN sims.
    • If you use your first allocation of sims properly they will start qualifying for more and can order them as usual.
    • If you waste your first allocation (use the free value & nothing else) the agent will not receive again.
  • Please note: that these Sims can only be distributed in Gauteng / Mpumalanga / Limpopo for now (we are working on getting this for the rest of the country).

How much I make?

  • You can sell the sims for up to R20.
  • These Sims still earn R30 when they activate like normal MTN sims
  • Sims still count towards Month End Bonuses like normal MTN sims

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "mtn social", "id": "9f9f6a05-d3cc-4b55-a640-a30397c5dceb", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-02-17T06:44:22.914+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/554c940b-2c84-43ed-b37a-cbd013c976b5" }, { "id": "b994b98f-4629-4f51-a45e-f82cec28d20d", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"b994b98f-4629-4f51-a45e-f82cec28d20d\",\"title\":\"MTN Sim Issue(s): Call Barred Error - Escalation Process \",\"content\":\"\nWhen an agent raises an issue(s) - please ask the agent to action the below checks\nbefore\nwe escalate with feedback.\nQuestion(s):\nDid you RICA the sim before inserting sim into the phone?\nCheck if they have received the number via SMS (we need to know this)\nSwitch the device off and on again\nTry the sim in a different device (preferably a smartphone) - capture what device and model was used? Example: Nokia 1 Plus\nOnce all above checks done and still experiencing issues then:-\nPlease advise the agent to\nnot\nthrow the sim(s) away, and we will confirm how to return the problematic sim(s).\nEscalation Process:\nRequest Sim Serial(s) and photo of Sim(s)\nand Screenshot of Error\nCapture responses to above questions in CMS Service Request (SR) and\nComplete #rica_issues workflow\nEscalate Service Request (SR) for Attention: John (Sales Manager)\nThe above detailed feedback, will help us in getting closer to resolving the issue with MTN.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"b994b98f-4629-4f51-a45e-f82cec28d20d\",\"title\":\"MTN Sim Issue(s): Call Barred Error - Escalation Process \",\"content\":\"

When an agent raises an issue(s) - please ask the agent to action the below checks before we escalate with feedback.

Question(s):

  1. Did you RICA the sim before inserting sim into the phone?
  2. Check if they have received the number via SMS (we need to know this)
  3. Switch the device off and on again
  4. Try the sim in a different device (preferably a smartphone) - capture what device and model was used? Example: Nokia 1 Plus

Once all above checks done and still experiencing issues then:-

Please advise the agent to not throw the sim(s) away, and we will confirm how to return the problematic sim(s).

Escalation Process:

  1. Request Sim Serial(s) and photo of Sim(s)
  2. and Screenshot of Error
  3. Capture responses to above questions in CMS Service Request (SR) and
  4. Complete #rica_issues workflow
  5. Escalate Service Request (SR) for Attention: John (Sales Manager)


The above detailed feedback, will help us in getting closer to resolving the issue with MTN.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "mtn", "id": "ba41049a-0143-41e7-b34a-775e3c0661f7", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "sim error", "id": "5cdf9164-457c-473a-866c-370ab45d1fbf", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-01-17T06:47:08.326+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/b994b98f-4629-4f51-a45e-f82cec28d20d" }, { "id": "4a22ca4d-6a45-46da-8655-c725a19be942", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"4a22ca4d-6a45-46da-8655-c725a19be942\",\"title\":\"New Agent Courtesy SRs - Training \",\"content\":\"\nNote:\nAfter 2 x Unsuccessful Attempts CMS will prompt the User to initiate a Chat - see [Unreachable WhatsApp]\nIn addition, the System will send [Unreachable SMS]\nNEW AGENT - DAY AFTER DELIVERY\nSMS\nAWESIM: Welcome to our TEAM! Dial *130*7422# to RICA Real Customers. *130*678# to Recharge their sims. Awesim Pays Best! Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666\nNEW AGENT - WEEK AFTER DELIVERY CALL\nNEW AGENT - WEEK AFTER DELIVERY CALL –\n1) Advise on the activity you can see the Agent has achieved on their sims so far (RICA right, Connections, Recharges?)\n2) Explain – what is an Activation?\n3) Remind them how we pay: for Activations, on the 25th of the month AFTER the Activation(s) occur\n4) Questions?\n[Unreachable WhatsApp]\nAWESIM: We tried to contact you twice. Contact us: SMS 43203 or WhatsApp 0600702666 Order: Awesim App\nhttps://tinyurl.com/awesimapp\nor dial *130*43203#\nand [Unreachable SMS]\nAWESIM: Rica, Connect and Recharge your sims to Real Customers to get Activations. Activations are paid on the 25th of the month AFTER. Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666\nNEW AGENT - 2 WEEKS AFTER DELIVERY\nSMS\nAWESIM: Register on my.awesim.co.za or dial our USSD *130*43203# to view your reports. Awesim Pays Best! Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666\nNEW AGENT -\nMONTH AFTER DELIVERY CALL\nNEW AGENT - MONTH AFTER DELIVERY CALL –\n1) Advise on the activity you can see the Agent has achieved on their sims so far (RICA right, Connections, Recharges?)\n2) Explain – what is an Activation?\n3) Remind them how we pay: for Activations, on the 25th of the month AFTER the Activation(s) occur\n4) Is the Agent registered on my.awesim? (Self Service Portal Benefits)\n5) If the Agent hasn’t already ordered, and qualifies, encourage a FastTrack Order (INFO SMS) or capture one for them (Allowed Balance >30)\n6) If the Agent does not qualify (Allowed Balance between 21 – 29), offer BuyStock option\n7) Questions?\n[Unreachable WhatsApp]\nAWESIM: We tried to contact you twice. Contact us: SMS 43203 or WhatsApp 0600702666 Order: Awesim App\nhttps://tinyurl.com/awesimapp\nor dial *130*43203#\n[Unreachable SMS]\nAWESIM: Check if you qualify for your next sim order on my.awesim.co.za App or dial our USSD *130*43203# Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"4a22ca4d-6a45-46da-8655-c725a19be942\",\"title\":\"New Agent Courtesy SRs - Training \",\"content\":\"

Note:

  • After 2 x Unsuccessful Attempts CMS will prompt the User to initiate a Chat - see [Unreachable WhatsApp]
  • In addition, the System will send [Unreachable SMS]

  1. NEW AGENT - DAY AFTER DELIVERY SMS
AWESIM: Welcome to our TEAM! Dial *130*7422# to RICA Real Customers. *130*678# to Recharge their sims. Awesim Pays Best! Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666

  1. NEW AGENT - WEEK AFTER DELIVERY CALL
NEW AGENT - WEEK AFTER DELIVERY CALL –
1) Advise on the activity you can see the Agent has achieved on their sims so far (RICA right, Connections, Recharges?)
2) Explain – what is an Activation?  
3) Remind them how we pay: for Activations, on the 25th of the month AFTER the Activation(s) occur
4) Questions?


[Unreachable WhatsApp]

AWESIM: We tried to contact you twice. Contact us: SMS 43203 or WhatsApp 0600702666 Order: Awesim App 
https://tinyurl.com/awesimapp or dial *130*43203#

and [Unreachable SMS]

AWESIM: Rica, Connect and Recharge your sims to Real Customers to get Activations. Activations are paid on the 25th of the month AFTER. Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666

  1. NEW AGENT - 2 WEEKS AFTER DELIVERY SMS
AWESIM: Register on my.awesim.co.za or dial our USSD *130*43203# to view your reports. Awesim Pays Best! Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666

  1. NEW AGENT - MONTH AFTER DELIVERY CALL
NEW AGENT - MONTH AFTER DELIVERY CALL –
1) Advise on the activity you can see the Agent has achieved on their sims so far (RICA right, Connections, Recharges?)
2) Explain – what is an Activation? 
3) Remind them how we pay: for Activations, on the 25th of the month AFTER the Activation(s) occur
4) Is the Agent registered on my.awesim? (Self Service Portal Benefits)
5) If the Agent hasn’t already ordered, and qualifies, encourage a FastTrack Order (INFO SMS) or capture one for them (Allowed Balance >30)
6) If the Agent does not qualify (Allowed Balance between 21 – 29), offer BuyStock option
7) Questions?


[Unreachable WhatsApp] AWESIM: We tried to contact you twice. Contact us: SMS 43203 or WhatsApp 0600702666 Order: Awesim App 
https://tinyurl.com/awesimapp or dial *130*43203#

[Unreachable SMS]

AWESIM: Check if you qualify for your next sim order on my.awesim.co.za App or dial our USSD *130*43203# Questions? WhatsApp 0600702666

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "courtesy", "id": "b1786853-1362-4b19-a321-6353f458f86a", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "training", "id": "7520481b-31b5-435c-ab0f-99b3c154ce15", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2025-01-16T13:52:15.196+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/4a22ca4d-6a45-46da-8655-c725a19be942" }, { "id": "51505314-82aa-4ade-83bf-9ae3bea283f0", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"51505314-82aa-4ade-83bf-9ae3bea283f0\",\"title\":\"Telkom Sim Serial(s) Box \\\"Kitting\\\" Error - Escalation Process \",\"content\":\"\nError:\nThe scanned Telkom SIM box containing 250 SIM cards does not match the SIM serial numbers recorded on file for that box. As a result, when an agent uses the \\\"Check SIM Payment\\\" function, the SIM serials are not correctly invoiced to the customer code.\nWhen an agent raises an issue - please ask the agent to share the below details before we escalate with feedback.\nAgent Questions:-\nRequest a clear photo of the\nTelkom SIM Box (250) with Label\nand;\nPhoto of 1 x Telkom SIM only! with Serial Number from the\nsame Box\n(\n1 x SIM Only! to avoid any confusion\n)\nPlease advise the agent to not discard of the sim(s), and we will confirm once the SIM Serial(s) are Invoiced correctly to the agent's account.\nEscalation Process:\nSR Comment: Att Jo - Telkom Kitting Error\nDropDown: ESCALATE and /or TO CC SUP (RTL to assist with Routing)\nThe above detailed feedback, will help us in getting closer to resolving the issue with Telkom Kitting & Shipments.\nCMS CORRECTION:\nRefer to the example below to see how the correction will be displayed in CMS:\nAdhox Invoice\n\\\"Telkom stock issue fix Dec 2024)\nService Request (SR) Invoiced and Closed\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"51505314-82aa-4ade-83bf-9ae3bea283f0\",\"title\":\"Telkom Sim Serial(s) Box \\\"Kitting\\\" Error - Escalation Process \",\"content\":\"

Error:

The scanned Telkom SIM box containing 250 SIM cards does not match the SIM serial numbers recorded on file for that box. As a result, when an agent uses the \\\"Check SIM Payment\\\" function, the SIM serials are not correctly invoiced to the customer code.


When an agent raises an issue - please ask the agent to share the below details before we escalate with feedback.

Agent Questions:-

  1. Request a clear photo of the Telkom SIM Box (250) with Label and;
  2. Photo of 1 x Telkom SIM only! with Serial Number from the same Box (1 x SIM Only! to avoid any confusion)

Please advise the agent to not discard of the sim(s), and we will confirm once the SIM Serial(s) are Invoiced correctly to the agent's account.


Escalation Process:

SR Comment: Att Jo - Telkom Kitting Error
DropDown: ESCALATE and /or TO CC SUP (RTL to assist with Routing)

The above detailed feedback, will help us in getting closer to resolving the issue with Telkom Kitting & Shipments.


CMS CORRECTION:

Refer to the example below to see how the correction will be displayed in CMS:

  • Adhox Invoice
  • \\\"Telkom stock issue fix Dec 2024)
  • Service Request (SR) Invoiced and Closed



\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "error", "id": "c602a613-d788-42a1-aab4-1a07d18f8baa", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "Telkom", "id": "56efb4ed-0f90-4d14-b2fd-cb730b3d2b9a", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-18T11:26:13.343+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/51505314-82aa-4ade-83bf-9ae3bea283f0" }, { "id": "b9951356-e35b-4f2b-9f6b-70a97a63158d", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"b9951356-e35b-4f2b-9f6b-70a97a63158d\",\"title\":\"Work address \",\"content\":\"\nPlease send your delivery address in the following format:\nBuilding or Shop Name\nStreet Address (House / Stand Number and Street Name):\nSuburb (Area):\nCity:\nProvince:\nPostal Code:\nNearest Landmark within walking distance (e.g. School, Clinic, Police Station or Mall):\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"b9951356-e35b-4f2b-9f6b-70a97a63158d\",\"title\":\"Work address \",\"content\":\"

Please send your delivery address in the following format:

  • Building or Shop Name
  • Street Address (House / Stand Number and Street Name):
  • Suburb (Area):
  • City:
  • Province:
  • Postal Code:
  • Nearest Landmark within walking distance (e.g. School, Clinic, Police Station or Mall):

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2024-12-17T08:00:27.508+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/b9951356-e35b-4f2b-9f6b-70a97a63158d" }, { "id": "5a5263ea-49ed-42d6-b715-a7dc6fc6516d", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"5a5263ea-49ed-42d6-b715-a7dc6fc6516d\",\"title\":\"Home address \",\"content\":\"\nPlease send your delivery address in the following format:\nStreet Address (House / Stand Number and Street Name):\nSuburb (Area):\nCity:\nProvince:\nPostal Code:\nNearest Landmark within walking distance (e.g. School, Clinic, Police Station or Mall):\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"5a5263ea-49ed-42d6-b715-a7dc6fc6516d\",\"title\":\"Home address \",\"content\":\"

Please send your delivery address in the following format:

  • Street Address (House / Stand Number and Street Name):
  • Suburb (Area):
  • City:
  • Province:
  • Postal Code:
  • Nearest Landmark within walking distance (e.g. School, Clinic, Police Station or Mall):


\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2024-12-17T07:59:45.482+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/5a5263ea-49ed-42d6-b715-a7dc6fc6516d" }, { "id": "b0cdd597-beb3-4aa9-a6cd-bc55aa87eb7c", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"b0cdd597-beb3-4aa9-a6cd-bc55aa87eb7c\",\"title\":\"Emergency Evacuation Policy \",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nThis policy outlines the procedures to be followed during an emergency evacuation at Awesim Telecoms. The purpose of this policy is to ensure the safety and well-being of all employees, contractors, and visitors in the event of an emergency. This policy outlines procedures for safely evacuating the premises, assigning roles and responsibilities, and ensuring communication during critical situations. By providing a framework for emergency responses, the company aims to minimise risks, reduce panic, and protect lives, while complying with safety regulations and promoting a culture of preparedness within the workplace.\nEMERGENCIES\nEmergency evacuations or procedures may be called for:\nAny emergency that requires all staff to evacuate the building immediately (e.g. fires, flooding, building integrity, etc.\nAny emergency that requires all staff to either evacuate, if safe to do so, or shelter in a hiding place (e.g. robberies, hostages, shooting, etc.).\nEMERGENCY PROCEDURE (Shelter and Hide)\nAn emergency procedure may require you to:\nEvacuate the building – only if safe to do so\nShelter in a hiding place and stay hidden until an emergency coordinator declares it safe to come out.\nIn cases where you have to shelter in place:\nIf evacuation is not possible, go to a safe location and call emergency services;\nLock the door;\nBlock the door with heavy furniture;\nKeep quiet;\nTurn your phone on silent;\nStay clear of windows;\nRemain calm;\nOnly come out when an emergency representative has declared it safe to do so.\nEMERGENCY EVACUATION (Exit the building)\nDo not panic.\nActivate the fire alarm or press the emergency sound horn to signal an evacuation. Notify the relevant HSE Representative or Committee member of the emergency & where is it located as soon as possible.\nEveryone is required to evacuate the building immediately using their closest evacuation exit. Consider alternative evacuation routes before an emergency.\nWalk, do\nnot run.\nClose all windows, if safe to do so.\nLast person to leave the office area (inside the building) to close the door behind them (e.g. Finance office, sales admin office, HR Office, etc.)\nDo\nnot\ncollect your personal belongings before exiting the building.\nDo\nnot\ntry and move your vehicle out of the parking area or basement.\nEveryone is required to meet at the assembly point and stand with their colleagues within their department (CC, IT, Finance, etc.).\nHead of Department is required to take a head count of all their department staff at the assembly point. Head count and missing staff to be reported to the HSE Representative.\nWait at the assembly point until the HSE Representative or Emergency Service Representative has declared it safe to enter the building or have advised on the next steps.\nEVACUATION DRILLS\nAwesim is required to implement evacuation drills 2 X a year.\nThese drills are used as training to prepare employees on what to do in the event there is areal emergency that requires evacuation.\nStaff will not be aware of the date or time of evacuation drills, however, all employees must comply and follow the evacuation procedure when they occur.\nAny concerns or findings that arise from the evacuation drills, will be address and training will be initiated to mitigate these in a real emergency situation.\nPlease be patient and cooperate with the HSE Committee during these drills.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"b0cdd597-beb3-4aa9-a6cd-bc55aa87eb7c\",\"title\":\"Emergency Evacuation Policy \",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

This policy outlines the procedures to be followed during an emergency evacuation at Awesim Telecoms. The purpose of this policy is to ensure the safety and well-being of all employees, contractors, and visitors in the event of an emergency. This policy outlines procedures for safely evacuating the premises, assigning roles and responsibilities, and ensuring communication during critical situations. By providing a framework for emergency responses, the company aims to minimise risks, reduce panic, and protect lives, while complying with safety regulations and promoting a culture of preparedness within the workplace.

EMERGENCIES

Emergency evacuations or procedures may be called for:

  1. Any emergency that requires all staff to evacuate the building immediately (e.g. fires, flooding, building integrity, etc.
  2. Any emergency that requires all staff to either evacuate, if safe to do so, or shelter in a hiding place (e.g. robberies, hostages, shooting, etc.).

EMERGENCY PROCEDURE (Shelter and Hide)

  • An emergency procedure may require you to:
    • Evacuate the building – only if safe to do so
    • Shelter in a hiding place and stay hidden until an emergency coordinator declares it safe to come out.
  • In cases where you have to shelter in place:
    • If evacuation is not possible, go to a safe location and call emergency services;
    • Lock the door;
    • Block the door with heavy furniture;
    • Keep quiet;
    • Turn your phone on silent;
    • Stay clear of windows;
    • Remain calm;
    • Only come out when an emergency representative has declared it safe to do so. 

EMERGENCY EVACUATION (Exit the building)

  1. Do not panic.
  2. Activate the fire alarm or press the emergency sound horn to signal an evacuation. Notify the relevant HSE Representative or Committee member of the emergency & where is it located as soon as possible.
  3. Everyone is required to evacuate the building immediately using their closest evacuation exit. Consider alternative evacuation routes before an emergency.
    1. Walk, do not run.
    2. Close all windows, if safe to do so.
    3. Last person to leave the office area (inside the building) to close the door behind them (e.g. Finance office, sales admin office, HR Office, etc.)
    4. Do not collect your personal belongings before exiting the building.
    5. Do not try and move your vehicle out of the parking area or basement.
  4. Everyone is required to meet at the assembly point and stand with their colleagues within their department (CC, IT, Finance, etc.).
  5. Head of Department is required to take a head count of all their department staff at the assembly point. Head count and missing staff to be reported to the HSE Representative.
  6. Wait at the assembly point until the HSE Representative or Emergency Service Representative has declared it safe to enter the building or have advised on the next steps.

EVACUATION DRILLS

  • Awesim is required to implement evacuation drills 2 X a year.
  • These drills are used as training to prepare employees on what to do in the event there is areal emergency that requires evacuation.
  • Staff will not be aware of the date or time of evacuation drills, however, all employees must comply and follow the evacuation procedure when they occur.
  • Any concerns or findings that arise from the evacuation drills, will be address and training will be initiated to mitigate these in a real emergency situation.
  • Please be patient and cooperate with the HSE Committee during these drills.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "emergency", "id": "84a3788b-0f8e-4b65-961d-a449bbf434b5", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "evacuation", "id": "257e203f-9118-462f-b646-18900e9e67cc", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "policy", "id": "4b4e383d-ef16-43ea-8781-9877bf0601e9", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-13T06:26:44.461+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/b0cdd597-beb3-4aa9-a6cd-bc55aa87eb7c" }, { "id": "21e7f4fd-347a-4510-949c-ebc577380f45", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"21e7f4fd-347a-4510-949c-ebc577380f45\",\"title\":\"Year End 2024/25 Ops Schedule \",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.\nPURPOSE\nThe purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to\nCREATIONS\nCreations (HO) - Closed Thurs, 14 December 2023\nCC to send TO CREATE, however inform agent\nStarter Package\nwill only be sent in New Year 2024\nCode Creations will only be actioned on 2 Jan 2024\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"21e7f4fd-347a-4510-949c-ebc577380f45\",\"title\":\"Year End 2024/25 Ops Schedule \",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.

PURPOSE

The purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to




CREATIONS

  • Creations (HO) - Closed Thurs, 14 December 2023
  • CC to send TO CREATE, however inform agent Starter Package will only be sent in New Year 2024
  • Code Creations will only be actioned on 2 Jan 2024








\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [], "last_modified": "2024-12-09T13:52:00.140+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/21e7f4fd-347a-4510-949c-ebc577380f45" }, { "id": "fa0bd9d8-05aa-419b-a6d1-c38680898f82", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"fa0bd9d8-05aa-419b-a6d1-c38680898f82\",\"title\":\"Awesim's Emergency Evacuation Plan\",\"content\":\"\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"fa0bd9d8-05aa-419b-a6d1-c38680898f82\",\"title\":\"Awesim's Emergency Evacuation Plan\",\"content\":\"

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "awesim", "id": "e062ed16-069a-457e-ae83-5775eb16403a", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "evacuation", "id": "257e203f-9118-462f-b646-18900e9e67cc", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "plan", "id": "bb41f908-0682-41a3-9c30-f3c34e6a14f4", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-05T06:12:03.421+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/fa0bd9d8-05aa-419b-a6d1-c38680898f82" }, { "id": "1f9d5120-9934-4ba3-9a3f-4891e377b7b6", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"1f9d5120-9934-4ba3-9a3f-4891e377b7b6\",\"title\":\"Employment Equity Plan\",\"content\":\"\nEMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN FOR THE BUSINESS OF AWESIM TELECOMS (PTY) LTD FOR 01/04/2021 TO 31/03/2026\nDTI Registration Name\nAwesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd\nCompany Registration Number\n2010/001502/07\nPAYE/SARS Number\n7050776139\nUIF Number\nU050776139\nEE Reference Number\n816963\nSDL Reference Number\nL050776139\nIndustry\nTransport, Storage and Communications\nSTATEMENT OF INTENT\nAwesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd, the company, is committed to creating and maintaining an environment that provides equal opportunities to all Awesim employees, with special consideration for historically disadvantaged groups. It will take steps to promote equal opportunity in the workplace by eliminating unfair discrimination in any employment policy or practice.\nWORDS, EXPRESSIONS AND DEFINITIONS\nWords, phrases and expressions used in this plan will have the meanings as defined in the Employment Equity Act, No. 55 of 1998 (hereinafter referred to as “the Act”) unless the context otherwise determines.\nEMPLOYMENT EQUITY COMMITTEE\nThe company is a diverse organisation with employees who work throughout South Africa. To ensure that all employee and employer representatives are sufficiently represented and have the opportunity to be heard on issues raised regarding the implementation of the employment equity plan, an employment equity committee will be established to ensure such diverse representation of all employees across the country.\nThis employment equity committee will have members from the various occupational levels, employees from previously disadvantaged groups and employees not from previously disadvantaged groups.\nThis committee will meet on a minimum of a bi-annual basis to discuss any pertinent information. The members of this committee will be nominated and selected in accordance with the Act. The minutes of these various meetings will be recorded and kept accordingly.\nGUIDING PRINCIPALS\nThe company is committed to striving to achieve its Employment Equity Objectives in a manner which conforms fully with the following guiding principles:\nThe company should do what may be necessary to promote equal opportunity in the workplace by eliminating unfair discrimination in any employment policy or practice.\nEmployment equity initiatives should be integrated with the company’s business strategy.\nEmployment equity must be pursued in a manner that will ensure that existing skills are retained.\nThe company should ensure that the appointment of staff would be based on merit and not on tokenism.\nDecisions taken should be presented to staff in a manner, which will avoid negative perceptions developing.\nThe company should exercise the company’s obligations pursuant to employment equity in the most cost-effective manner.\nThe Company should ensure that the process would be transparent and effectively communicated.\nThe goals that the employment equity committee seek to achieve, will be communicated in order to develop positive perceptions and support for the implementation of employment equity.\nThe activities of the company and the employment equity committee will be such that it complies fully with the provisions of the Employment Equity Act.\nThe company and the employment equity committee will ensure that all employees in the company embrace the task of redressing potential inequalities in the company, and will develop a sense of ownership with regard to this process.\nDecisions should be made in a manner that does not adversely affect current employees’ job security.\nThe company will use their best endeavours’ to promote the internal development of their designated employees where possible rather than recruit designated people from external sources.\nASSIGNED EMPLOYMENT EQUITY MANAGER\nThe company assigns the HR Manager as the employment equity manager. The employment equity manager will take responsibility for monitoring and implementing policies and procedures relating to employment equity, as well as the Employment Equity Act. In addition, the employment equity manager will take reasonable steps to ensure that management perform their duties with regard to employment equity. The company gives the HR Manage the authority and means to perform this function.\nThe assignment of these responsibilities to the appointed employ equity manager does not relieve the company of its obligations regarding employment equity.\nEMPLOYMENT EQUITY POLICY\nThe employment equity manager will adopt an employment equity policy that will assist the company to meet its obligations contain within.\nEMPLOYMENT EQUITY ASSESSMENT AND MONITORING\nThe employment equity committee will monitor the implementation of the plan, as well as policies, practices, procedures and programs in the organisation and assess their effectiveness in the light of fairness, transparency and equity to strive for diversity in the organisation and an organisational culture supportive of this.\nMonitoring and assessment will be carried out in a transparent and participative manner. The employment equity committee will meet at least twice a year and be responsible for the transfer of information to the employees they represent. The employment equity manager will be responsible for keeping Awesim Telecoms Managing Director appraised on employment equity matters and concerns.\nCOMMUNICATION\nThe company will share the principals of the employment equity policy and plan by means of training and awareness sessions. In addition, the company will have available at its workplace a copy of its equity plan and the mot recent report to the Director – General. To fulfill section 25 of the Employment Equity Act, the company will ensure that employees are kept informed on the aims and outcomes of the employment equity plan and that necessary information is available.\nCommunication will be achieved through various initiatives such as, inter alia:\nDiscussions and feedback sessions during the employment equity meetings;\nTraining on the employment equity policy, plan and relevant information;\nEmployment equity representatives will communicate with staff, where applicable, and report directly to the employment equity manager;\nThe employment equity manager will keep the Managing Director appraised;\nSharing of information through informal processes such as posters, informal discussions, informal sessions, etc.;\nThe company will ensure that a summary of the Employment Equity Act is available at the workplace.\nRECRUITMENT\nRecruitment and selection will be done in accordance with the company’s Recruitment and Selection Policy. Recruitment and Selection will be based on fairness, objectivity, having regard for competence and seeking to redress historical imbalances to achieve broad representation as far as possible.\nTRAINING & DEVELOPMENT\nThe company is committed to the development of employees through effective training, skills development and upliftment through either external or internal initiatives.\nEMPLOYMENT EQUITY AND NON-CONFORMANCE\nIn the interest of achieving the objectives of this policy and plan, non-conformance will constitute as misconduct. Non-conforming employees will be counselled and disciplined in terms of the company’s Disciplinary Policy and Procedure.\nINTERNAL DISPUTE RESOLUTION PROCEDURE\nEmployment Equity disputes will be addressed in accordance with the company’s Employment Equity Dispute Resolution Procedure.\nAny employee who is dissatisfied with any matter relating to the company’s obligations regarding employment equity, the employment equity plan or policy, the interpretation or implementation of it or any of the reports submitted to the Director-General should direct the complaint for consideration in accordance with the company’s employment equity dispute resolution procedure.\nAn employee or applicant for employment, who believes that he or she is the subject of unfair discrimination, harassment or victimisation in areas identified by this policy should direct the complaint for determination in accordance with the company’s employment equity dispute resolution procedure. A grievant that believes he or she is a victim of harassment will be entitled to a confidential dispute resolution procedure and will be supported by a person employed by the company of his/her choice. In such circumstances the employee concerned will not be obliged to approach his or her superior first with the grievance if the grievance relates to conduct of such superior.  The company has adopted a sexual harassment policy.\nGrievants’ are assured of the freedom to raise a complaint, without fear of reprisal, based on the reasonable belief that the policy has been contravened.\nAWESIM TELECOMS EMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN (EEA 13)\nThe company understands that apartheid and other historic discriminatory laws and practices created disparities in employment, occupation and income within the national labour market of South Africa. Such disparities created disadvantages for certain categories of people that simply repealing discriminatory laws couldn’t redress them.\nIt is the company’s strategic intent to:\nTo promote the constitutional right of equality and the exercise of true democracy;\nTo eliminate unfair discrimination in employment;\nTo redress the effects of discrimination;\nTo pursue a diverse workforce which is representative of the South African people;\nTo promote economic development and efficiency in the workforce;\nTo redress historical, legal, social and economic discrimination against designated groups;\nTo establish conditions which will further the creation of equality of opportunity for all employees to develop into higher level posts where possible, considering the projected shortfall of adequately trained and experienced staff in the country;\nTo ensure the continued survival, growth and competitiveness of the company in a changing South Africa and world order.\nDURATION OF THE EMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN\nAwesim Telecoms employment equity plan will run for a period of five (5) years (01/04/2021 – 31/03/2026). Prior to the expiry of the plan, the employment equity manager will develop a new plan for the proceeding period upon consultation with the employment equity committee.\nOBJECTIVES OF EMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN\nOBJECTIVES FOR YEAR ONE (01/04/2021 - 31/03/2022)\nEmployment Equity Awareness\nRecruitment committee awareness on employment equity objectives\nEmployment of an additional 1 job position within the Skilled Technical level\nEmployment of an additional 2 job positions within the Semi-skilled level\nOBJECTIVES FOR YEAR TWO (01/04/2022 - 31/03/2023)\nDisability Awareness\nAwesim to provide resources with information regarding Disability to all employees to create awareness and ensure that employees understand the topic and scope of Diversity.\nEmployment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level\nPossible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets\nOBJECTIVES FOR YEAR THREE (01/04/2023 - 31/03/2024)\nHIV & Aids Awareness\nEmployment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level\nPossible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets\nOBJECTIVES FOR YEAR FOUR (01/04/2024 - 31/03/2025)\nEmployment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level\nPossible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets\nPossible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates with disabilities that could potentially be accommodated and/or for which reasonable accommodation could be made\nOBJECTIVES FOR YEAR FIVE (01/04/2025 - 31/03/2026)\nEmployment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level\nPossible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets\nPossible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates with disabilities that could potentially be accommodated and/or for which reasonable accommodation could be made.\nNUMERICAL GOALS\nSNAPSHOT OF THE CURRENT WORKFORCE PROFILE\nThe workforce profile snapshot tables used for the conducting of the analysis to inform this plan are used below as a baseline for the setting of numerical goals and targets for each year of the plan.\nWorkforce profile snapshot date: 18/03/2021\nTable 1: Snapshot of workforce profile for all employees, including people with disabilities\nTable 2: Snapshot for workforce profile for people with disabilities ONLY\nNUMERICAL GOALS\nNumerical Goals: 5 Year Plan\nStart date:             01/04/2021                                                                                          End date: 31/03/2026\nNumerical Goals for all employees, including people with disabilities\nNumerical Goals for people with disabilities ONLY\nNUMERICAL TARGETS\nNumerical targets must include the entire workforce profile, and\nNOT\nthe difference that is projected to be achieved by the next reporting period. Below are two tables on numerical targets, one covering all employees, including people with disabilities, and the other only covers people with disabilities\nONLY\n.\nNumerical targets: Year 1\nStart date:             01/04/2021                                                                                          End date: 31/03/2022\nNumerical targets for all employees, including people with disabilities\nNumerical targets for people with disabilities ONLY\nNumerical targets: Year 2\nStart date:             01/04/2022                                                                                            End date: 31/03/2023\nNumerical targets for all employees, including people with disabilities\nNumerical targets for people with disabilities ONLY\nNumerical targets: Year 3\nStart date:             01/04/2023                                                                                         End date: 31/03/2024\nNumerical targets for all employees, including people with disabilities\nNumerical targets for people with disabilities ONLY\nNumerical targets: Year 4\nStart date:             01/04/2024                                                                                         End date: 31/03/2025\nNumerical targets for all employees, including people with disabilities\nNumerical targets for people with disabilities ONLY\nNumerical targets: Year 5\nStart date:             01/04/2025                                                                                         End date: 31/03/2026\nNumerical targets for all employees, including people with disabilities\nNumerical targets for people with disabilities ONLY\nBARRIERS AND AFFIRMATIVE ACTION\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"1f9d5120-9934-4ba3-9a3f-4891e377b7b6\",\"title\":\"Employment Equity Plan\",\"content\":\"

EMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN FOR THE BUSINESS OF AWESIM TELECOMS (PTY) LTD FOR 01/04/2021 TO 31/03/2026

DTI Registration Name

Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd

Company Registration Number

2010/001502/07

PAYE/SARS Number

7050776139

UIF Number

U050776139

EE Reference Number

816963

SDL Reference Number

L050776139

Industry

Transport, Storage and Communications

STATEMENT OF INTENT

Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd, the company, is committed to creating and maintaining an environment that provides equal opportunities to all Awesim employees, with special consideration for historically disadvantaged groups. It will take steps to promote equal opportunity in the workplace by eliminating unfair discrimination in any employment policy or practice.

WORDS, EXPRESSIONS AND DEFINITIONS

Words, phrases and expressions used in this plan will have the meanings as defined in the Employment Equity Act, No. 55 of 1998 (hereinafter referred to as “the Act”) unless the context otherwise determines.

EMPLOYMENT EQUITY COMMITTEE

The company is a diverse organisation with employees who work throughout South Africa. To ensure that all employee and employer representatives are sufficiently represented and have the opportunity to be heard on issues raised regarding the implementation of the employment equity plan, an employment equity committee will be established to ensure such diverse representation of all employees across the country.

This employment equity committee will have members from the various occupational levels, employees from previously disadvantaged groups and employees not from previously disadvantaged groups.

This committee will meet on a minimum of a bi-annual basis to discuss any pertinent information. The members of this committee will be nominated and selected in accordance with the Act. The minutes of these various meetings will be recorded and kept accordingly.  

GUIDING PRINCIPALS

  • The company is committed to striving to achieve its Employment Equity Objectives in a manner which conforms fully with the following guiding principles:
  • The company should do what may be necessary to promote equal opportunity in the workplace by eliminating unfair discrimination in any employment policy or practice.
  • Employment equity initiatives should be integrated with the company’s business strategy.
  • Employment equity must be pursued in a manner that will ensure that existing skills are retained.
  • The company should ensure that the appointment of staff would be based on merit and not on tokenism.
  • Decisions taken should be presented to staff in a manner, which will avoid negative perceptions developing.
  • The company should exercise the company’s obligations pursuant to employment equity in the most cost-effective manner.
  • The Company should ensure that the process would be transparent and effectively communicated.
  • The goals that the employment equity committee seek to achieve, will be communicated in order to develop positive perceptions and support for the implementation of employment equity.
  • The activities of the company and the employment equity committee will be such that it complies fully with the provisions of the Employment Equity Act.
  • The company and the employment equity committee will ensure that all employees in the company embrace the task of redressing potential inequalities in the company, and will develop a sense of ownership with regard to this process.
  • Decisions should be made in a manner that does not adversely affect current employees’ job security.
  • The company will use their best endeavours’ to promote the internal development of their designated employees where possible rather than recruit designated people from external sources.

ASSIGNED EMPLOYMENT EQUITY MANAGER

The company assigns the HR Manager as the employment equity manager. The employment equity manager will take responsibility for monitoring and implementing policies and procedures relating to employment equity, as well as the Employment Equity Act. In addition, the employment equity manager will take reasonable steps to ensure that management perform their duties with regard to employment equity. The company gives the HR Manage the authority and means to perform this function.

The assignment of these responsibilities to the appointed employ equity manager does not relieve the company of its obligations regarding employment equity.

EMPLOYMENT EQUITY POLICY

The employment equity manager will adopt an employment equity policy that will assist the company to meet its obligations contain within.

EMPLOYMENT EQUITY ASSESSMENT AND MONITORING

The employment equity committee will monitor the implementation of the plan, as well as policies, practices, procedures and programs in the organisation and assess their effectiveness in the light of fairness, transparency and equity to strive for diversity in the organisation and an organisational culture supportive of this.

Monitoring and assessment will be carried out in a transparent and participative manner. The employment equity committee will meet at least twice a year and be responsible for the transfer of information to the employees they represent. The employment equity manager will be responsible for keeping Awesim Telecoms Managing Director appraised on employment equity matters and concerns. 

COMMUNICATION

The company will share the principals of the employment equity policy and plan by means of training and awareness sessions. In addition, the company will have available at its workplace a copy of its equity plan and the mot recent report to the Director – General. To fulfill section 25 of the Employment Equity Act, the company will ensure that employees are kept informed on the aims and outcomes of the employment equity plan and that necessary information is available.

Communication will be achieved through various initiatives such as, inter alia:

  • Discussions and feedback sessions during the employment equity meetings;
  • Training on the employment equity policy, plan and relevant information;
  • Employment equity representatives will communicate with staff, where applicable, and report directly to the employment equity manager;
  • The employment equity manager will keep the Managing Director appraised;
  • Sharing of information through informal processes such as posters, informal discussions, informal sessions, etc.;
  • The company will ensure that a summary of the Employment Equity Act is available at the workplace.

RECRUITMENT

Recruitment and selection will be done in accordance with the company’s Recruitment and Selection Policy. Recruitment and Selection will be based on fairness, objectivity, having regard for competence and seeking to redress historical imbalances to achieve broad representation as far as possible.

TRAINING & DEVELOPMENT

The company is committed to the development of employees through effective training, skills development and upliftment through either external or internal initiatives.

EMPLOYMENT EQUITY AND NON-CONFORMANCE

In the interest of achieving the objectives of this policy and plan, non-conformance will constitute as misconduct. Non-conforming employees will be counselled and disciplined in terms of the company’s Disciplinary Policy and Procedure.

INTERNAL DISPUTE RESOLUTION PROCEDURE

Employment Equity disputes will be addressed in accordance with the company’s Employment Equity Dispute Resolution Procedure.

Any employee who is dissatisfied with any matter relating to the company’s obligations regarding employment equity, the employment equity plan or policy, the interpretation or implementation of it or any of the reports submitted to the Director-General should direct the complaint for consideration in accordance with the company’s employment equity dispute resolution procedure.

An employee or applicant for employment, who believes that he or she is the subject of unfair discrimination, harassment or victimisation in areas identified by this policy should direct the complaint for determination in accordance with the company’s employment equity dispute resolution procedure. A grievant that believes he or she is a victim of harassment will be entitled to a confidential dispute resolution procedure and will be supported by a person employed by the company of his/her choice. In such circumstances the employee concerned will not be obliged to approach his or her superior first with the grievance if the grievance relates to conduct of such superior.  The company has adopted a sexual harassment policy.

Grievants’ are assured of the freedom to raise a complaint, without fear of reprisal, based on the reasonable belief that the policy has been contravened.

AWESIM TELECOMS EMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN (EEA 13)

The company understands that apartheid and other historic discriminatory laws and practices created disparities in employment, occupation and income within the national labour market of South Africa. Such disparities created disadvantages for certain categories of people that simply repealing discriminatory laws couldn’t redress them.

It is the company’s strategic intent to:

  • To promote the constitutional right of equality and the exercise of true democracy;
  • To eliminate unfair discrimination in employment;
  • To redress the effects of discrimination;
  • To pursue a diverse workforce which is representative of the South African people;
  • To promote economic development and efficiency in the workforce;
  • To redress historical, legal, social and economic discrimination against designated groups;
  • To establish conditions which will further the creation of equality of opportunity for all employees to develop into higher level posts where possible, considering the projected shortfall of adequately trained and experienced staff in the country;
  • To ensure the continued survival, growth and competitiveness of the company in a changing South Africa and world order.

DURATION OF THE EMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN

Awesim Telecoms employment equity plan will run for a period of five (5) years (01/04/2021 – 31/03/2026). Prior to the expiry of the plan, the employment equity manager will develop a new plan for the proceeding period upon consultation with the employment equity committee.

OBJECTIVES OF EMPLOYMENT EQUITY PLAN

OBJECTIVES FOR YEAR ONE (01/04/2021 - 31/03/2022)

  • Employment Equity Awareness
  • Recruitment committee awareness on employment equity objectives
  • Employment of an additional 1 job position within the Skilled Technical level
  • Employment of an additional 2 job positions within the Semi-skilled level

OBJECTIVES FOR YEAR TWO (01/04/2022 - 31/03/2023)

  • Disability Awareness
  • Awesim to provide resources with information regarding Disability to all employees to create awareness and ensure that employees understand the topic and scope of Diversity.
  • Employment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level
  • Possible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets

OBJECTIVES FOR YEAR THREE (01/04/2023 - 31/03/2024)

  • HIV & Aids Awareness
  • Employment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level
  • Possible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets

OBJECTIVES FOR YEAR FOUR (01/04/2024 - 31/03/2025)

  • Employment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level
  • Possible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets
  • Possible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates with disabilities that could potentially be accommodated and/or for which reasonable accommodation could be made

OBJECTIVES FOR YEAR FIVE (01/04/2025 - 31/03/2026)

  • Employment of an additional 3 job positions within the Semi-skilled level
  • Possible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates from previously disadvantages groups/ groups aligned to the EE targets
  • Possible use of recruitment facilities to obtain CV’s of possible candidates with disabilities that could potentially be accommodated and/or for which reasonable accommodation could be made.

NUMERICAL GOALS

SNAPSHOT OF THE CURRENT WORKFORCE PROFILE

The workforce profile snapshot tables used for the conducting of the analysis to inform this plan are used below as a baseline for the setting of numerical goals and targets for each year of the plan.

Workforce profile snapshot date: 18/03/2021

Table 1: Snapshot of workforce profile for all employees, including people with disabilities

Table 2: Snapshot for workforce profile for people with disabilities ONLY

NUMERICAL GOALS

Numerical Goals: 5 Year Plan

Start date:             01/04/2021                                                                                          End date: 31/03/2026

Numerical Goals for all employees, including people with disabilities

Numerical Goals for people with disabilities ONLY

NUMERICAL TARGETS

Numerical targets must include the entire workforce profile, and NOT the difference that is projected to be achieved by the next reporting period.
