Below are two tables on numerical targets, one covering all employees, including people with disabilities, and the other only covers people with disabilities ONLY.

Numerical targets: Year 1

Start date:             01/04/2021                                                                                          End date: 31/03/2022

BARRIERS AND AFFIRMATIVE ACTION

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "ee", "id": "c79493d0-5ba6-4076-a22d-6ddefdfbd04c", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "employment equity", "id": "b4b9cc32-d662-41ee-b849-88e61fd1e230", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "plan", "id": "bb41f908-0682-41a3-9c30-f3c34e6a14f4", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-05T06:05:25.134+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/1f9d5120-9934-4ba3-9a3f-4891e377b7b6" }, { "id": "6211f9f4-b4c1-42b7-84c9-02de5da1c216", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"6211f9f4-b4c1-42b7-84c9-02de5da1c216\",\"title\":\"HIRA Matrix\",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nA quick reference guide to Awesims' HIRA Matrix. HIRA stands for Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment. This matrix/document is used to identify possible risks and hazards at Awesim, the risk rating, and actions required in order to try to mitigate or prevent them from occurring.\nHow to use this card:\nDownload the matrix/document:\nHIRA Matrix_HSE_F_005_(09-09-2024)_protected.xlsx\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"6211f9f4-b4c1-42b7-84c9-02de5da1c216\",\"title\":\"HIRA Matrix\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

A quick reference guide to Awesims' HIRA Matrix. HIRA stands for Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment. This matrix/document is used to identify possible risks and hazards at Awesim, the risk rating, and actions required in order to try to mitigate or prevent them from occurring.

How to use this card:

  1. Download the matrix/document: HIRA Matrix_HSE_F_005_(09-09-2024)_protected.xlsx

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "document", "id": "583e2174-53fd-455f-a504-bea399e4749f", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "hira", "id": "44b63e77-c3bf-4a70-b430-b283526f83d5", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "hira matrix", "id": "6dae4fa2-7733-44c8-93b0-a953fa662299", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-05T05:41:57.755+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/6211f9f4-b4c1-42b7-84c9-02de5da1c216" }, { "id": "6ed8da55-2bf1-4cd5-a16f-b71b6dc60d80", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"6ed8da55-2bf1-4cd5-a16f-b71b6dc60d80\",\"title\":\"Remote/Hybrid Working Policy\",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nAwesim have implemented this remote working policy/procedure to allow eligible employees to work from a remote location approved by Awesim. This policy/procedure will also give guidance on the terms, conditions and minimum required standards in this regard.\nEach role in Awesim has differing operational requirements to work either physically present in the office, remotely or a hybrid of the two. These requirements are at the discretion of Awesim which may change from time to time.\nAlthough a certain role within Awesim may qualify for remote/hybrid working, the employee appointed in that role is not guaranteed this benefit nor is it an automatic right. Working remotely/hybrid is a benefit offered by Awesim to eligible employees. Awesim may revoke this benefit from an employee at any time whereby the employee will be required to return to working in the office accordingly.\nShould eligible employees fail to meet or follow the requirements herein or as instructed by management, the employee will no longer be eligible for remote/hybrid working and will be required to return to the office to carry out their duties accordingly. In addition, possible disciplinary action may be implemented.\nBy working remotely/hybrid means that you agree to abide by the provisions herein. Working remotely/hybrid does not alter your duties, obligations, responsibilities and/or conditions of employment with Awesim unless specifically agreed upon in writing and signed by both the employee and the HR Manager of Awesim.\nDEFINITIONS\nRemote/remotely\n- Working from a location that is not based at Awesim offices.\nRemote employees\n- Employees employed in a role whereby their responsibilities can be fulfilled remotely, without any degradation of performance, at the discretion of their manager.\nHybrid\n- A combination of working remotely and at Awesim offices for which time periods of each are determined by their manager.\nHybrid employees\n- Employees employed in a role whereby a portion of their responsibilities can be fulfilled remotely with other responsibilities requiring the employee to be physically present at Awesim Offices, without any degradation of performance, at the discretion of their manager.\nIn office employees\n- Employees employed in a role whose responsibilities cannot be fulfilled remotely and are required to work from Awesim offices at all times.\nAwesim offices\n- Premises located at 36 Morsim Road, Hyde Park, Johannesburg, Block A\nAwesim events\n- Any event hosted by or for Awesim at their offices and/or at any other location.\nPhysical presence\n- Employees required to be physically present at Awesim offices, company events, etc. for any work-related requirements.\nJitter\n- Is the variation in time delay between when a signal is transmitted and when it's received over a network connection. This is often caused by network congestion, poor hardware performance and not implementing packet prioritization.\nLatency\n- Is the time it takes for data to be transferred between its original source and its destination, measured in milliseconds. Internet latency and network latency affect satellite internet connections, cable internet connections, as well as some WiFi connections.\nROLE BASED ELIGIBILITY\nRole\nWorking Eligibility\nCustomer Care\nHybrid\nInvoicing Consultants\nIn Office\nStickers\nIn Office\nStores Consultants\nIn Office\nHR\nHybrid\nFinance\nHybrid\nIT\nHybrid\nSales – Admin\nHybrid\nSales – In field\nRemote & In field\nDriver & Assistant\nIn Office\nCleaning & Hygiene\nIn Office\nLine Managers\nHybrid\nBENEFITS OF REMOTE/HYBRID WORKING\nEliminate the commute to and from the office\nReduces costs\nIncreases productivity\nIncreases motivation & work-life balance\nREMOTE/HYBRID MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS\nIn order to qualify, the employee within the eligible role must have, and be able to prove, the following minimum requirements:\nA designated work space in the remote location to work from (Table, desk, chair, stationary, etc.);\nMust have sufficient electricity and connection points within the designated work space;\nMust have access to an internet connection which must meet the minimum speed requirements;\nMust be able to connect to Awesim’s VPN at all times in order to be able to access the required drives, systems, etc.;\nMust have a means of being able to make contact with Awesim offices and be contacted accordingly e.g. cellphone, landline phone, etc. (Any costs associated with this in order to meet these requirements are for the employee’s account.);\nMust have access to a webcam for remote meeting purposes. The webcam must be tested and must be in working order. All of the equipment required to facilitate the above must be tested daily, before the start of your working day, and must be in working order. If the equipment is not in working order, this needs to be reported immediately to the employee’s line manager. If the employee is unable to work due to this, they will be required to take the time as annual leave. The employee will have one working day from the time of the equipment failure get the equipment into working order again, which must be taken as annual leave, if the employee is unable to get the equipment working again within one working day, they will be required to return to the office to continue working from the office until the equipment is repaired and their line manager gives them permission to work from home again.\nMINIMUM PERFORMANCE STANDARDS\nRole\nMinimum Performance Standards\nCustomer Care\nRefer to Annexure A\nIT\nRefer to Annexure B\nHR\nRefer to Annexure C\nFinance\nRefer to Annexure C\nSales – Admin\nRefer to Annexure C\nLine Managers\nRefer to Annexure C\nEligible employees working remotely/hybrid are required to continue to meet and exceed the required minimum performance standards as determined by their manager and/or as set out in the annexure attached hereto.\nShould eligible employees fail to meet/exceed minimum performance standards, they will no longer be eligible for remote/hybrid working and will be required to return to working at Awesim’s office to carry out their duties and responsibilities. Should this occur, the employee will not be eligible to work remotely/hybrid for three (3) months following their return to the office date. Thereafter, their manager will assess their performance accordingly to determine if this benefit will be granted again.\nREMOTE DESIGNATED WORKSPACE\nIt is the responsibility of the employee to designate a remote workspace in their remote location that will allow them to work effectively and efficiently. No work should be performed outside of this designated workspace.\nThe designated workspace must be in a location which is considered adequate for working purposes, similar to the environment created in the office. Examples include but are not limited to, low to no disturbances for the employee throughout the work day, low or no noise from non-work related sources for the employee throughout the work day, no shared use of any exclusive use working equipment with others such as chairs, internet connectivity, screens etc.\nAwesim cannot be held liable for any injuries or damages that may occur within employees designated work space. It is therefore the employee’s responsibility to ensure their designated work space is safe, kept in a safe condition, is free from hazards or dangerous items in order to protect themselves, as well as company equipment.\nAll company equipment issued to the employee to use during remote/hybrid working remains the property of Awesim. The employee is required to safe guard the equipment to prevent any loss of damages. Should loss or damage occur, the employee is required to report it immediately in accordance with company policy/procedure which will be managed accordingly.\nThe employee must safe-guard and ensure all Awesim’s computers and equipment is not accessed or used by any non-Awesim staff (e.g. Family, relatives, friends, etc.). Employees are prohibited from downloading and/or installing any unauthorized software on the allocated computer/laptop. In addition, employee is prohibited from uninstalling/removing any existing software required and/or used for safeguarding the laptop/computer.\nELECTRICITY AND ELECTRICAL CONNECTIONS\nEmployees working remotely/hybrid are required to ensure they have sufficient access to electricity supply and electrical connection points in order to fulfil their role duties and responsibilities, this includes a full back up power source for load shedding purposes. If no full back up power source is available when load shedding occurs, and the employee does not have the required battery power or is unable to connect to the internet, this time will be taken as annual leave.\nShould access to electricity be intermittent or not available, the employee is required to return to Awesim’s offices in order to attend to their duties and responsibilities accordingly. The employee is required to make up for any time lost during the downtime experienced that day/evening. Should the employee be unable to make up for the downtime, an annual leave request must be submitted accordingly.\nLoadshedding/blackouts:\nEmployees working remotely/hybrid are required to stay informed of their loadshedding/blackout schedules. It is the employee’s responsibility to check the load shedding schedule daily and report instances of potential or actual load shedding to their line manager before or when they occur. If the employee’s designated workspace is affected by loadshedding/blackouts and they do not have access to alternative means of electricity supplies, the employee is required to come in to Awesim offices to attend to their duties and responsibilities.\nIf the employee is unable to work due to load shedding/no access to power and this is not reported immediately to their line manager, this will result in a disciplinary action in accordance with company disciplinary code. All costs/accounts incurred to supply electricity to the employees designated work space in order for them to attend to their duties and responsibilities will be for the employees’ account.\nINTERNET CONNECTION AND SPEED\nEligible employees working remotely/hybrid must have a minimum internet connection of 10 megabits per second (mbps) for downloads, not less than 2Mbpps for uploads and with low latency and low jitter.\nA speed test may be required by IT to check that the employee’s internet connection is stable and fast and according to the above requirements.\nIT is not able to assist with troubleshooting connection issues – the employee is required to contact their chosen internet service provider to resolve any connection issues.\nIf the eligible employee cannot facilitate a proper internet connection that is stable (as per the above requirements), then the employee is required to return to Awesim offices in order to attend to their duties and responsibilities.\nIf the eligible employee is able to provide a proper internet connection however, they are experiencing other issues that cannot be solved over the phone/TeamViewer, they will be required to come into Awesim offices in order for IT to attempt to resolve the issues. If IT are unable to resolve the issues which is preventing the employee from working remotely/hybrid – the employee will no longer be considered eligible and will need to work from Awesim offices.\nThe employee must have adequate data available to be able to cater for all work-related needs (this includes full video calls) for their role. This data should be loaded prior to the start of their working day in order to avoid data running out during working hours.\nAny costs/accounts incurred for internet connectivity, internet upgrades to meet the required standard, internet repairs, data bundles/packages, etc. in order for the employee to work remotely/hybrid will be for the employee’s account.\nMEETINGS\nEmployees working remotely/hybrid are required to attend all essential meetings via video conference or by phone where applicable. Meetings that are not hosted remotely or if an employee is unable to attend remotely, but is essential to partake in the meeting, they are to make the necessary arrangements to physically attend the meeting at its hosted location. Employees are required to dress in smart casual, work appropriate clothing for any video meetings that take place with any 3rd parties (non-Awesim staff) as well as for any internal meetings.\nCOMPULSORY IN OFFICE WEEK\nAwesim requires all employees to work physically in the office for either three (3) consecutive days (Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday) or five (5) consecutive days during the compulsory in office week per month. The number of consecutive day requirement will be determined by the Exco Team based on the employee’s role and operational factors. The dates of the compulsory in office week will be communicated to remote/hybrid employees by management.\nFor remote positions that require flights into JHB & for the five (5) day in office week period, flights can be booked for early morning the Monday whereby the employee will travel to the office after arrival with a return flight that can be booked for a Friday afternoon with the morning spent in the office. Travel time lost during the business day will need to be accounted for/worked back the same week or within the first two days of the following week.\nEmployees who are unable to physically work in the office and/or do not physically attend work during the compulsory in office week are required to inform their Line Manager, as well as the CHRO, before that Monday of the in-office week or immediately if the circumstances change. The Line Manager & CHRO will consider the reasonings for not being able to attend and will approve/decline accordingly. Employees who do not attend the in-office week without a valid/approved reasoning will be required to take annual leave regardless if they have worked or not (remotely or in the office).\nAll costs associated with attending compulsory in-office weeks for remote/flexible positions will be for the employee’s account (accommodation, travel, food, etc.).\nFUNCTIONS, EVENTS, CONFERENCES, TRAINING, TEAMBUILDING, ETC\nAwesim organises a variety of functions, events, conferences, training and teambuilding sessions which may require employee’s physical attendance to same. Employees are required to physically attend all functions, events, conferences, training, teambuilding, etc. (either at Awesim offices and/or at a designated third-party location) whereby your physical attendance is required.\nShould an employee fail to physically attend any function, event, conference, training, teambuilding, etc. then annual leave will be taken accordingly, regardless whether the employee worked during that time or not (remotely or at the office). In addition, regardless of the employees reasoning for not attending (whether it’s a valid reason or not), if an employee fails to attend any functions, events, conferences, training, teambuilding – annual leave will be taken.\nIf an employee is unable to attend due to illness, then a Dr note must be obtained and submitted accordingly with their sick leave application for the period, regardless whether the employee worked during that time or not (remotely or at the office).\nDESIGNATED WORKING HOURS\nEmployees must follow their regular assigned work schedule/ working hours, unless otherwise discussed with and approved by the employee’s manager. All arrangements for childcare, elderly care, repairs and maintenance, etc. must be arranged so as not to interfere with the employee’s regular working hours.\nAlthough working remotely allows an employee to work at times outside of the standard office hours (08H00 AM to 17H00 PM) in some instances and/or based on the role requirements, all employees are expected to be available between standard working hours regardless of working remotely, in the field or in the office.\nMEALS AND BREAKS\nEmployees working from a designated workspace is entitled to meal and rest breaks as set out in the employees’ contract of employment with Awesim Telecoms.\nCONFIDENTIALITY AND SECURITY\nEmployees working remotely are reminded that even if they are working remotely/hybrid, they are bound by any confidentiality and/or security agreements in connection with their employment with Awesim.\nThus, consistent with Awesim expectations of information security for employees working in the office, remote employees will be expected to ensure the protection of proprietary company and customer information accessible from their remote designated work space.\nSteps include regular password maintenance, locked file cabinets and desks to store sensitive information, and any other measures appropriate for the role and the environment of the designated workspace.\nCOMMUNICATION AND SLACK\nEmployees are required to stay up to date on all Awesim communication platforms (email, slack, etc.). Employees working remotely are to ensure they keep their manager and team informed and updated. There should be no negative implications on communication as a result of working remotely.\nEmployees working remotely/hybrid are required to update their slack status accordingly. This is important in order to inform fellow employees of when you are working remotely vs when you are in the office, on leave, etc.\nPOLICIES, PROCEDURES, SOP'S AND GENERAL RULES\nAll of Awesim’s policies, procedures, SOP’s and general rules apply while working remotely/hybrid. Employee’s failing to comply with them will be subject to disciplinary action in accordance with Awesim’s disciplinary code.\nEmployees working remotely/hybrid understand and agree that their computers and internet may be monitored by Awesim during working hours and/or whilst the employee is attending to their duties and responsibilities.\nAwesim reserves the right to implement any software or hardware necessary to help facilitate the monitoring of work performed at home (eg. time keeping software).\nTIPS FOR WORKING REMOTELY/HYBRID\nMaintain your daily routine.\nJust because you’re not going into an office doesn’t mean you should skip the things that ordinarily set you up for success. Wake at the same time, and continue the routine that works for you.\nRepurpose commute time.\nDon’t let this reclaimed time of commuting to and from work go to waste. Utilise your time wisely and productively.\nDress for success.\nJust because you can get away with wearing your PJs doesn’t mean you should. You may still be interfacing with colleagues, partners, or customers via video. Plus, dressing comfortably yet professionally helps get you in the right mindset to stay productive.\nDesignate a dedicated workspace.\nFind an environment that fosters productivity. It should be quiet, comfortable, and free of distractions.\nConduct video meetings with your team/colleagues.\nFace-to-face interaction doesn’t have to be a casualty of remote work. Conduct daily stand-ups or team meetings via video conferencing software to maintain that personal connection and stay in touch with your team members/colleagues. It is important to ensure you continue to build and maintain your work relationships. Be careful not to distance yourself and make others feel that you are not approachable due to working remotely.\nANNEXURE A - CUSTOMER CARE\nThe Hybrid option will be offered as an added benefit to Top Performing Customer Care Consultants and/or Customer Care Ambassadors in accordance with set\nqualifying criteria and Minimal Acceptable Standards (MAS).\nHYBRID WORKING\nThe Hybrid option will be offered as an added benefit to Top Performing Customer Care Consultants and/or Customer Care Ambassadors in accordance with set qualifying criteria and Minimal Acceptable Standards (MAS).\nThe details of the qualifying criteria and MAS are determined by management based on operational requirements and can be changed from time to time. These will be communicated to the team accordingly.\nFrom those who meet and exceed the MAS, the top performers will be eligible for working remotely.\nOutput and Quality Assurance Requirements\nTop Performer/s based on the Average Performance Output in relation to the wider team, but no more than half the team at any given time and;\nMeets and exceeds Minimal Acceptable Standard (MAS) requirements across performance categories communicated accordingly.\nStandard Operating Procedures (SOP)\nFailure to comply with requirements outlined within this policy and/or any policies, procedures or SOP’s of Awesim, the consultant will not be eligible for the remote working benefit and/or if already granted the remote working benefit, then same will be revoked and the Consultant will be advised to return to the office the next business day.\nExample:-\nQuality Assurance Escalation due to poor customer service\nRepeat Service Request (SR) Flags\nCOMMUNICATION IN REGARD TO QUALIFYING TOP PERFORMERS WHO MAY WORK REMOTELY\nThe Top Performing Consultant/s and/or Ambassador/s who have met/exceeded the Qualifying Criteria will be advised if they can work remotely once performance statistics have been finalized and communicated accordingly.\nOnce determined, the Customer Care Supervisor will schedule the remote working in the calendar for the subsequent month and taking in-office compulsory week into account (e.g. Second and fourth week of the subsequent\nmonth, subject to In Office Week).\nSUBJECT TO CHANGE\nThe content of this Annexure is subject to change as a result of operational requirements and/or the availability of resources.\nANNEXURE B – IT\nEmployees will be allowed the opportunity to carry out their daily duties by making use of a hybrid model consisting of a combination of in office days & remote working days. This schedule is at manager’s discretion, as well as needs to be approved beforehand.\nHYBRID WORKING SCHEDULE\nAll employees within the IT Department are required to follow the hybrid working schedule that is communicated by management accordingly.\nThis schedule will be a combination of which employees are eligible to work remotely on certain days of the week, as well as those who are expected to work from the office. The IT Department requires at least one employee in the office a day in order to attend to any employee assistance, trouble shooting, etc. that may arise. This schedule is also subject to change based on operational requirements that may arise (e.g. fellow colleague calls in sick and is unable to come into the office. Etc.).\nThis schedule is not applicable for the in-office week as all IT employees will be expected to work from the office during this period.\nPROVIDE REMOTE IT SUPPORT\nAll IT employees must make themselves available to provide remote IT Support as required.\nShould remote IT Support be required, please ensure:\nWhen connecting to an employee’s computer, ensure the remote computers have Internet Connectivity and confirm Internet network performance using the required Speed Test utilities.\nOn the IT Computer, ensure Team viewer software is installed and be able to connect to any remote computers.\nCommunicate all / any IT related issues to affected employees and the employees Manager as required.\nTIME TRACKING\nAll time to be tracked accurately on Toggl using the relevant project and task descriptions on what the work entailed for the duration logged or through JIRA integration.\nDaily, weekly and monthly hours worked must be met in order for the employee to be able to make use of the hybrid working model. If time requirements are not met, the employee will no longer be eligible to work remotely.\nTASK MANAGEMENT (JIRA)\nEmployees are required to track and record tasks using JIRA and attend to their relevant allocated tasks as set out in their weekly sprints.\nANNEXURE C – OTHER\nEmployees will be allowed the opportunity to carry out their daily duties by making use of a hybrid model consisting of a combination of in office days & remote working days. This schedule is at manager’s discretion, as well as needs to be approved beforehand.\nPERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS/MINIMUM ACCEPTABLE STANDARDS\nAll employees working remotely will be required to carry out their daily roles and responsibilities as documented in their job profiles, Standard Operating Procedures (SOP’s) and as discussed and agreed with their manager.\nNo daily duties and responsibilities should be affected as a result of working remotely.\nHYBRID WORKING SCHEDULE\nThe Hybrid working schedule will need to be planned, presented and approved by the relevant Line Manager at least one week in advance. The number of days per week will vary based on operational requirements, as well as to ensure there is sufficient staffing capacity in office on the days when staff are working from home.\nTIME TRACKING\nAll time to be tracked accurately on Toggl using the relevant project and task descriptions on what the work entailed for the duration logged as communicated by the relevant manager.\nDaily, weekly and monthly hours worked must be met in order for the employee to be able to make use of the hybrid working model. If time requirements are not met, the employee will no longer be eligible to work remotely.\nTASK MANAGEMENT\nEmployees are required to track and record tasks using JIRA.\nIf the employee has JIRA tasks allocated to them, they are required to address them accordingly regardless of whether they are working in the office or remotely. JIRA tasks cannot be delayed due to the employee working remotely.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"6ed8da55-2bf1-4cd5-a16f-b71b6dc60d80\",\"title\":\"Remote/Hybrid Working Policy\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

Awesim have implemented this remote working policy/procedure to allow eligible employees to work from a remote location approved by Awesim. This policy/procedure will also give guidance on the terms, conditions and minimum required standards in this regard.

Each role in Awesim has differing operational requirements to work either physically present in the office, remotely or a hybrid of the two. These requirements are at the discretion of Awesim which may change from time to time.

 Although a certain role within Awesim may qualify for remote/hybrid working, the employee appointed in that role is not guaranteed this benefit nor is it an automatic right. Working remotely/hybrid is a benefit offered by Awesim to eligible employees. Awesim may revoke this benefit from an employee at any time whereby the employee will be required to return to working in the office accordingly.

Should eligible employees fail to meet or follow the requirements herein or as instructed by management, the employee will no longer be eligible for remote/hybrid working and will be required to return to the office to carry out their duties accordingly. In addition, possible disciplinary action may be implemented.

By working remotely/hybrid means that you agree to abide by the provisions herein. Working remotely/hybrid does not alter your duties, obligations, responsibilities and/or conditions of employment with Awesim unless specifically agreed upon in writing and signed by both the employee and the HR Manager of Awesim.

DEFINITIONS

  1. Remote/remotely - Working from a location that is not based at Awesim offices.
  2. Remote employees - Employees employed in a role whereby their responsibilities can be fulfilled remotely, without any degradation of performance, at the discretion of their manager.
  3. Hybrid - A combination of working remotely and at Awesim offices for which time periods of each are determined by their manager.
  4. Hybrid employees - Employees employed in a role whereby a portion of their responsibilities can be fulfilled remotely with other responsibilities requiring the employee to be physically present at Awesim Offices, without any degradation of performance, at the discretion of their manager.
  5. In office employees - Employees employed in a role whose responsibilities cannot be fulfilled remotely and are required to work from Awesim offices at all times.
  6. Awesim offices - Premises located at 36 Morsim Road, Hyde Park, Johannesburg, Block A
  7. Awesim events - Any event hosted by or for Awesim at their offices and/or at any other location.
  8. Physical presence - Employees required to be physically present at Awesim offices, company events, etc. for any work-related requirements.
  9. Jitter - Is the variation in time delay between when a signal is transmitted and when it's received over a network connection. This is often caused by network congestion, poor hardware performance and not implementing packet prioritization.
  10. Latency - Is the time it takes for data to be transferred between its original source and its destination, measured in milliseconds. Internet latency and network latency affect satellite internet connections, cable internet connections, as well as some WiFi connections.

ROLE BASED ELIGIBILITY

Role

Working Eligibility

Customer Care

Hybrid

Invoicing Consultants

In Office

Stickers

In Office

Stores Consultants

In Office

HR

Hybrid

Finance

Hybrid

IT

Hybrid

Sales – Admin

Hybrid

Sales – In field

Remote & In field

Driver & Assistant

In Office

Cleaning & Hygiene

In Office

Line Managers

Hybrid

BENEFITS OF REMOTE/HYBRID WORKING

  • Eliminate the commute to and from the office
  • Reduces costs
  • Increases productivity
  • Increases motivation & work-life balance

REMOTE/HYBRID MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS

In order to qualify, the employee within the eligible role must have, and be able to prove, the following minimum requirements:

  • A designated work space in the remote location to work from (Table, desk, chair, stationary, etc.);
  • Must have sufficient electricity and connection points within the designated work space;
  • Must have access to an internet connection which must meet the minimum speed requirements;
  • Must be able to connect to Awesim’s VPN at all times in order to be able to access the required drives, systems, etc.;
  • Must have a means of being able to make contact with Awesim offices and be contacted accordingly e.g. cellphone, landline phone, etc. (Any costs associated with this in order to meet these requirements are for the employee’s account.);
  • Must have access to a webcam for remote meeting purposes. The webcam must be tested and must be in working order. All of the equipment required to facilitate the above must be tested daily, before the start of your working day, and must be in working order. If the equipment is not in working order, this needs to be reported immediately to the employee’s line manager. If the employee is unable to work due to this, they will be required to take the time as annual leave. The employee will have one working day from the time of the equipment failure get the equipment into working order again, which must be taken as annual leave, if the employee is unable to get the equipment working again within one working day, they will be required to return to the office to continue working from the office until the equipment is repaired and their line manager gives them permission to work from home again.

MINIMUM PERFORMANCE STANDARDS

Role

Minimum Performance Standards

Customer Care

Refer to Annexure A

IT

Refer to Annexure B

HR

Refer to Annexure C

Finance

Refer to Annexure C

Sales – Admin

Refer to Annexure C

Line Managers

Refer to Annexure C

Eligible employees working remotely/hybrid are required to continue to meet and exceed the required minimum performance standards as determined by their manager and/or as set out in the annexure attached hereto.

Should eligible employees fail to meet/exceed minimum performance standards, they will no longer be eligible for remote/hybrid working and will be required to return to working at Awesim’s office to carry out their duties and responsibilities. Should this occur, the employee will not be eligible to work remotely/hybrid for three (3) months following their return to the office date. Thereafter, their manager will assess their performance accordingly to determine if this benefit will be granted again.

REMOTE DESIGNATED WORKSPACE

It is the responsibility of the employee to designate a remote workspace in their remote location that will allow them to work effectively and efficiently. No work should be performed outside of this designated workspace.

The designated workspace must be in a location which is considered adequate for working purposes, similar to the environment created in the office. Examples include but are not limited to, low to no disturbances for the employee throughout the work day, low or no noise from non-work related sources for the employee throughout the work day, no shared use of any exclusive use working equipment with others such as chairs, internet connectivity, screens etc.

Awesim cannot be held liable for any injuries or damages that may occur within employees designated work space. It is therefore the employee’s responsibility to ensure their designated work space is safe, kept in a safe condition, is free from hazards or dangerous items in order to protect themselves, as well as company equipment.

All company equipment issued to the employee to use during remote/hybrid working remains the property of Awesim. The employee is required to safe guard the equipment to prevent any loss of damages. Should loss or damage occur, the employee is required to report it immediately in accordance with company policy/procedure which will be managed accordingly.

The employee must safe-guard and ensure all Awesim’s computers and equipment is not accessed or used by any non-Awesim staff (e.g. Family, relatives, friends, etc.). Employees are prohibited from downloading and/or installing any unauthorized software on the allocated computer/laptop. In addition, employee is prohibited from uninstalling/removing any existing software required and/or used for safeguarding the laptop/computer.

ELECTRICITY AND ELECTRICAL CONNECTIONS

Employees working remotely/hybrid are required to ensure they have sufficient access to electricity supply and electrical connection points in order to fulfil their role duties and responsibilities, this includes a full back up power source for load shedding purposes. If no full back up power source is available when load shedding occurs, and the employee does not have the required battery power or is unable to connect to the internet, this time will be taken as annual leave.

Should access to electricity be intermittent or not available, the employee is required to return to Awesim’s offices in order to attend to their duties and responsibilities accordingly. The employee is required to make up for any time lost during the downtime experienced that day/evening. Should the employee be unable to make up for the downtime, an annual leave request must be submitted accordingly.

Loadshedding/blackouts:

Employees working remotely/hybrid are required to stay informed of their loadshedding/blackout schedules. It is the employee’s responsibility to check the load shedding schedule daily and report instances of potential or actual load shedding to their line manager before or when they occur. If the employee’s designated workspace is affected by loadshedding/blackouts and they do not have access to alternative means of electricity supplies, the employee is required to come in to Awesim offices to attend to their duties and responsibilities.

If the employee is unable to work due to load shedding/no access to power and this is not reported immediately to their line manager, this will result in a disciplinary action in accordance with company disciplinary code. All costs/accounts incurred to supply electricity to the employees designated work space in order for them to attend to their duties and responsibilities will be for the employees’ account.

INTERNET CONNECTION AND SPEED

Eligible employees working remotely/hybrid must have a minimum internet connection of 10 megabits per second (mbps) for downloads, not less than 2Mbpps for uploads and with low latency and low jitter.

A speed test may be required by IT to check that the employee’s internet connection is stable and fast and according to the above requirements.

IT is not able to assist with troubleshooting connection issues – the employee is required to contact their chosen internet service provider to resolve any connection issues.

If the eligible employee cannot facilitate a proper internet connection that is stable (as per the above requirements), then the employee is required to return to Awesim offices in order to attend to their duties and responsibilities.  

If the eligible employee is able to provide a proper internet connection however, they are experiencing other issues that cannot be solved over the phone/TeamViewer, they will be required to come into Awesim offices in order for IT to attempt to resolve the issues. If IT are unable to resolve the issues which is preventing the employee from working remotely/hybrid – the employee will no longer be considered eligible and will need to work from Awesim offices.

The employee must have adequate data available to be able to cater for all work-related needs (this includes full video calls) for their role. This data should be loaded prior to the start of their working day in order to avoid data running out during working hours.

Any costs/accounts incurred for internet connectivity, internet upgrades to meet the required standard, internet repairs, data bundles/packages, etc. in order for the employee to work remotely/hybrid will be for the employee’s account.

MEETINGS

Employees working remotely/hybrid are required to attend all essential meetings via video conference or by phone where applicable. Meetings that are not hosted remotely or if an employee is unable to attend remotely, but is essential to partake in the meeting, they are to make the necessary arrangements to physically attend the meeting at its hosted location. Employees are required to dress in smart casual, work appropriate clothing for any video meetings that take place with any 3rd parties (non-Awesim staff) as well as for any internal meetings.

COMPULSORY IN OFFICE WEEK

Awesim requires all employees to work physically in the office for either three (3) consecutive days (Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday) or five (5) consecutive days during the compulsory in office week per month. The number of consecutive day requirement will be determined by the Exco Team based on the employee’s role and operational factors. The dates of the compulsory in office week will be communicated to remote/hybrid employees by management.

For remote positions that require flights into JHB & for the five (5) day in office week period, flights can be booked for early morning the Monday whereby the employee will travel to the office after arrival with a return flight that can be booked for a Friday afternoon with the morning spent in the office. Travel time lost during the business day will need to be accounted for/worked back the same week or within the first two days of the following week.

Employees who are unable to physically work in the office and/or do not physically attend work during the compulsory in office week are required to inform their Line Manager, as well as the CHRO, before that Monday of the in-office week or immediately if the circumstances change. The Line Manager & CHRO will consider the reasonings for not being able to attend and will approve/decline accordingly. Employees who do not attend the in-office week without a valid/approved reasoning will be required to take annual leave regardless if they have worked or not (remotely or in the office).

All costs associated with attending compulsory in-office weeks for remote/flexible positions will be for the employee’s account (accommodation, travel, food, etc.).

FUNCTIONS, EVENTS, CONFERENCES, TRAINING, TEAMBUILDING, ETC

Awesim organises a variety of functions, events, conferences, training and teambuilding sessions which may require employee’s physical attendance to same. Employees are required to physically attend all functions, events, conferences, training, teambuilding, etc. (either at Awesim offices and/or at a designated third-party location) whereby your physical attendance is required.

Should an employee fail to physically attend any function, event, conference, training, teambuilding, etc. then annual leave will be taken accordingly, regardless whether the employee worked during that time or not (remotely or at the office). In addition, regardless of the employees reasoning for not attending (whether it’s a valid reason or not), if an employee fails to attend any functions, events, conferences, training, teambuilding – annual leave will be taken.

If an employee is unable to attend due to illness, then a Dr note must be obtained and submitted accordingly with their sick leave application for the period, regardless whether the employee worked during that time or not (remotely or at the office).

DESIGNATED WORKING HOURS

Employees must follow their regular assigned work schedule/ working hours, unless otherwise discussed with and approved by the employee’s manager. All arrangements for childcare, elderly care, repairs and maintenance, etc. must be arranged so as not to interfere with the employee’s regular working hours.

Although working remotely allows an employee to work at times outside of the standard office hours (08H00 AM to 17H00 PM) in some instances and/or based on the role requirements, all employees are expected to be available between standard working hours regardless of working remotely, in the field or in the office.

MEALS AND BREAKS

Employees working from a designated workspace is entitled to meal and rest breaks as set out in the employees’ contract of employment with Awesim Telecoms.

CONFIDENTIALITY AND SECURITY

Employees working remotely are reminded that even if they are working remotely/hybrid, they are bound by any confidentiality and/or security agreements in connection with their employment with Awesim.

Thus, consistent with Awesim expectations of information security for employees working in the office, remote employees will be expected to ensure the protection of proprietary company and customer information accessible from their remote designated work space.

Steps include regular password maintenance, locked file cabinets and desks to store sensitive information, and any other measures appropriate for the role and the environment of the designated workspace.

COMMUNICATION AND SLACK

Employees are required to stay up to date on all Awesim communication platforms (email, slack, etc.). Employees working remotely are to ensure they keep their manager and team informed and updated. There should be no negative implications on communication as a result of working remotely.  

Employees working remotely/hybrid are required to update their slack status accordingly. This is important in order to inform fellow employees of when you are working remotely vs when you are in the office, on leave, etc.

POLICIES, PROCEDURES, SOP'S AND GENERAL RULES

All of Awesim’s policies, procedures, SOP’s and general rules apply while working remotely/hybrid. Employee’s failing to comply with them will be subject to disciplinary action in accordance with Awesim’s disciplinary code. 

Employees working remotely/hybrid understand and agree that their computers and internet may be monitored by Awesim during working hours and/or whilst the employee is attending to their duties and responsibilities.

Awesim reserves the right to implement any software or hardware necessary to help facilitate the monitoring of work performed at home (eg. time keeping software).

TIPS FOR WORKING REMOTELY/HYBRID

  • Maintain your daily routine. Just because you’re not going into an office doesn’t mean you should skip the things that ordinarily set you up for success. Wake at the same time, and continue the routine that works for you.
  • Repurpose commute time. Don’t let this reclaimed time of commuting to and from work go to waste. Utilise your time wisely and productively.
  • Dress for success. Just because you can get away with wearing your PJs doesn’t mean you should. You may still be interfacing with colleagues, partners, or customers via video. Plus, dressing comfortably yet professionally helps get you in the right mindset to stay productive.
  • Designate a dedicated workspace. Find an environment that fosters productivity. It should be quiet, comfortable, and free of distractions.
  • Conduct video meetings with your team/colleagues. Face-to-face interaction doesn’t have to be a casualty of remote work. Conduct daily stand-ups or team meetings via video conferencing software to maintain that personal connection and stay in touch with your team members/colleagues. It is important to ensure you continue to build and maintain your work relationships. Be careful not to distance yourself and make others feel that you are not approachable due to working remotely.    

ANNEXURE A - CUSTOMER CARE

The Hybrid option will be offered as an added benefit to Top Performing Customer Care Consultants and/or Customer Care Ambassadors in accordance with set qualifying criteria and Minimal Acceptable Standards (MAS).

HYBRID WORKING

The Hybrid option will be offered as an added benefit to Top Performing Customer Care Consultants and/or Customer Care Ambassadors in accordance with set qualifying criteria and Minimal Acceptable Standards (MAS).  

The details of the qualifying criteria and MAS are determined by management based on operational requirements and can be changed from time to time. These will be communicated to the team accordingly.

From those who meet and exceed the MAS, the top performers will be eligible for working remotely.

Output and Quality Assurance Requirements

  • Top Performer/s based on the Average Performance Output in relation to the wider team, but no more than half the team at any given time and;
  • Meets and exceeds Minimal Acceptable Standard (MAS) requirements across performance categories communicated accordingly.

Standard Operating Procedures (SOP)

Failure to comply with requirements outlined within this policy and/or any policies, procedures or SOP’s of Awesim, the consultant will not be eligible for the remote working benefit and/or if already granted the remote working benefit, then same will be revoked and the Consultant will be advised to return to the office the next business day.


Example:-

    • Quality Assurance Escalation due to poor customer service
    • Repeat Service Request (SR) Flags

COMMUNICATION IN REGARD TO QUALIFYING TOP PERFORMERS WHO MAY WORK REMOTELY

The Top Performing Consultant/s and/or Ambassador/s who have met/exceeded the Qualifying Criteria will be advised if they can work remotely once performance statistics have been finalized and communicated accordingly.

Once determined, the Customer Care Supervisor will schedule the remote working in the calendar for the subsequent month and taking in-office compulsory week into account (e.g. Second and fourth week of the subsequent month, subject to In Office Week).

SUBJECT TO CHANGE

The content of this Annexure is subject to change as a result of operational requirements and/or the availability of resources.

ANNEXURE B – IT

Employees will be allowed the opportunity to carry out their daily duties by making use of a hybrid model consisting of a combination of in office days & remote working days. This schedule is at manager’s discretion, as well as needs to be approved beforehand.

HYBRID WORKING SCHEDULE

All employees within the IT Department are required to follow the hybrid working schedule that is communicated by management accordingly.

This schedule will be a combination of which employees are eligible to work remotely on certain days of the week, as well as those who are expected to work from the office. The IT Department requires at least one employee in the office a day in order to attend to any employee assistance, trouble shooting, etc. that may arise. This schedule is also subject to change based on operational requirements that may arise (e.g. fellow colleague calls in sick and is unable to come into the office. Etc.).

This schedule is not applicable for the in-office week as all IT employees will be expected to work from the office during this period.

PROVIDE REMOTE IT SUPPORT

All IT employees must make themselves available to provide remote IT Support as required.

Should remote IT Support be required, please ensure:

  • When connecting to an employee’s computer, ensure the remote computers have Internet Connectivity and confirm Internet network performance using the required Speed Test utilities. 
  • On the IT Computer, ensure Team viewer software is installed and be able to connect to any remote computers.
  • Communicate all / any IT related issues to affected employees and the employees Manager as required.

TIME TRACKING

All time to be tracked accurately on Toggl using the relevant project and task descriptions on what the work entailed for the duration logged or through JIRA integration.

Daily, weekly and monthly hours worked must be met in order for the employee to be able to make use of the hybrid working model. If time requirements are not met, the employee will no longer be eligible to work remotely.

TASK MANAGEMENT (JIRA)

Employees are required to track and record tasks using JIRA and attend to their relevant allocated tasks as set out in their weekly sprints.

ANNEXURE C – OTHER

Employees will be allowed the opportunity to carry out their daily duties by making use of a hybrid model consisting of a combination of in office days & remote working days. This schedule is at manager’s discretion, as well as needs to be approved beforehand.

PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS/MINIMUM ACCEPTABLE STANDARDS

All employees working remotely will be required to carry out their daily roles and responsibilities as documented in their job profiles, Standard Operating Procedures (SOP’s) and as discussed and agreed with their manager.

No daily duties and responsibilities should be affected as a result of working remotely.

HYBRID WORKING SCHEDULE

The Hybrid working schedule will need to be planned, presented and approved by the relevant Line Manager at least one week in advance. The number of days per week will vary based on operational requirements, as well as to ensure there is sufficient staffing capacity in office on the days when staff are working from home.

TIME TRACKING

All time to be tracked accurately on Toggl using the relevant project and task descriptions on what the work entailed for the duration logged as communicated by the relevant manager.

Daily, weekly and monthly hours worked must be met in order for the employee to be able to make use of the hybrid working model. If time requirements are not met, the employee will no longer be eligible to work remotely.

TASK MANAGEMENT

Employees are required to track and record tasks using JIRA.

If the employee has JIRA tasks allocated to them, they are required to address them accordingly regardless of whether they are working in the office or remotely. JIRA tasks cannot be delayed due to the employee working remotely.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "HR policy", "id": "6dee3fb5-0aa6-432f-b8af-753794b08509", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "Hybrid", "id": "546fa36f-b36b-455c-81b9-f70c99fa20d8", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Remote", "id": "00479041-15b1-4537-830c-f31d880ab167", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" }, { "value": "Remote working", "id": "7a2b98d1-ee8a-483e-add7-206fd6ff9317", "categoryId": "f1824c7f-80e1-44e8-9c49-e3aca58b442f", "categoryName": "HR Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-05T05:30:36.475+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/6ed8da55-2bf1-4cd5-a16f-b71b6dc60d80" }, { "id": "7de874e6-9024-4c7f-b451-68833725d4a9", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"7de874e6-9024-4c7f-b451-68833725d4a9\",\"title\":\"Training Register\",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nA quick reference guide to Awesims' Training Register template. Completing a Training Register is essential for tracking employee training and skills development, ensuring compliance with industry standards, and identifying gaps in training across the company. It also provides a clear record of completed training sessions, which is required for annual skills submissions to SETA and supports accountability within Awesim.\nHow to use this card:\nDownload the Form:\nTraining Register_HR_F_025_(07-02-2020)_Protected.docx\nComplete the Form:\nFill in all the required information.\nSend the Form:\nSend the completed form to the HR Assistant or Chief HR Officer so that it may be loaded into HR Manage for record purposes.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"7de874e6-9024-4c7f-b451-68833725d4a9\",\"title\":\"Training Register\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

A quick reference guide to Awesims' Training Register template. Completing a Training Register is essential for tracking employee training and skills development, ensuring compliance with industry standards, and identifying gaps in training across the company. It also provides a clear record of completed training sessions, which is required for annual skills submissions to SETA and supports accountability within Awesim.

How to use this card:

  1. Download the Form: Training Register_HR_F_025_(07-02-2020)_Protected.docx
  2. Complete the Form: Fill in all the required information.
  3. Send the Form: Send the completed form to the HR Assistant or Chief HR Officer so that it may be loaded into HR Manage for record purposes.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "register", "id": "ae7b272c-b900-4f17-ada4-d160b7e7971c", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "training", "id": "7520481b-31b5-435c-ab0f-99b3c154ce15", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" }, { "value": "training register", "id": "bfe89890-3703-4cbc-8f31-b9cc1c456b96", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-05T05:28:01.976+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/7de874e6-9024-4c7f-b451-68833725d4a9" }, { "id": "2a76ffaf-cb47-463b-bcb8-24df061a36f6", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"2a76ffaf-cb47-463b-bcb8-24df061a36f6\",\"title\":\"Internal Meeting Attendance Register \",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nA quick reference guide to Awesims' Internal Meeting Attendance Register. Completing an Internal meeting attendance register is essential to record employee's who have attended crucial internal meetings/forums, ensuring accurate record-keeping for compliance, auditing, and tracking.\nHow to use this card:\nDownload the Form:\nInternal Attendance Register_HR_F_026_(10-02-2020)_Protected.docx\nComplete the Form:\nFill in all the required information.\nSend/save the Form:\nSend the completed form to the relevant Manager or to HR where applicable. Save a copy of the completed form for record-keeping and ease of reference when required.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"2a76ffaf-cb47-463b-bcb8-24df061a36f6\",\"title\":\"Internal Meeting Attendance Register \",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

A quick reference guide to Awesims' Internal Meeting Attendance Register. Completing an Internal meeting attendance register is essential to record employee's who have attended crucial internal meetings/forums, ensuring accurate record-keeping for compliance, auditing, and tracking.

How to use this card:

  1. Download the Form: Internal Attendance Register_HR_F_026_(10-02-2020)_Protected.docx
  2. Complete the Form: Fill in all the required information.
  3. Send/save the Form: Send the completed form to the relevant Manager or to HR where applicable. Save a copy of the completed form for record-keeping and ease of reference when required.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "attendance register", "id": "723b80b0-2ee1-4bd5-8f6c-e3af8ec05a9b", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "internal", "id": "93a81a5e-8cd9-4018-9e91-73748e5e14e9", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "register", "id": "ae7b272c-b900-4f17-ada4-d160b7e7971c", "categoryId": "e0751ef6-b3fd-4bd0-babb-3bc7619c24e1", "categoryName": "Support Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-05T05:26:51.302+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/2a76ffaf-cb47-463b-bcb8-24df061a36f6" }, { "id": "ba031050-a1e8-42c3-84e7-c568f4f17863", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"ba031050-a1e8-42c3-84e7-c568f4f17863\",\"title\":\"Absenteeism Policy\",\"content\":\"\nSCOPE/OBJECTIVE\nThe purpose of this absenteeism policy is to establish guidelines and procedures for addressing and managing absenteeism and desertion from work among all employees, including permanent employees, temporary employees, and employees on fixed-term contracts. This policy aims to ensure that the company maintains procedural correctness in handling absenteeism cases and promotes a culture of attendance and punctuality among employees.\nDEFINITIONS\n\\\"Absenteeism”\nmeans not being at work (i.e. not turning up to work at all) without permission / authorisation (i.e. leave), as well as arriving late, leaving early, extended tea or lunch breaks, attending to private business during working hours, extended toilet breaks, feigned illness, undue length of time in fetching or carrying and other unexplained absences.\n“Authorised absenteeism”\nrefers to the Employee being away from work with permission/authorisation.  The Employee is thus on leave (with the assumption that the leave was applied for by the Employee and authorised by the Employer).\n“Unauthorised absenteeism”\nmeans being away from work without authorisation.  The Employee is thus away from work or not doing his/her work because of reasons such as staying away from work without authorisation, attending to private work during business hours, extended toilet breaks, feigned illness etc.\n“Desertion” or “Absconding”\noccurs when an Employee who is absent from work for three (3) or more consecutive days, without notifying the Employer of the reason for the absence, and without the intention of returning to work.\nLEGAL PRINCIPLES\nThe following legal principles apply to this policy:\nBasic Conditions of Employment Act, Act 75 of 1997;\nLabour Relations Act, Act 66 of 1995; and\nDepartment of Labour Code of Good Practice, 2004.\nPOLICY/PROCEDURE\nRegardless of the reason, employees are not entitled to be absent without leave from work or from their workstations during working hours. Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd’s disciplinary policy provides for disciplinary action where employees infringe this policy. Employees are entitled to take authorised leave as per the applicable South African labor laws and company policies. Types of authorized leave may include:\nSick Leave: For instances of personal illness or injury.\nAnnual Leave: For planned vacations and time off.\nFamily Responsibility Leave: In the event that the employee's child is sick and/or in the unfortunate death of a spouse/life partner, parent, adoptive parent, grandparent, child, adoptive child, grandchild or sibling.\nMaternity/Parental Leave: As prescribed by the law.\nWhere an employee is absent unexpectedly for reasons of ill health or unavoidable reasons, the employee must follow Awesim Telecoms Leave policy/procedure.\nAny absence from work without authority or good cause will result in the employee not being entitled to any remuneration for the period of absence, as well as possible disciplinary action in line with Awesim Telecoms disciplinary policy/procedure.\nFORMS OF UNAUTHORISED ABSENTEEISM\nAbsence from overtime work. When an employee fails to report for overtime after the employee has been scheduled to work overtime on a specific date and time.\nUnrest, violence and political stay-away (any absence because of unrest, violence, political stay-away or national strike or stay-away).\nDeliberate and willful absenteeism. When the employee does not come to work because he/she just feels like a day off or for any other reason not justifiable and/or permissible by the company.  There is usually a pattern such as the employee is always absent on payday or the day after payday, or is always absent on a Monday or a Friday.\nAbsence after permission has been refused. If the employee does absent him/herself after refusal (provided the refusal is fair and equitable).\nDesertion/Absconding: employee does not attend work for three (3) or more consecutive days without permission/authorization and has no intention of returning to work.\nLeaving Work Early without Authorization: Leaving work before the scheduled time without obtaining permission from a supervisor.\nFalse Reporting of Absences: Providing inaccurate reasons or falsifying documents to cover up unauthorised absenteeism.\nExcessive Tardiness: Repeatedly arriving late to work without valid reasons or prior approval.\nDEALING WITH ABSENTEEISM & DESERTION/ABSCONDING\nABSENTEEISM\nAuthorised absenteeism (i.e. approved leave) will be handled in accordance with Awesim Telecoms leave policy/procedure.\nUnauthorised absenteeism will be handled in accordance with Awesim Telecoms disciplinary policy/procedure, as well as the guidelines to offenses and possible sanctions.\nDESERTION/ABSCONDING\nShould an employee desert/abscond themselves from work (Absent for three (3) or more consecutive days), the manager will make reasonable attempts to locate the whereabouts of the employee (phoning the employee, leaving voicemail messages, SMS, etc.). Records of the attempts to contact the employee must be kept for record purposes.\nShould the manager be unsuccessful in making contact with the employee and/or locating the employee’s whereabouts, then a disciplinary enquiry will be conducted. Should the employee fail to attend the disciplinary enquiry without valid reasoning’s, then the enquiry can be held in their absence. Should the employee be found guilty, his/her services will be terminated.\nShould the employee return to the workplace after their employment has been terminated, a new disciplinary enquiry will be held whereby the chairperson will make a determination on whether the dismissal will be upheld and/or if the employee will be reinstated.\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"ba031050-a1e8-42c3-84e7-c568f4f17863\",\"title\":\"Absenteeism Policy\",\"content\":\"

SCOPE/OBJECTIVE

The purpose of this absenteeism policy is to establish guidelines and procedures for addressing and managing absenteeism and desertion from work among all employees, including permanent employees, temporary employees, and employees on fixed-term contracts. This policy aims to ensure that the company maintains procedural correctness in handling absenteeism cases and promotes a culture of attendance and punctuality among employees.

DEFINITIONS

  1. \\\"Absenteeism” means not being at work (i.e. not turning up to work at all) without permission / authorisation (i.e. leave), as well as arriving late, leaving early, extended tea or lunch breaks, attending to private business during working hours, extended toilet breaks, feigned illness, undue length of time in fetching or carrying and other unexplained absences.
  2. “Authorised absenteeism” refers to the Employee being away from work with permission/authorisation.  The Employee is thus on leave (with the assumption that the leave was applied for by the Employee and authorised by the Employer).
  3. “Unauthorised absenteeism” means being away from work without authorisation.  The Employee is thus away from work or not doing his/her work because of reasons such as staying away from work without authorisation, attending to private work during business hours, extended toilet breaks, feigned illness etc.
  4. “Desertion” or “Absconding” occurs when an Employee who is absent from work for three (3) or more consecutive days, without notifying the Employer of the reason for the absence, and without the intention of returning to work.

LEGAL PRINCIPLES

The following legal principles apply to this policy:

  1. Basic Conditions of Employment Act, Act 75 of 1997;
  2. Labour Relations Act, Act 66 of 1995; and
  3. Department of Labour Code of Good Practice, 2004.

POLICY/PROCEDURE

Regardless of the reason, employees are not entitled to be absent without leave from work or from their workstations during working hours. Awesim Telecoms (Pty) Ltd’s disciplinary policy provides for disciplinary action where employees infringe this policy. Employees are entitled to take authorised leave as per the applicable South African labor laws and company policies. Types of authorized leave may include:

  • Sick Leave: For instances of personal illness or injury.
  • Annual Leave: For planned vacations and time off.
  • Family Responsibility Leave: In the event that the employee's child is sick and/or in the unfortunate death of a spouse/life partner, parent, adoptive parent, grandparent, child, adoptive child, grandchild or sibling.
  • Maternity/Parental Leave: As prescribed by the law.

Where an employee is absent unexpectedly for reasons of ill health or unavoidable reasons, the employee must follow Awesim Telecoms Leave policy/procedure.

Any absence from work without authority or good cause will result in the employee not being entitled to any remuneration for the period of absence, as well as possible disciplinary action in line with Awesim Telecoms disciplinary policy/procedure.

FORMS OF UNAUTHORISED ABSENTEEISM

  • Absence from overtime work. When an employee fails to report for overtime after the employee has been scheduled to work overtime on a specific date and time.
  • Unrest, violence and political stay-away (any absence because of unrest, violence, political stay-away or national strike or stay-away).
  • Deliberate and willful absenteeism. When the employee does not come to work because he/she just feels like a day off or for any other reason not justifiable and/or permissible by the company.  There is usually a pattern such as the employee is always absent on payday or the day after payday, or is always absent on a Monday or a Friday. 
  • Absence after permission has been refused. If the employee does absent him/herself after refusal (provided the refusal is fair and equitable).
  • Desertion/Absconding: employee does not attend work for three (3) or more consecutive days without permission/authorization and has no intention of returning to work.
  • Leaving Work Early without Authorization: Leaving work before the scheduled time without obtaining permission from a supervisor.
  • False Reporting of Absences: Providing inaccurate reasons or falsifying documents to cover up unauthorised absenteeism.
  • Excessive Tardiness: Repeatedly arriving late to work without valid reasons or prior approval.

DEALING WITH ABSENTEEISM & DESERTION/ABSCONDING

ABSENTEEISM

  1. Authorised absenteeism (i.e. approved leave) will be handled in accordance with Awesim Telecoms leave policy/procedure.
  2. Unauthorised absenteeism will be handled in accordance with Awesim Telecoms disciplinary policy/procedure, as well as the guidelines to offenses and possible sanctions.

DESERTION/ABSCONDING

  1. Should an employee desert/abscond themselves from work (Absent for three (3) or more consecutive days), the manager will make reasonable attempts to locate the whereabouts of the employee (phoning the employee, leaving voicemail messages, SMS, etc.). Records of the attempts to contact the employee must be kept for record purposes.
  2. Should the manager be unsuccessful in making contact with the employee and/or locating the employee’s whereabouts, then a disciplinary enquiry will be conducted. Should the employee fail to attend the disciplinary enquiry without valid reasoning’s, then the enquiry can be held in their absence. Should the employee be found guilty, his/her services will be terminated.
  3. Should the employee return to the workplace after their employment has been terminated, a new disciplinary enquiry will be held whereby the chairperson will make a determination on whether the dismissal will be upheld and/or if the employee will be reinstated.

\"}", "collection_id": "", "collection_name": "", "tags": [ { "value": "absenteeism policy", "id": "bc884d5e-044d-4cf1-b930-f92e3410ee74", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" }, { "value": "policy", "id": "4b4e383d-ef16-43ea-8781-9877bf0601e9", "categoryId": "877b2ec0-0822-48f7-ac44-e37af6584b8d", "categoryName": "Tags" } ], "last_modified": "2024-12-04T17:50:49.103+0000", "url": "https://app.getguru.com/cards/ba031050-a1e8-42c3-84e7-c568f4f17863" }, { "id": "d63c01f8-a24f-4640-a46a-9f8964f47417", "title": "", "content_text": "{\"id\":\"d63c01f8-a24f-4640-a46a-9f8964f47417\",\"title\":\"Customer Care Service Request (SR) Handling SOP\",\"content\":\"\nSTANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE\nThis Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.\nPURPOSE\nThe purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to provide clear guidelines for Customer Care Consultants to ensure high-quality customer service and satisfaction through effective use of resources such as the knowledge base, CMS, CRM, and other internal tools, in alignment with best practices for handling Service Requests (SRs).\nKey guidelines include:\nCOMPREHENSIVE PRODUCT AND SERVICE UNDERSTANDING\nCustomer Care Consultants are required to stay well-informed and must have a thorough understanding of all products and services by actively referencing the knowledge base, CMS, CRM, and other internal resources.\nQUERY CLASSIFICATION\nCarefully read and identify the query type during the first, or at most second, service request interaction, and apply an accurate tag for categorization to guide the Service Request Handling process.\nIDENTITY VERIFICATION\nVerify the customer’s identity and relevant details to ensure security and a personalized experience, especially when information is not pre-verified in the CMS and/or CRM database.\nSERVICE HISTORY REVIEW\nCustomer Care Consultants are required to review the service history, including both open and closed cases and recent agent communications, to clarify the query and determine the next steps toward resolution. Avoid repetitive or irrelevant questions and focus on providing accurate, relevant assistance\nCLEAR AND ACCURATE INFORMATION AND SOLUTIONS\nRespond promptly by asking relevant questions to fully understand the query and provide accurate, customer-friendly responses that directly resolve the agent’s specific query.\nCommunicate product and service information in clear, simple language, mindful that customers may have varying levels of understanding of the service offering.\nAddress agent inquiries directly, avoiding unnecessary or irrelevant questions, and focus on delivering solutions that are clear, actionable, and relevant.\nSTANDARDS FOR USING PRE-WRITTEN RESPONSES\nAppropriate Use:\nUse verified canned responses only when they accurately address the customer's query, based on resources such as the knowledge base, CMS, CRM, and other internal resources.\nCustomization:\nAdapt canned responses to fit the specific customer query, incorporating insights about account information, to provide well-informed, supportive responses.\nPROFESSIONAL AND COURTEOUS COMMUNICATION\nMaintain a respectful, helpful, and customer-friendly tone in all responses, using language that aligns with company standards.\nDOCUMENT ALL ACTIONS\nService Request Comments:\nEnsure all Service Request (SR) comments are clear and complete to support consistency, avoid duplication, and prevent delays in future interactions. This helps ensure colleagues require no further clarification.\nAccuracy in CRM Updates:\nEnsure all information entered into the CMS and/or CRM is accurate, complete, and up-to-date, reflecting the details of each interaction and any actions taken.\nTROUBLESHOOTING AND PROBLEM SOLVING GUIDELINES\nThorough Knowledge:\nMaintain a strong understanding of troubleshooting steps and potential solutions for common product or service issues to effectively assist customers.\nDocument and Escalate Issues:\nLog any issues, concerns, or recurring trends, including technical issues such as hardware or software problems. Ensure these are reported immediately with supporting images and detailed descriptions so they can be addressed promptly.\nESCALATION AND RESOLUTION PROTOCOLS\nEscalation Process\n: If the issue cannot be resolved promptly, is complex, or beyond your expertise, follow escalation protocols to engage the appropriate support teams or supervisors, ensuring all relevant details are thoroughly documented. Clearly communicate the customer’s issue and actions taken so far to the next agent or team, providing a concise summary to prevent the customer from having to repeat information and to ensure a smooth transition.\nInform the Customer:\nNotify the customer that you are connecting them with a team member who can provide additional assistance and inform them of the expected escalation turnaround time.\nError Management and Escalation Protocol:\nIf you identify an error, whether it is your own or one made by a colleague, immediately document the details in the CMS SR Comments and take steps to rectify it. Ensure all relevant information is clearly and transparently recorded; errors must not be concealed or covered up. Adhere to established escalation protocols to engage with the appropriate supervisors, as necessary.\nCLOSING STANDARDS\nConfirm Satisfaction:\nBefore ending the interaction, ensure the customer has no additional questions and is satisfied with the solution provided.\nOffer Further Assistance:\nEncourage the customer to reach out if they need additional help. For example, say, “If you have any more questions, please don’t hesitate to contact us.”\nADHERENCE TO SOP GUIDELINES\nFollow all established SOPs to maintain consistency, quality, and professionalism in every interaction, including handling ad hoc Service Requests (SRs).\n\"}", "content_html": "{\"id\":\"d63c01f8-a24f-4640-a46a-9f8964f47417\",\"title\":\"Customer Care Service Request (SR) Handling SOP\",\"content\":\"

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is effective immediately and compliance is mandatory. Please note this is not exhaustive and may not cover every possible scenario, process or detail. Non-compliance with the guidelines or instructions outlined herein may result in disciplinary measures. Should you have any questions or need further clarification, you are responsible for consulting with your Line Manager promptly.

PURPOSE

The purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to provide clear guidelines for Customer Care Consultants to ensure high-quality customer service and satisfaction through effective use of resources such as the knowledge base, CMS, CRM, and other internal tools, in alignment with best practices for handling Service Requests (SRs).


Key guidelines include:

COMPREHENSIVE PRODUCT AND SERVICE UNDERSTANDING

Customer Care Consultants are required to stay well-informed and must have a thorough understanding of all products and services by actively referencing the knowledge base, CMS, CRM, and other internal resources.

QUERY CLASSIFICATION

Carefully read and identify the query type during the first, or at most second, service request interaction, and apply an accurate tag for categorization to guide the Service Request Handling process.

IDENTITY VERIFICATION

Verify the customer’s identity and relevant details to ensure security and a personalized experience, especially when information is not pre-verified in the CMS and/or CRM database.

SERVICE HISTORY REVIEW

Customer Care Consultants are required to review the service history, including both open and closed cases and recent agent communications, to clarify the query and determine the next steps toward resolution.
